name: 3D Panel
text: 3D Panel 3D Panel To insert new panel on the form, click button 3D panel in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/3D panel. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the top left corner of the rectangle. Drag to the right and bottom and release the button. New panel is placed on the form. Create a square panel Click button 3D panel in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/3D panel. Press Shift. Move the cursor to the desired position, click and drag to the bottom. Create panel with maximal width Click button 3D panel in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/3D panel. Press Ctrl. Move the cursor to the desired position, click and drag to the bottom. To change panel properties double click on the panel edge -or- select the panel, right click to display context menu and choose 'Properties'.

name: 3D Panel properties
text: 3D Panel properties 3D Panel properties Object name When the new panel is created, generator automatically assigns the name Panel_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed. Z-Order Possible values: 0, -1, -2, -3, ... See Z-Order. All other attributes are ignored

name: ABS
text: ABS ABS This function returns absolute value. Format ABS(numeric_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - numeric_exp - numeric expression Examples $A = ABS(acc_1) $A = ABS(acc_1 – acc_2) See Also Numeric Functions

name: Absolute positions
text: Absolute positions Absolute positions Normally, a lineset is printed after the preceding lineset. Iaf enables you to print a lineset in the same position on each page. In Lineset column you see all linesets in the report. In Position column, you have to enter the absolute position in millimeters. Example: the invoice form. The invoice amount is always printed at the bottom of the page. If you want to reserve the space where this lineset is to be printed, even when the lineset is not printed on every page, set 'Overflow position' to absolute_position - 1. See 'Define report dimensions' dialog. Note Page header is always printed on the top of the page and does not make sense to define the absolute position for it.

name: ADD
description: This statement adds a numeric value to numeric field.
text: ADD ADD This statement adds a numeric value to numeric field. Format ADD numeric_value TO numeric_field [TRUNCATED] Where - numeric_value - any numeric expression, like numeric literal, numeric field, formula, numeric function, etc. - numeric_field - a numeric database field, numeric local field, counter_nn or acc_nn TRUNCATED - this keyword can be used if decimal arithmetic enabled, see Options dialog. If numeric_field is unpacked numeric, BCD packed or integer, output result will be truncated. Otherwise, the result will be rounded according to selected rounding method. If decimal arithmetic is disabled, or numeric_field is a floating-point number, the TRUNCATED has no effect. Examples ADD 1 TO acc_1 ADD acc_1 * acc_2 TO acc_3 ADD day(date_field) TO counter_1 ADD acc_1 TO $numarr($ndx) ADD ($a * $b) to $res TRUNCATED ! if $a = 0.25 and $b = 0.35 then $a * $b = 0.0875 ! if $res has two decimals, its value will be $res = 0.08 ADD ($a * $b) to $res ! if $a = 0.25 and $b = 0.35 -> $res = 0.09

name: ADD_DAYS
description: This function adds number of days to a date.
text: ADD_DAYS ADD_DAYS This function adds number of days to a date. Format ADD_DAYS(date, numdays) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date - date expression - numdays - number of days, numeric expression Examples $Date = ADD_DAYS(today,5) $Date = ADD_DAYS(BEGINCY,-12) $Date = ADD_DAYS(today,counter_1) $Date = ADD_DAYS(beginy(today),15) See Also ADD_WEEKS, ADD_MONTHS, ADD_YEARS, Date Functions

name: ADD_HOURS
description: This function adds number of hours to a time or datetime field.
text: ADD_HOURS ADD_HOURS This function adds number of hours to a time or datetime field. Format ADD_HOURS(time, numhours) Return Value Type If 'time' parameter is a time field, return value is time. If 'time' parameter is a datetime field, return value is datetime. Parameters - time - time expression, or datetime field - numhours - number of hours, numeric_exp Note 1 If 'time' parameter is a time, out-of-range result time will be corrected automatically. Note 2 If 'time' parameter is a datetime, result is datetime field and all the field's components will be adjusted. Examples $Time = ADD_HOURS (systime,5) $Time = ADD_HOURS (systime,counter_1) $Time = ADD_HOURS ("23:55:00",1) !! result is 00:55:00 $dt = ADD_HOURS("2009-12-31 23:00:00",2) !!result is 2010-01-01 01:00:00 See Also ADD_SECONDS, ADD_MINUTES, Time Functions

name: Additional view actions
description: Additional view actions Additional view actions
text: Additional view actions Additional view actions As we already seen, the view determines selection criteria, joins, sort order etc. In many cases, this is quite adequate to define main input record stream. But, if we have some complex selection criterion we can calculate it at this place and, eventually, ignore the record if the criterion was not satisfied. To ignore current record, we use NEXT statement. Additional view actions execute just after the reading of record from input record stream. Example: calc1 calc2 ... if $flag = 0 NEXT end_if This means the following: if $FLAG has value 0, then read next record from the input stream (the current record will be ignored). See also View definition

name: Addline
text: Addline Addline This method adds one line to a grid. The line will be added after last displayed one. Format Addline(field1, field2, ..., fieldN ) Parameters - field1 - field to be displayed in the first column. - field2 - field to be displayed in the second column. - fieldN - field to be displayed in the last column. Applied To Grid. Note Number of parameters must be equal to number of the columns. Example Table_1.Addline(counter_1, acc_1, today, systime) See also Getline Removeline Methods

name: Add local field
description: Add local field Add local field
text: Add local field Add local field In this dialog we can create one local field. Local field name Local field name is maximum 16 characters long, the first character must be dollar sing ($). Allowed characters: A - Z, 0 - 9, _ and $. Examples: $LOC1 $LL $ALPHA1 $ALPHA_1 $ALPHA$1 $AAAAAAAAAA12345678 invalid, name too long $$ALPHA invalid, the second character is $, see DEFINE_LOCAL Field type Select one of field types from combobox. Alpha (ANSI) - single-byte alpha field Alpha (Unicode) - double-byte, UCS-2, alpha field. Number of bytes occupied by such field is 2 * number_of_characters. Field size For alpha fields, maximum number of characters, not bytes. For date, allowed values are 8 or 6. For time, allowed values are 6 or 4. For numeric, field size is in the form NN[,DD] where is NN - total number of digits, DD - number of decimals. For instance, 5,2 means: total digits 5; integer part 3 digits; two decimals. Data type This parameter applies to numeric fields only. Default is unpacked: one digit occupies one byte, the sign is combined with last digit. Number of elements If you want to define an array, enter number of elements. Maximum number of elements is 9999. Show local field...

name: ADD_MINUTES
description: This function adds number of minutes to a time or datetime field.
text: ADD_MINUTES ADD_MINUTES This function adds number of minutes to a time or datetime field. Format ADD_MINUTES(time, numminutes) Return Value Type If 'time' parameter is a time field, return value is time. If 'time' parameter is a datetime field, return value is datetime. Parameters - time - time_exp, or datetime field - numminutes - number of minutes, numeric_exp Note 1 If 'time' parameter is a time, out-of-range result time will be corrected automatically. Note 2 If 'time' parameter is a datetime, result is datetime field and all the field's components will be adjusted. Examples $Min = ADD_MINUTES (systime,5) $Min = ADD_MINUTES (systime,counter_1) $Min = ADD_MINUTES ("23:55:00",10) !! result is 00:05:00 $dt = ADD_MINUTES("2009-12-31 23:59:00",2) !!result is 2010-01-01 00:01:00 See Also ADD_SECONDS, ADD_HOURS, Time Functions

name: ADD_MONTHS
description: This function adds number of months to a date.
text: ADD_MONTHS ADD_MONTHS This function adds number of months to a date. Format ADD_MONTHS(date, nummonths) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date - date expression - nummonths - number of months, numeric_exp Note Out-of-range result date will be corrected automatically. Examples $Months = ADD_MONTHS (today,5) $Months = ADD_MONTHS (BEGINCY,-3) $Months = ADD_MONTHS (today,counter_1) $Months = ADD_MONTHS ("31-mar-2001",1) !! result is 30-apr-2001 $Months = ADD_MONTHS ("31-jan-2001",1) !! result is 28-feb-2001 See Also ADD_DAYS, ADD_WEEKS, ADD_YEARS, Date Functions

name: ADD_SECONDS
description: This function adds number of seconds to a time or datetime field.
text: ADD_SECONDS ADD_SECONDS This function adds number of seconds to a time or datetime field. Format ADD_SECONDS(time, numseconds) Return Value Type If 'time' parameter is a time field, return value is time. If 'time' parameter is a datetime field, return value is datetime. Parameters - time - time_exp, or datetime field - numseconds - number of seconds, numeric_exp Note 1 If 'time' parameter is a time, out-of-range result time will be corrected automatically. Note 2 If 'time' parameter is a datetime, result is datetime field and all the field's components will be adjusted. Examples $res_time = ADD_SECONDS (systime,5) $res_time = ADD_SECONDS (systime,counter_1) $res_time = ADD_SECONDS ("23:59:50",20) !! result is 00:00:10 $dt = ADD_SECONDS("2009-12-31 23:59:59",2) !!result is 2010-01-01 00:00:02 See Also ADD_MINUTES, ADD_HOURS, SUB_SECONDS, Time Functions

name: ADD_WEEKS
description: This function adds number of weeks to a date.
text: ADD_WEEKS ADD_WEEKS This function adds number of weeks to a date. Format ADD_WEEKS(date, numweeks) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date - date_exp - numweeks - number of weeks, numeric_exp Note 1 week = 7 days. ADD_WEEKS(date,numweeks) = ADD_DAYS(date,7*numweeks) Examples $date = ADD_WEEKS(today,2) See Also ADD_DAYS, ADD_MONTHS, ADD_YEARS, Date Functions

name: ADD_YEARS
description: This function adds number of years to a date.
text: ADD_YEARS ADD_YEARS This function adds number of years to a date. Format ADD_YEARS(date, numyears) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date - date expression - numyears - number of years, numeric Note Out-of-range result date will be corrected automatically. Examples $Years = ADD_YEARS (today,5) $Years = ADD_YEARS (BEGINCY,-3) $Years = ADD_YEARS (today,counter_1) $Years = ADD_YEARS("29-feb-2000",1) !! result is 28-feb-2001 See Also ADD_DAYS, ADD_WEEKS, ADD_MONTHS, Date Functions

name: Aggregate Functions
text: Aggregate Functions Aggregate Functions AVG returns the average value of the input values for all processed records between two break fields. COUNT returns number of records between two break fields. MAX returns the greatest of the input values for all processed records between two break fields. MIN returns the smallest of the input values for all processed records between two break fields. TOTAL returns sum of numeric_exp for all processed records between two break fields.

name: Align/Size
text: Align/Size Align/Size Left Aligns the left edges of the selected objects with the dominant object. Right Aligns the right edges of the selected objects with the dominant object. Top Aligns the top edges of the selected objects with the dominant object. Bottom Aligns the bottom edges of the selected objects with the dominant object. Horizontal Center Horizontally center selected objects with the dominant object. Vertical Center Vertically center selected objects with the dominant object. Align To Grid Aligns selected objects to the grid if grid is visible. Bring To Front Moves passive object to the top (sets Z-Order = 0). Send To Back Moves passive object to the bottom. Make same width Resize selected objects to have the width of the dominant object. Make same height Resize selected objects to have the height of the dominant object. Make same size Resize selected objects to have the size (width and height) of the dominant object. Equal Vertical Distance Evenly spaces the selected objects vertically. The distance between objects is calculated with possible rounding. Both top and bottom objects preserve unchanged positions. Equal Vertical Distance (fixed) Evenly spaces the selected...

name: ALLOCSTRING
description: This function creates new memo field and allocates continuous memory block to it.
text: ALLOCSTRING ALLOCSTRING This function creates new memo field and allocates continuous memory block to it. Format ALLOCSTRING (number_of_chars) Return Value Type String. Parameters - number_of_chars - number of characters, size of memory block, numeric. Note When we use many consecutive concatenations, heap memory can be fragmented (because every new concatenation can request enlarged memory block) : $s = 'Any text' $s = concat($s,'string1') $s = concat($s,'string2') ... $s = concat($s,'stringN') To avoid such situation, we can initially allocate a memory block of sufficient size. $s = allocstring(1000) !! Allocate block of 1000 characters $s = 'Any text' $s = concat($s,'string1') $s = concat($s,'string2') ... $s = concat($s,'stringN') ALLOCSTRING can be used in assignment statement only. Examples $memo = ALLOCSTRING(10000) ! If $memo is defined as ANSI memo, above function allocates 10000 bytes. ! If $memo if defined as UTF-16 memo, 20000 bytes will be allocated. See Also LTRIM, RTRIM, String Functions

name: ALLTRIM
description: This functions removes white space from both ends of alpha expression.
text: ALLTRIM ALLTRIM This functions removes white space from both ends of alpha expression. Format ALLTRIM(alpha_exp) Return Value Type String. Parameters - alpha_exp - alpha field Examples $a = ALLTRIM(alpha_fld) See Also LTRIM, RTRIM, String Functions

name: ALPHA
description: This function converts any field to alphanumeric one.
text: ALPHA ALPHA This function converts any field to alphanumeric one. Format ALPHA(field) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - field - any field Examples $a = alpha(counter_1) if alpha(fld) = space ... end_if See Also String Functions

name: ANNUITY
text: ANNUITY ANNUITY Format ANNUITY(principal,interest_rate,interval,number_of_periods_in_year,flag) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - principal - - interest_rate - - interval - - number_of_periods_in_year - - flag - Examples See Also

name: Append
text: Append Append Appends a Word file or Excel worksheet (or a part of the worksheet) to the current Word document. Format Append(filename) Parameter - filename - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the existing Word or Excel document. Logical names in filename are allowed. If we append Excel worksheet, filename format is file_name [, worksheet_number [, range]] where is: file_name - physical or logical file name, worksheet_number - number of worksheet, range - in the form start_cell : end_cell Default values: worksheet_number = 1, range of cells = whole sheet. For example "c:\temp\a.xls" "c:\temp\a.xls, 3" "c:\temp\a.xls, 3, A1:D10" "excel_dir:a.xls, 3, A1:D10" Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -3 File not found Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Append("c:\temp\a.doc") $stat = Word.Append("docdir:a.doc") $stat = Word.Append("c:\temp\a.xls") $stat = Word.Append("c:\temp\a.xls, 3") $stat = Word.Append("c:\temp\a.xls, 3, A1: F20") Using Word

name: Applications
text: Applications Applications Rebuild programs Generates one or more executable programs. This option gives us possibility to generate all the executable programs in an application. Rebuild Record Descriptions Generates records (tables) descriptions and stores them into text files. These files are used later by C compiler to generate an executable program. File extension is CLB. Show Prints program documentation and shows source code of global procedures. Undelete Programs When we delete a program in generator, the program will be just marked as removed. Physically, it stays in the Data Dictionary. This option gives us chance to restore such programs. Note: function 'Compress DD', removes these programs from the DD. After that, we can not undelete any removed program.

name: ASC
description: This function returns the code value of the leftmost character of alpha_exp.
text: ASC ASC This function returns the code value of the leftmost character of alpha_exp. Format ASC (alpha_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - alpha_exp - alpha field, ANSI or UTF-16. Notes If alpha_exp is ANSI field, result is a number in the range 0 to 255. If alpha_exp is UTF-16 field, result is number in the range 0 to 65535. Examples $N = ASC('1') !! result is 49 $N = ASC('A') !! result is 65 $N = ASC('ABC') !! result is 65 $N = ASC('@') !! result is 64 ! $UTF16 is defined as ! $UTF16 W 1 $UTF16 = 'Š' $N = ASC($UTF16) !! result is 352 See Also CHR, CHRW, String Functions

name: Assignment operator
text: Assignment operator Assignment operator (=) Assigns value(s) to a variable, array or record. Format 1 variable = value_expression Assigns the value of the right-hand value expression to the left-hand variable. The variable on the left side of the equal sign can be: - database field, - local field, - some of internal fields (acc_nn, counter_nn, eof, iaf_printmode, page_counter), - function name (within function body), - argument of the function (within function body), - recap field within define_recap_record block. Format 2 array1() = array2() Moves all the elements from the righ-hand array to the left-hand array. If the left-hand array has more elements than the right one, remain elements will be initialized. One variation of this format is alpha_array() = 'alpha_literal' Example: If $arr is an array of 10 elements, each element is 1 character long, then $arr() = '0000011111' has the same effect as $arr(1) = '0' $arr(2) = '0' ... $arr(10) = '1' Format 3 record_name1 = record_name2 Moves corresponding fields from the right-hand record buffer to the left-hand record buffer (see CORR). Format 4 filename.FILE_NAME = value_expression where filename is a file from Datasource dialog, F...

name: Autocomplete
text: Autocomplete Autocomplete In this dialog parameters for autocomplete operation can be entered . Strings source Array or a lookup dialog. Array holding autocomplete strings Enter name of array containing strings needing to be displayed in autocomplete list. The array should be filled before the field gets the focus. Number of strings Numeric literal or variable denoting real number of strings in the array. Lookup Name of lookup dialog used in autocomplete operation. SELECT statement in the dialog should be in the form SELECT text_field FROM mytable WHERE text_field LIKE :LOOKUP_FIELD ORDER BY text_field SELECT TOP n text_field FROM mytable WHERE text_field LIKE :LOOKUP_FIELD SELECT id,name FROM mytable WHERE id LIKE :LOOKUP_FIELD ORDER BY id Minimum number of characters Minimum number of characters a user has to type before the autocomplete operation activates. Delay Number of milliseconds the autocomplete waits after a keystroke to activate itself.

name: Autofit
text: Autofit Autofit This method fills-up whole grid if there exists a gap between the last column and right edge of the grid. Format Autofit ([column]) Parameters - column - the column whose width will be changed, a numeric variable or numeric literal. Row heading has value -1, the first column is 1, etc. If omitted, the last visible column will be adjusted. Applied To Grid. Note If you use Hidecolumn method, an empty space will appear at the right side of the grid. To remove this space, Autofit method can be used. Examples Table_1.Hidecolumn(10) !! Empty space appears Table_1.Autofit(1) !! Adjust the first column Table_1.Autofit(-1) !! Adjust row heading Table_1.Autofit() !! Adjust the last column See Methods

name: AVG
description: This function returns the average value of the input values for all processed records
text: AVG AVG This function returns the average value of the input values for all processed records between two break fields. Format AVG(numeric_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - numeric_exp - numeric expression Note This function can be used in reports and batch programs only. Examples See Also COUNT, MAX, MIN, TOTAL

name: Barcode printing
text: Barcode printing Barcode printing Barcode can be printed on laser or inkjet printers in the two ways: using a barcode font or via internal routines. Barcode font If you have a barcode font installed, just create sourcefield object, select Source field style = standard (this is the default) and choose barcode font (in 'Property' dialog). A little disadvantage is that the font must be installed on every PC running the program. Internal routines To use this feature, create sourcefield object and select Source field style = Barcode_XXX. The generator can print the following barcodes: EAN13, EAN8, Code 39, Code 93 and Code 128. EAN13 is numeric barcode used to identify product at the point of sales. Valid characters: 0123456789 Field length is fixed, 13 digits: 12 data digits + check_digit (modulo 10). If program variable contains 12 digits, the generator automatically calculates and adds check digit. EAN8 is short version of EAN13 for small products. This code can be used only if EAN13 occupies over 25% of the front sufrace of the product. Valid characters: 0123456789 Field length is fixed, 8 digits: 7 data digits + check_digit (modulo 10). If program variable contains 7 digits, the...

name: BASE64_DECODE
description: This function transforms data back from base64 format to the original data.
text: BASE64_DECODE BASE64_DECODE This function transforms data back from base64 format to the original data. Format BASE64_DECODE(base64_encoded_data, data_length) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - base64_encoded_data - input data in base64 format. - data_length - number of input bytes. Example $out = BASE64_DECODE($base64,str_length($base64)) See Also Base64_encode

name: BASE64_ENCODE
description: This function transforms input data to a base64 format.
text: BASE64_ENCODE BASE64_ENCODE This function transforms input data to a base64 format. Format BASE64_ENCODE(input_data, data_length) Return Value Type Alpha in base64 format. Parameters - input_data - input data, alpha or binary. - data_length - number of input bytes. Note Base64 data requires 33% more space than original data. Formula for output length calculation is: num_of_base64_chars = ((input_length+2)/3)*4 Example $out64 = BASE64_ENCODE($inp,str_length($inp)) See Also Base64_decode

name: BEGINM
description: This function returns a date representing beginning of the month of date_exp.
text: BEGINM BEGINM This function returns a date representing beginning of the month of date_exp. Format BEGINM(date_exp) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date_exp - input date or datetime. Examples $Date = BEGINM("12-mar-2001") !! result is 1-mar-2001 See Also ENDM, BEGINW, BEGINY, Date Functions

name: BEGINQ
description: This function returns a date representing beginning of the quarter of date_exp.
text: BEGINQ BEGINQ This function returns a date representing beginning of the quarter of date_exp. Format BEGINQ(date_exp) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date_exp - input date or datetime. Examples $Date = BEGINQ("12-mar-2001") !! result is 1-jan-2001 See Also ENDM, ENDQ, ENDW, ENDY, BEGINM, BEGINW, BEGINY, Date Functions

name: BEGINW
description: This function returns a date representing beginning of the week of date_exp. A week starts with Monday, according to ISO 8601.
text: BEGINW BEGINW This function returns a date representing beginning of the week of date_exp. A week starts with Monday, according to ISO 8601. Format BEGINW(date_exp) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date_exp - input date or datetime. Examples $begweek = BEGINW("27-nov-2006") !! result is 27-nov-2006 $begweek = BEGINW("28-nov-2006") !! result is 27-nov-2006 See Also ENDW, BEGINM, BEGINY, Date Functions

name: BEGINY
description: This function returns a date representing beginning of the year of date_exp.
text: BEGINY BEGINY This function returns a date representing beginning of the year of date_exp. Format BEGINY(date_exp) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date_exp - input date or datetime. Examples $Year = BEGINY("12-mar-2001") !! result is 1-jan-2001 See Also ENDY, BEGINM, BEGINW, Date Functions

name: Bitmap Button
text: Bitmap Button Bitmap Button The bitmap button has the same function as push button. Visually, this object displays text and/or an image. To insert new bitmap button on the form, click button BitmapButton in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/BitmapButton. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. Definition dialog appears on the screen. Choose one of images, enter text and click OK. New bitmap button is placed on the form. To change button properties double click on the button -or- select the button, right click to display context menu and choose Properties.

name: Bitmapbutton properties
text: Bitmapbutton properties Bitmapbutton properties Object name When the new button is created, generator automatically assigns the name Bitmapbutton_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Caption The text which appears on the button. If you want to define one letter as a prefix (mnemonic) character, precede it with ampersand (&). At run-time, that letter will be underlined and user can click that button by pressing Alt + letter. If you want to display single ampersand in the caption, enter two consecutive ampersands. Within one subform, all prefix characters must be different. To change caption text dynamically, use the Display method. Sequence number If the button has sequence number 0, click is allowed at all times if the button is enabled. If the button has nonzero sequence, click will be accepted only if all objects with the sma...

name: Bookmarks
text: Bookmarks Bookmarks The bookmarks will be written into the print file. The print file will be created if we click Preview button in Print dialog or if we set IAF_PRINTMODE internal variable to 9 or 12 (See Internal fields). Bookmark is document navigation tool allowing users to instantly skip to any location in the print file. If you use this feature, bookmarks tree will be displayed when you review print file. Usually, this tree resides on the left side of the screen. Create bookmarks Enables bookmark creation. Generate default bookmarks If bookmark creation is enabled, this option creates default bookmarks for the report. If this check-box is cleared, you have to create custom bookmarks using context menu command "Create bookmark: level N". Root node text options - Root node does not exist. - Document title - root node text is document title defined in Report parameters dialog. - Program caption - root node text is program title. - Value expression - root node text is an alpha literal or alpha variable. How to create default bookmarks? - Select 'Create bookmarks'. - Select 'Generate default bookmarks'. - Optionally set root node text. - Click OK. How to create custom bookmarks...

name: BREAK
description: This statement exits immediately innermost loop (DO_WHILE , READ_ALL or SELECT_ALL).
text: BREAK BREAK This statement exits immediately innermost loop (DO_WHILE , READ_ALL or SELECT_ALL). Format BREAK Example Select_all * from tbl If tbl.fld1 = 100 Break End_if ... End_select See also Do_while Read_all Select_all Next

name: BREAK_FIELD
description: This statement defines break fields in batch programs. When break field changes its value, some group (total) actions are executed.
text: BREAK_FIELD BREAK_FIELD This statement defines break fields in batch programs. When break field changes its value, some group (total) actions are executed. Format 1 BREAK_FIELD = field_1|field_2|...|field_n Format 2 BREAK_FIELD = LAST_RECORD Where - field_1,..., field_n - fields from input record. Maximum number of segments is 100. - LAST_RECORD - a special value which represents actions after the last record of the main file (table). Note This statement can be used in batch calculations after TOTAL_CALC only. Example DETAIL_CALC ... some_code_det ... TOTAL_CALC BREAK_FIELD = fld1 ... some_code_t1 ... BREAK_FIELD = LAST_RECORD ... some_code_t2 ... See also Detail calculations, Total calculations

name: Break fields definition
description: Break fields definition Break fields definition
text: Break fields definition Break fields definition A break is an event which causes printing of a lineset in a report, or activation subtotal calculations in a batch program. Break field is a field whose change activates the break, ie. on any change of this field, one of linesets will be printed. Break field can be defined for page header, subheaders and subtotals. Generally, the break field is a concatenated expression in the form field_1 | field_2 | field_3 | ... | field_N where field_1, ..., field_N belong to the tables participating in view definition. Change of any segment activates break. Break fields are tightly connected to sort order of input table(s). Sort keys from ORDER BY clause are candidates for break field segments. Let us look at the next view definition: Select * from inptbl order by CUSTOMER_ID, ORDER_NO If we define subheader break field as CUSTOMER_ID then we can display customer_id in the subheader, while the orders (sorted by order_no) can be printed in the detail line. Using functions in break field Allowed functions are: YEAR, MONTH, QUARTER, WEEK_OF_YEAR, LOWER and UPPER. Example: field_1 | field_2 | ... | week_of_year(date_field) This break will occur eve...

name: Button
text: Button Button The push button is an object enabling the user to execute some actions by clicking it. To insert new push button on the form, click button Button in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Button. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the top left corner of the button. Drag to the right and bottom and release the button. New push button with default text "Button" is placed on the form. The code buffer is empty. The code (actions to be performed when user clicks the button at run-time) can be changed by context menu command "Modify code". To change button properties double click on the button -or- select the button, right click to display context menu and choose Properties. See Bitmapbuton

name: Button properties
text: Button properties Button properties Object name When the new button is created, generator automatically assigns the name Button_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Caption The text which appears on the button. If you want to define one letter as a prefix (mnemonic) character, precede it with ampersand (&). At run-time, that letter will be underlined and user can click that button by pressing Alt + letter. If you want to display single ampersand in the caption, enter two consecutive ampersands. Within one subform, all prefix characters must be different. To change caption text dynamically, use the Display method. Sequence number If the button has sequence number 0, click is allowed at all times if the button is enabled. If the button has nonzero sequence, click will be accepted only if all objects with the smaller sequence numb...

name: CALL_PROGRAM
description: This statement calls an external executable program (EXE).
text: CALL_PROGRAM CALL_PROGRAM This statement calls an external executable program (EXE). Format CALL_PROGRAM [/WAIT][/NOWAIT][/NONIAF] program_name Where - program_name - name of the executable program (must be an alphanumeric literal or an alpha field) Qualifiers /WAIT Syncronous call, blocks calling program until execution of new program is complete. /NOWAIT Asyncronous call, continues to execute calling program concurrently with new one. /NONIAF New program was not generated by Iaf. Default is Iaf program. Note If neither /WAIT nor /NOWAIT appears in the line, Iaf use an internal algorithm to choose the type of the call: in button click actions, or toolbar_button click actions, call will be asyncronous. Otherwise, it will be syncronous (procedures, calculations, etc...). With /NONIAF, command line parameters can be used, i.e. CALL_PROGRAM /NONIAF "noniaf_program arg1 arg2 arg3" Examples CALL_PROGRAM "pgm1" CALL_PROGRAM /WAIT "c:\myappl\pgm" CALL_PROGRAM /WAIT/NONIAF "appl_exe:pgm" CALL_PROGRAM /WAIT/NONIAF "pgm arg1 arg2" $cmd = "vmslogin c:\appl" call_program $cmd

name: CALL_STOREDPROCEDURE
description: This statement calls a stored database procedure via ODBC.
text: CALL_STOREDPROCEDURE CALL_STOREDPROCEDURE This statement calls a stored database procedure via ODBC. Format CALL_STOREDPROCEDURE procname([arg1][,arg2][,...argN]) [RETURN_VALUE retval] Where - procname - name of the stored procedure. Procedure name is an identifier or a local alpha variable. - arg1,..., argN - arguments. - retval - return value, integer, optional parameter. Stored procedure can return (via RETURN statement) an integer value to the calling program. This value is usually used to indicate success or failure of the stored procedure. Notes If procedure name contain blanks, use [ and ] separators. Unused arguments can be omitted. An argument can be: NULL - null value literal, variable Examples CALL_STOREDPROCEDURE proc1(NULL, 'ABC',, $FLD1) CALL_STOREDPROCEDURE owner.proc1(NULL, 'ABC',, $FLD1) CALL_STOREDPROCEDURE [procname with blanks]('ABC') CALL_STOREDPROCEDURE p1('ABC') RETURN_VALUE $retval ! where $retval is numeric local field ! Local variable can be used as procedure name $procname = 'owner.proc1' CALL_STOREDPROCEDURE $procname(NULL, 'ABC',, $FLD1)

name: CALL_WINDOW
text: CALL_WINDOW CALL_WINDOW This statement calls a window (subform). Format CALL_WINDOW window_number Where - window_number - number of the window, numeric field. Note It's value has to be greater than zero and less or equal to a number of subforms in the program. Example CALL_WINDOW 5 !! activates 5th subform $subform = 3 CALL_WINDOW $subform

name: CEIL
description: This function rounds a numeric value.
text: CEIL CEIL This function rounds a numeric value. Format CEIL(numeric_exp, ceil_factor) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - numeric_exp - numeric value to be rounded. - ceil_factor - ceil factor, numeric. Note Ceil_factor is round position. If ceil_factor >= 0, integer part of numeric_exp will be rounded. If ceil_factor < 0, decimal part of numeric_exp will be rounded. Ceil_factor is defined as follows: Numeric value 1 8 2, 5 6 Ceil factor 3 2 1 0 -1 -2 The first digit after decimal point has ceil_factor -1. The first digit left from decimal point has ceil_factor 0. Ceil factor -3 means: round the third decimal to 0. Ceil factor -2 means: round the second decimal to 0. Ceil factor -1 means: round the first decimal to 0. Algorithm: If the digit at round position has value less than 5, it will be rounded to 0. All digits right from this position will be set to 0. All digits left from this position stay unchanged. If the digit at round position has value greater or equal 5, it will be rounded to 0, but first digit left from this position will be incremented by 1. All digits right from this position will be set to 0. Examples acc_1 = 100,56 $C = CEIL(acc_1,-2) !! result is 100,60 ...

name: CELL
description: This function returns a cell contents from a grid.
examples: The function is OBSOLETE, see Getcell method
text: CELL CELL This function returns a cell contents from a grid. Format CELL(grid_name,line,column) Return Value Type Any type. Parameters - grid_name - name of a grid object, text. - line - line, integer. Special values CURRENT_LINE or * denote current line. - column - column, integer Note If we try to read a CHECKBOX column, result is 0 (not checked) or 1 (checked) despite of check box definition (Destination=field/checked:"X"/unchecked:"Y"). Examples acc_1 = cell(table_1,*,1) !! line=current line, column=1 acc_1 = cell(table_1,CURRENT_LINE,1) !! current line acc_1 = cell(table_1,counter_1,1) The function is OBSOLETE, see Getcell method

name: CHAIN
description: This statement calls another executable program.
text: CHAIN CHAIN This statement calls another executable program. Format CHAIN program_name Where - program_name - name of the executable program (must be an alphanumeric literal or an alpha field). Command line parameters can be used. Note The current (active) program stops the execution after activating next program. Examples CHAIN "nextpgm" CHAIN "nextpgm PARAM=1" CHAIN "c:\test\pgm1" CHAIN "apli_exe:pgm1" $pgm = "P1.EXE" CHAIN $pgm

name: Change Password
text: Change Password Change Password

name: Change Setup Parameters
description: Change Setup Parameters Change Setup Parameters
text: Change Setup Parameters Change Setup Parameters Application description Short description of the application. Decimal point Decimal point determines the way of defining edit mask for numeric fields. If you select comma, decimal separator is COMMA and group separator is POINT, for example 1.000,00. If you select point, decimal separator is POINT and group separator is COMMA, for example 1,000.00. This setting also affects numeric literal notation (see Value expression): Decimal point Numeric literal COMMA 100,00 POINT 100.00 Default date edit mask When we insert sourcefield object or destinationfield object in form painter, and field type is date, this edit mask will appear on the screen. Timeout period This parameter applies to the all programs in the application. If there is no user's activities (keyboard, mouse movement) in that period, current transaction will be rolled back, and program will be stopped. Timeout period unit is minute. For instance, value 10 means ten minutes. Value -1 (minus 1) means no timeout. File system If the application use flat files only, select Classic. Otherwise, if you use any of relational databases (MS Access, MS SQL Server, Oracle, DB2, Informix...

name: CHAR_OF
description: This function extracts one character from an alpha_exp.
text: CHAR_OF CHAR_OF This function extracts one character from an alpha_exp. Format CHAR_OF(alpha_exp, position) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - input field from which a character has to be extracted, alpha. - position - character position, numeric. Note The leftmost character has position 1. Examples $A = CHAR_OF("ABCD",3) !! result is "C" See Also String Functions

name: CHECK
examples: ERRMSG = "Entered value must be less than 100"
text: CHECK CHECK This statement controls entered value. Format CHECK cond_exp Where - cond_exp - a conditional expression (which determines control under entered field) Note CHECK can be used after a DESTINATION statement. CHECK must be followed by a ERRMSG or WARMSG. Example DESTINATION = field1 CHECK field1 < 100 ERRMSG = "Entered value must be less than 100"

name: Check Box
text: Check Box Check Box We can consider check box as a destination field which stores one of two values: 0 or 1, "Y" or "N", etc. To insert new checkbox on the form, click button Checkox in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Checkbox. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. Definition dialog appears on the screen. Caption Check box label Variable Destination variable. Run-time engine will move one of two values into this variable. Unchecked This value will be moved into destination variable if check box is not checked. Checked This value will be moved into destination variable if check box is checked. Default Default value will be selected when runtime displays the form. Read only If check box is read only, mouse click and keyboard input is not allowed. New checkbox is placed on the form: check box button and text label are displayed on the screen, and one line is placed to the code buffer. The line is Destination = variable_name/checked:V1/unchecked:V2 See Destination statement. The check box object has the two components: visual appearance (check box button and text label) and code buffer (contains statements defining the output variable, "checked" value, "uncheck...

name: Checkbox properties
text: Checkbox properties Checkbox properties Object name When the new checkbox is created, generator automatically assigns the name Checkbox_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Caption The text which appears on the button. If you want to define one letter as a prefix (mnemonic) character, precede it with ampersand (&). At run-time, that letter will be underlined and user can click that button by pressing Alt + letter. If you want to display single ampersand in the caption, enter two consecutive ampersands. Within one subform, all prefix characters must be different. Field name (information only, cannot be changed) Name of the destination field. In IAF, data entered via keyboard must be recorded somewhere: into a table field, a local field, a counter, an accumulator. This place we call destination field. Checked value (information o...

name: CHECK_DIGIT
text: CHECK_DIGIT CHECK_DIGIT This function calculates check digit. Format CHECK_DIGIT (field, field_length, modulo, qualifier) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - field - alphanumeric, unpacked numeric field - field_length - number of characters - modulo, qualifier Modulo Qualifier Description 10 0 modulo 10, weight digits 1,2 10 1 modulo 10, weight digits 1,2 reduced to single digit (IBM check for credit cards) 10 2 modulo 10, weight digits 1,3,7 (routing numbers for EFT) 11 0 modulo 11, weight digits 7,6,5,4,3,2; all numbers included 11 1 modulo 11, weight digits 7,6,5,4,3,2; ignore remainders 0 and 1 11 2 ISBN mod 11 check 1000 0 barcode check digit, EAN13, EAN8 Examples $Dgt = CHECK_DIGIT("0001",4,11,0) !! result is 9 $Dgt = CHECK_DIGIT("860016960002",12,1000,0) !! result is 2 $Dgt = CHECK_DIGIT("2050000000011742",16,97,0) !! result is 7 $fld = alltrim($fld) | check_digit($fld,12,1000,0) See Also

name: CHECK_RECORD
description: This statement controls (non)existing record with given key.
examples: ERRMSG = "Record with key AAA does not exist"
text: CHECK_RECORD CHECK_RECORD This statement controls (non)existing record with given key. Format CHECK_RECORD[/TRIM] file_name [PART_]KEY[n] = key [WHERE cond_exp] Where - file_name - file (table) from where the record is read. - n - key number: 1 for primary, .... If omitted, primary key will be used. - key - concatenation which represents the key of the file (table). - cond_exp - condition for record check. Qualifiers /TRIM trims trailing blanks from last key segment. Record is read with new key length. This applies only if the last key segment is alphanumeric. Prefix PART_ can be used only if the number of key segments is greater than 1. Note This statement is mainly intended to use with index-sequential files like Btrieve. /TRIM and PART_ can be used with indexed files only. Not available with RDB tables. Example CHECK_RECORD file1 KEY="AAA" ERRMSG = "Record with key AAA does not exist" ! Another way to control record existence READ file1 key="AAA" CHECK record file1 found ERRMSG = "Record with key AAA does not exist" See also CHECK, READ, SELECT, ERRMSG

name: CHR
description: This function returns the character that has the ASCII code value specified by numeric_exp.
examples: $A = CHR(9) !! result is a TAB character
$A = CHR(13) !! result is a carriage return char
$A = CHR(27) !! result is an ESC character
text: CHR CHR This function returns the character that has the ASCII code value specified by numeric_exp. Numeric_exp must be in the range 0 to 255. Format CHR(numeric_exp) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - numeric_exp - numeric literal or numeric field in the range 0 to 255. Examples $A = CHR(9) !! result is a TAB character $A = CHR(13) !! result is a carriage return char $A = CHR(27) !! result is an ESC character $A = CHR(65) !! result is "A" See Also ASC, CHRW, String Functions

name: CHRW
description: This function returns the wide character (Unicode) that has the code value specified by numeric_exp.
examples: $W = CHRW(9) !! result is a TAB haracter
$W = CHRW(27) !! result is an ESC character
text: CHRW CHRW This function returns the wide character (Unicode) that has the code value specified by numeric_exp. Format CHRW(numeric_exp) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - numeric_exp - numeric literal or numeric field in the range 0 to 65535. Examples $W = CHRW(9) !! result is a TAB haracter $W = CHRW(27) !! result is an ESC character $W = CHRW(65) !! result is "A" $W = CHRW(1040) !! result is cyrillic "A" See Also ASC, CHR, String Functions

name: Click actions
text: Click actions Click actions The diagram bellow shows how run-time engine processes click when a data-entry grid has the focus.

name: CLOSE
description: This statement closes a file or database.
text: CLOSE CLOSE This statement closes a file or database. Format 1 CLOSE[/DELETE] file_name Format 2 CLOSE /DATABASE Where - file_name - a name of the file from datasource dialog. Qualifiers /DELETE deletes file after closing. /DATABASE closes the connection to a database. Example CLOSE file1 CLOSE/DELETE file1 CLOSE/DATABASE See Also OPEN File.Close

name: Close
description: This method closes a file opened by Open method. After this, another file can be opened, etc.
text: Close Close This method closes a file opened by Open method. After this, another file can be opened, etc. Format Close() Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 File not opened Applied To File object. Example $stat = File.Close() See also Open Working with files

name: Close
description: This method closes the connection to the FTP server. After this action, another connection can be established, etc.
examples: Ftp.Close() !! If you ignore return value
text: Close Close This method closes the connection to the FTP server. After this action, another connection can be established, etc. Format Close() Return Value Alpha variable. Applied To Ftp object. Example $msg = Ftp.Close() Ftp.Close() !! If you ignore return value Using FTP See also Open

name: Closetable
text: Closetable Closetable This method closes previously opened table. Format Closetable() Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Closetable() Using Word See also Opentable, Setcell

name: Close
description: This method closes XML file opened by Open method. After this, another XML file can be opened, etc.
text: Close Close This method closes XML file opened by Open method. After this, another XML file can be opened, etc. Format Close() Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 File not opened Applied To XML object. Example $stat = Xml.Close() See also Open Using XML

name: IAF Help
text: IAF Help Collation names Collation name has the format Language[_CI] where _CI means case-insensitive. Examples: English : English alphabet, case-sensitive English_CI : English alphabet, case-insensitive Serbian_Cyrillic Serbian_Cyrillic_CI Serbian_Latin Serbian_Latin_CI Languages: Afrikaans Albanian Arabic Armenian Azeri_Cyrillic Azeri_Latin Basque Belarusian Bulgarian Catalan Chinese Croatian Czech Danish Divehi Dutch English Estonian Faeroese Farsi Finnish French Galician Georgian German Greek Gujarat Hebrew Hindi Hungarian Icelandic Indonesian Italian Japanese Kannada Kazakh Konkani Korean Kyrgyz Latvian Lithuanian Macedonian Malay Marathi Mongolian Norwegian Polish Portuguese Punjab_Gurmuhki Romanian Russian Sanskrit Serbian_Cyrillic Serbian_Latin Slovak Slovenian Spanish Swahili Swedish Syriac Tamil Tatar Telugu Thai Turkish Ukrainian Urdu Uzbek_Latin Uzbek_Cyrillic Vietnamese

name: Column heading properties
description: Column heading properties Column heading properties
text: Column heading properties Column heading properties In this dialog we can set some attributes for column heading text. These attributes can be different from appropriate field attributes. Background color This attribute displays selected color or text Default color if the color is the same as grid default color. Double click brings up a color definition dialog. Text color This attribute displays selected color or text Default color if the color is the same as grid default color. Double click brings up a color definition dialog. Font name Choose one from offered font names. Font size Specifies font size of the text. Bold Specify that font weight is 700. Normal weight is 400. Italic Specify whether text is italic. Underline Specify whether text is underlined. Horizontal text alignment Values: Left Center Right Default is Center.

name: Combo Box
text: Combo Box Combo Box We can consider combo box as a destination field which stores one of many values: 0 or 1 or 2 or 3, "A" or "B" or "C" or ... See also Radio button. To select many items at the same time, use Data selector. To insert new combobox on the form, click button Combobox in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Combobox. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. Definition dialog appears on the screen. New combobox is placed on the form. Iaf supports drop-down list style: list-box portion is visible when dropped down and user cannot type any character in edit-box portion To change combobox properties double click on the combobox -or- select the combobox, right click to display context menu and choose Properties.

name: Combobox properties
text: Combobox properties Combobox properties Object name When the new combobox is created, generator automatically assigns the name Combobox_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Field name (information only, cannot be changed) Name of the destination field. In IAF, data entered via keyboard must be recorded somewhere: into a table field, a local field, a counter, an accumulator. This place we call destination field. Show combo as Listbox If there is enough space on the form, a list box can be created instead of a combobox. Just increase object height and select this check box. By default, vertical scroll bar is hidden. If the list box contains number of items that can not fit the visible space, vertical scroll bar will automatically appear. See examples in InsertItemEx. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subfo...

name: Command line parameters
description: Command line parameters Command line parameters
text: Command line parameters Command line parameters Programs generated by Iaf can use command line parameters. There are three named parameters recognized by an Iaf program: WORK, DSN and PARAM. WORK - specifies current (working) directory for the program. Parameter value must contain fully qualified path. Quotation marks are required if the path contains spaces. If this parameter does not appear in the command line, the program uses a previously defined working directory. Current (working) directory is the location on which will be created internal files during the sort. Examples: command_line> program.exe WORK=C:\MYPROG command_line> program.exe WORK="c:\Program Files\myprog" DSN - datasource name. Program connects to datasource defined by this parameter. If this parameter does not exist, program checks existence of the DSN_applname symbol in the Registry (key value is HKEYCurrentUser\Software\Sing\Iafw\RT). If the symbol does not exist, program uses datasource defined by the first table in the datasource dialog. Let's remember that 'Load data definition' records table definition and appropriate datasource name into Iaf DD. Example: command_line> program.exe DSN=GK;UID=myuser;PWD=...

name: COMMIT
text: COMMIT COMMIT This statement confirms transaction. Format COMMIT Note If program uses classic files only (Btrieve, RMS, seq), this statement will be ignored. Example COMMIT See also ROLLBACK

name: COMPARE_COLLATE
description: This function compares two alpha fields according to a collation.
text: COMPARE_COLLATE COMPARE_COLLATE This function compares two alpha fields according to a collation. Format COMPARE_COLLATE(str1, str2, collation_name) Return Value Type Numeric. Result value: < 0 if str1 < str2 > 0 if str1 > str2 = 0 if str1 = str2 Parameters - str1 - the first alpha field. - str2 - the second alpha field. - collation_name - alpha literal denoting collation. Note Examples $res = COMPARE_COLLATE(str1, str2, 'English') $res = COMPARE_COLLATE(str1, str2, 'English_CI') $res = COMPARE_COLLATE('A', 'B', 'English') !! $res =-1 $res = COMPARE_COLLATE('C', 'B', 'English') !! $res = 1 $res = COMPARE_COLLATE('A', 'a', 'English') !! $res =-1 $res = COMPARE_COLLATE('A', 'a', 'English_CI') !! $res = 0 See Also

name: COMPARE_RECORDS
description: This function compares two record buffers, field by field. CLOB and BLOB fields are ignored.
text: COMPARE_RECORDS COMPARE_RECORDS This function compares two record buffers, field by field. CLOB and BLOB fields are ignored. Format 1 Compare all fields COMPARE_RECORDS(rec1, rec2) Format 2 Compare all fields except Field1, Field2, ... COMPARE_RECORDS(rec1, rec2,'E',field1,field2, ...) Format 3 Compare only fields Field1, Field2, ... COMPARE_RECORDS(rec1, rec2,'I',field1,field2, ...) Return Value Type Numeric. Result value: = 0 if rec1 = rec2 != 0 if rec1 != rec2 Parameters - rec1 - the first record, from Datasource dialog. - rec2 - the second record, from Datasource dialog. - field1, field2, ...- list of fields. Note Records REC1 and REC2 have the same structure. Examples $res = COMPARE_RECORDS(rec1, rec2) $res = COMPARE_RECORDS(rec1, rec2, 'E', fld1) $res = COMPARE_RECORDS(rec1, rec2, 'I', fld1,fld2) See Also

name: Comparison operators
text: Comparison operators Comparison operators Comparison operators are: = equal to <> not equal to > greater than >= greater than or equal to < less than <= less than or equal to is [not] containing Result is TRUE if an alpha field contains a text, case-sensitive. Example: IS containing 'abc' is [not] date Result is TRUE if an alpha field contains valid date in the form YYYYMMDD. If alpha field is 6 characters long, the date form is YYMMDD. is [not] holiday Result is TRUE if a date field is a holiday. is [not] in Result is TRUE if a field matches any value in the list. A list consists of items separated by semicolon. The item can be a field or a range in the form lower_limit ... upper_limit. For example: IS in (1; 3; 5; 100) IS in (1; 3; 5 ... 80; 150 ...) IS in ('A'; 'B'; 'F' ... 'H') IS NOT in (50; 100 ... 200; 350; 500...) is [not] in_masktable is [not] matching Result is TRUE if an alpha field matches a specified pattern, case-sensitive. Special characters in pattern are: * one or more characters ? any character # any digit [charlist] any character in charlist [!charlist] any character not in charlist Examples: IS matching '*abc*' IS matching 'AB*' IS matching '*AB' IS matching ...

name: COMPRESS_CSV
description: This function creates a concatenation of many trimmed (left and right) values adding a separator at the end of each input value. Resulting string can be written to a CSV file. Length of separator is one character. If input variables are numeric or date/time...
examples: $A = COMPRESS_CSV(';', 'AB C ', ' AB', 'X') !! result is "AB C";"AB";"X"
text: COMPRESS_CSV COMPRESS_CSV This function creates a concatenation of many trimmed (left and right) values adding a separator at the end of each input value. Resulting string can be written to a CSV file. Length of separator is one character. If input variables are numeric or date/time ones, default editing will be applied. Format COMPRESS_CSV(separator, val_exp1, val_exp2, ..., val_expN) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - separator - one character. - val_exp1,..., val_expN - input variables. Maximum number of input values is 512. Notes - All alpha fields are surrounded by quotation marks. - One quotation mark in input field will be replaced by two quotation marks. - Special function LEADZERO (alpha_field) can be used as an input parameter. This function preserves leading zeros (blanks) when MS Excel loads CSV file. - Special function BLANKZERO(num_field) can be used as an input parameter. This function writes out empty (null) value if input field is zero. - Special function DATEONLY(datetime_field) can be used as an input parameter. This function writes out date part of a datetime field. - Special function MAX255(alpha_field) can be used as an input parameter. This function wri...

name: COMPRESS
description: This function creates a concatenation of many values adding one blank at the end of each input value. Input val_exp are left and right trimmed. If input variables are numeric or date/time ones, default editing will be performed.
examples: $C = COMPRESS("ABC", " AB", "XX") !! result is "ABC AB XX"
text: COMPRESS COMPRESS This function creates a concatenation of many values adding one blank at the end of each input value. Input val_exp are left and right trimmed. If input variables are numeric or date/time ones, default editing will be performed. Format COMPRESS(val_exp1, val_exp2, ..., val_expN) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - val_exp1,..., val_expN - input variables. Maximal number of input values is 512. Note If the length of an input value is 1, run-time engine supposes that the input value is an user-supplied separator and does not insert one blank space before it. Default edit mask for numeric variables includes leading minus, see edit mask. Examples $C = COMPRESS("ABC", " AB", "XX") !! result is "ABC AB XX" $C = COMPRESS("ABC", ",", "XX") !! result is "ABC,XX" $C = COMPRESS(counter_1, acc_1, counter_2) See Also COMPRESS_SEP, COMPRESS_TRIM, String Functions

name: COMPRESS_SEP
description: This function creates a concatenation of many trimmed (left and right) values adding a separator at the end of each input value. Length of separator is one character. If input variables are numeric or date/time ones, default editing will be performed.
examples: $A = COMPRESS_SEP(";", "AB C ", " AB", "X") !! result is "AB C;AB;X"
text: COMPRESS_SEP COMPRESS_SEP This function creates a concatenation of many trimmed (left and right) values adding a separator at the end of each input value. Length of separator is one character. If input variables are numeric or date/time ones, default editing will be performed. Format COMPRESS_SEP(separator, val_exp1, val_exp2, ..., val_expN) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - separator - one character. - val_exp1,..., val_expN - input variables. Maximal number of input values is 512. Example $A = COMPRESS_SEP(";", "AB C ", " AB", "X") !! result is "AB C;AB;X" $A = COMPRESS_SEP(CHR(9), 'AB C ', ' AB', 'X') !! separator=TAB See Also COMPRESS, COMPRESS_CSV, COMPRESS_TRIM, SPLIT, String Functions

name: COMPRESS_TRIM
description: This function creates a concatenation of many trimmed (left and right) input variables. Only leading and trailing SPACE characters are trimmed (removed from input variables).
examples: $A = COMPRESS_TRIM("AB C ", " AB", "X") !! result is "AB CABX"
text: COMPRESS_TRIM COMPRESS_TRIM This function creates a concatenation of many trimmed (left and right) input variables. Only leading and trailing SPACE characters are trimmed (removed from input variables). If input variables are numeric or date/time ones, default editing will be performed. Format COMPRESS_TRIM(val_exp1, val_exp2, ..., val_expN) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - val_exp1,..., val_expN - input variables. Maximum number of input values is 512. Special constant NBSP (non-breaking space) can be used as input variable. This constant indicate that one space will be inserted into the output buffer. Note Trim operation, used in this function, is a little bit different from the other trim functions (LTRIM, RTRIM, ALLTRIM). COMPRESS_TRIM removes SPACE characters only, while LTRIM, RTRIM and ALLTRIM remove SPACE, TAB, newline and carriage return. Example $A = COMPRESS_TRIM("AB C ", " AB", "X") !! result is "AB CABX" $A = COMPRESS_TRIM("ABC",NBSP,"AB") !! result is "ABC AB" See Also COMPRESS, COMPRESS_SEP, LTRIM, RTRIM, ALLTRIM, String Functions

name: CONCAT
description: This function concatenates many alpha fields into one string.
text: CONCAT CONCAT This function concatenates many alpha fields into one string. Format CONCAT (val_exp1, val_exp2, ..., val_expN) Return Value Type Alpha or memo. Parameters - val_exp1,..., val_expN - input variables. Maximal number of input variables is 512. Notes - To append text to a string use the format $s = CONCAT ($s,'any text') or $s = CONCAT (alltrim($s),'any text') - Default editing (as in COMPRESS functions) is not applied to input variables. Therefore, integer, packed or double floating numeric cannot be used as input variable. Unpacked numeric, date or time field will be stored in its internal form. Examples $S = CONCAT("AB C", "AB", "X") $S = CONCAT(alltrim($s), 'some text') $S = CONCAT($s, alltrim($s1), alltrim($s2)) $S = CONCAT($s, edit(acc_1,'ZZZ.ZZ9,99'), alltrim($s2)) See Also COMPRESS, COMPRESS_SEP, LTRIM, RTRIM, ALLTRIM, String Functions

name: Conditional Expression
text: Conditional Expression Conditional Expression A conditional expression (cond_exp) is a symbol or string of symbols used to calculate one of two values: true or false. Conditional expression consist of value expressions, comparison operators and logical operators.

name: Constants
text: Constants Constants Constants are predefined fields whose values cannot be changed, i.e. these fields cannot be used on the left side of an assignment. BEGINCM - date, beginning of the current month BEGINCY - date, beginning of the current year BEGIN_Q1 - date, beginning of the 1st quarter of the current year BEGIN_Q2 - date, beginning of the 2nd quarter of the current year BEGIN_Q3 - date, beginning of the 3rd quarter of the current year BEGIN_Q4 - date, beginning of the 4th quarter of the current year BEGIN_QC - date, beginning of the current quarter CRLF - carriage return/line feed pair, alphanumeric constant, 2 bytes, hex value = 0D 0A ENDCY - date, end of current year END_Q1 - date, end of 1st quarter of the current year END_Q2 - date, end of 2nd quarter of the current year END_Q3 - date, end of 3rd quarter of the current year END_Q4 - date, end of 4th quarter of the current year END_QC - date, end of the current quarter NBSP - non-breaking space, used in COMPRESS_TRIM only NULL - null value, can be used to assign null value to a RDB table field PI - Ludolph's number, value is 3,14159265358979 P_MODE - alpha, program mode ("I" - main program, "S" - subprogram called by call...

name: Context menu definition
description: Context menu definition Context menu definition
text: Context menu definition Context menu definition Context menu is a popup menu displayed as the result of a right-click within the form. There are two phases in context menu definition process. The first, context menu layout has to be defined. The second, the event handlers for each menu item should be programmed. If, for particular menu item, event handler does not exist, this item will be disabled at run-time. Add new menu item text Enter the text in "Menu item text" field and click "Add text". This text appears on the context menu panel, along with menu item identifier. This identifier is automatically assigned by generator. Default identifiers are cmitem_1, cmitem_2, etc. Menu item identifier is a sequence of English letters, digits, underscore character(_) and dollar sign ($). The first character must be a letter (A to Z). Maximum number of characters in an identifier is 30. Identifiers are case-insensitive, so CMITEM_1 and cmitem_1 denotes the same menu item. Add separator Click "Add separator". Menu separator appears on the context menu panel. Delete menu item (text or separator) Right-click the menu item on context menu panel. Select "Delete menu item" from the context men...

name: Context menu item handlers
description: Context menu item handlers Context menu item handlers
text: Context menu item handlers Context menu item handlers Each event handler has the form ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE OnClickMenuItem(menu_item_identifier) ... event handler code ... END_PROCEDURE On_event_procedure and OnClickMenuItem are keywords denoting the start of the handler. Menu_item_identifier defines menu item, case-insensitive. End_procedure denotes end of the particular handler. When you select menu command Define/Context menu/Context menu code for the first time, the skeleton for all the handlers will be stored automatically into the code buffer. Enabling/disabling menu items at run-time To enable/disable a menu item, Setenable method can be used in the form menu_item_identifier.SetEnable(flag) See also On_event_procedure

name: CONVERT
description: This function converts an alpha_exp from one character set to another.
text: CONVERT CONVERT This function converts an alpha_exp from one character set to another. Format CONVERT(alpha_exp, source_code_page, dest_code_page) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - alpha field - source_code_page - code page of alpha_exp - dest_code_page - code page to which alpha_exp is converted - code_page - code page Note Both the destination and the source code page arguments can be either numeric literals or numeric fields. Example $Out_Alpha = CONVERT($inp_alpha,437,1250) See Also TRANSLITER String functions

name: CONVERT_DMY_YMD
description: This function converts a numeric or alphanumeric field to the date value.
text: CONVERT_DMY_YMD CONVERT_DMY_YMD This function converts a numeric or alphanumeric field to the date value. The input field contains date value in the form DDMMYY[YY]. Format CONVERT_DMY_YMD(input_value) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - input_value - Note This functions is usually used to import data from external applications. Example $Date_Value = CONVERT_DMY_YMD("01102002") See Also

name: CONVERT_LIT_YMD
description: This function converts a date literal to the date value.
text: CONVERT_LIT_YMD CONVERT_LIT_YMD This function converts a date literal to the date value. Format CONVERT_LIT_YMD(alpha_exp) Result Value Type Date. Parameters - alpha_exp - Example $Date = CONVERT_LIT_YMD("2-jan-2000") See Also

name: CONVERT_MDY_YDM
description: This function converts a numeric or alphanumeric field to the date value.
text: CONVERT_MDY_YDM CONVERT_MDY_YMD This function converts a numeric or alphanumeric field to the date value. The input field contains date value in the form MMDDYY[YY]. Format CONVERT_MDY_YMD(input_value) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - input_value - Note This functions is usually used to import data from external applications. Example $Date_Value = CONVERT_MDY_YMD("01312002") See Also

name: CONVERT_YMD_MDY
description: This function converts a date value to an alphanumeric one.
text: CONVERT_YMD_MDY CONVERT_YMD_MDY This function converts a date value to an alphanumeric one. Format CONVERT_YMD_MDY(date_exp) Result Value Type Alpha. Parameters - date_exp - Note Result is a 8 or 6-character alphanumeric value. The first two characters denote the month of date_exp, the second two characters denote the day of date_exp, and last four (two) digits denote year of date_exp. This functions is usually used to export data to external applications. Example $C = CONVERT_YMD_MDY("1-feb-2001") !! result is "02012001" See Also

name: Copy data definitions
description: Copy data definitions Copy data definitions
text: Copy data definitions Copy data definitions List List on the left-side of the dialog shows all tables, synonyms and views belonging to the selected ODBC datasource. To copy tables definitions from database to your DD, just select one or more tables and click 'Copy to DD'. A view will be considered as an ordinary table. Select all Click this button to select all the tables/views/synonyms in the list. Copy to DD Click this button to copy definitions to Data Dictionary. Consider text data as Unicode This check-box is visible if we load definitions from MS Access database only. If the control is checked, all text fields will be described in Data Dictionary as Unicode. This option is recommended because the text data in MS Access are stored in Unicode format. Otherwise, text fields in Data Dictionary will be described as ANSI fields. This solution can bring us some difficulties at run-time.

name: Copy program
text: Copy program Copy program Create a copy of a program with, possible, renaming. From Program to be copied (input program). To Program to be created (output program). Is we copy programs within the same application, this name must be different from input program name. Output application If our Data Dictionary contains many applications, we can copy (move) a program from one application to another one. Copy Make new copy of a program. Move Move (rename) program from one application to another one. If the both applications are the same, this is an ordinary rename operation. Note Form painter will be activated immediately, if we copy a program within the same application. Error messages If output program name is blank or invalid, error message is: "Invalid program name". If the output program with the same type already exists, error message appears on the screen: "Program already exists". If the output program with different type already exists (input is interactive one, output is report, and vice versa), error message is "Non-unique program name".

name: CORR
description: This function moves corresponding fields from one table (record) to another table (record).
text: CORR CORR This function moves corresponding fields from one table (record) to another table (record). Format 1 output_record = CORR(input_record) Format 2 output_file.output_record = CORR(input_file.input_record) Return Value Type None. Parameters - input_record - input record Note Corresponding fields are fields which have equal names and types in both tables. The following table displays allowed combinations. Source field Destination field ANSI alpha (char) Unpacked numeric ANSI alpha (char) Unicode UTF-16 Numeric Date/datetime (1) Date/datetime Time (1) Possible datetime combinations: date -> date date -> datetime datetime -> date datetime -> datetime Examples ! Table_1 descr Table_2 descr ! fld1, type A fld1, type A ! fld1x, type N fld2, type N ! fld2, type N fld3, type D ! fld3, type D fld4, type D ! fld4, type T table_1 = CORR(table_2) ! Result: ! ! table_1.fld1 = table_2.fld1 ! table_1.fld2 = table_2.fld2 ! table_1.fld3 = table_2.fld3 ! !If file File1 has two record redefinitions (rec1, rec2) !and File2 has redefinitions rec11 and rec12, we can use !the following formats: File1.rec1 = CORR(file2.rec2) File1.rec2 = CORR(file2.rec1) File1 = CORR(file2) !! defaults=rec1,rec1...

name: COUNT
description: This aggregate function returns number of records between two break fields.
text: COUNT COUNT This aggregate function returns number of records between two break fields. Format COUNT [(cond_expr)] Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - cond_expr - optional conditional expression. If cond_expr does not exist, all records from the input record stream will be counted. If cond_expr is specified, only records satisfying cond_expr will be counted. Notes - Cond_expr is calculated in detail lineset. - This function can be used in reports and batch programs only. Examples Source = COUNT Source = COUNT(fld1 > 0) See Also AVG, MAX, MIN, TOTAL

name: CreateDirectory
text: CreateDirectory CreateDirectory This method creates new directory on the connected FTP server. Format Createdirectory(dirname) Parameters - dirname - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies directory name. Return Value Alpha variable. If connecting was successful, value is blank. Otherwise, the variable holds an error message. Applied To Ftp object. Note Examples $msg = Ftp.Createdirectory('/dir1/dir2') if $msg <> SPACE Pause 'Error: ' | $msg end_if Using FTP

name: Create New Application
description: Create New Application Create New Application
text: Create New Application Create New Application Up to 10 applications can be stored into one Data Dictionary. Enter new application name. The application name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. The first character must be a letter.

name: Create New Data Dictionary
description: Create New Data Dictionary Create New Data Dictionary
text: Create New Data Dictionary Create New Data Dictionary A Data Dictionary is place (warehouse) which contains all the necessary information about the application(s). Data Dictionary name Data Dictionary name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. The first character must be a letter. Location Location is directory (folder) for new Data Dictionary. You can click Browse button to select an existing directory or manually enter the path if the directory does not exist. Application name Application name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. The first character must be a letter. The application name need to represent nature of job which should be processed, like PERSONNEL, PAYROLL, INVENTORY, etc. Protect Data Dictionary by password If you want to protect your Data Dictionary by password, select this check box. In that case, an user attempting to open the Data Dictionary must enter the password. Password The password is up to eight characters long. Click Create DD. If the directory does not exist, message box "Directory does not exist. Create it?" appears on the screen. Click Yes if you want to continue. After ...

name: Create record definition from datasource
description: Create record definition from datasource Create record definition from datasource
text: Create record definition from datasource Create record definition from datasource

name: Create SQL script from datasource
description: Create SQL script from datasource Create SQL script from datasource
text: Create SQL script from datasource Create SQL script from datasource This dialog enables creating SQL script directly from an ODBC datasource. List shows all the tables defined in datasource. Select one or more tables and click "Create SQL script" button.

name: Create SQL script from record definition
description: Create SQL script from record definition Create SQL script from record definition
text: Create SQL script from record definition Create SQL script from record definition Available records List on the left-side of the dialog shows all the records defined in Data Dictionary. Select one or more records and click button ">" to move them to the right-side list. Rdb tables SQL script will be created for all records in this list. To remove record from the list, select it and click button "<". Database Select one the the databases. Depending on this choice, various keywords will be used in the script file. Example: the data type containing data and time in MS SQL Server is DATETIME, in Oracle is DATE, in DB2 is TIMESTAMP. Create SQL script Click this button to create SQL script for selected tables. Result is a text file with extension SQL on default directory. This file can be modified by any text editor. Later on, this file can be used by a Database administrator program to create tables, indexes, etc.

name: CURRENT
description: This function returns a field from current record (table) buffer.
text: CURRENT CURRENT This function returns a field from current record (table) buffer. Format CURRENT(field) Return Value Type Any type. Parameters - field - field from main input table Note This function can be used in total calculations of a report or a batch program. Examples See Also

name: Data Dictionary
text: Data Dictionary Data Dictionary Show This dialog shows list of the applications defined in the Data Dictionary. Compress Removes deleted entities (fields, records, procedures, programs, ...) from the DD. The previous versions of these entities also will be removed. Previous Data Dictionary files *.DDn will be renamed to *.BAn (name.DD1 -> name.BA1, name.DD2 -> name.BA2, ...), and new files *.DDn will be created. Copy DD to another DD Create new Data Dictionary and copy one application to the new dictionary. DD Manager Shows, copies and compares DD entites. DD Analyzer Analyzes contents of Data Dictionary files: DD2, DD3 and DD4. DD Converter Converts DD structure version_1 into version_2. The version_2 structure allows size of the DD4 file up to 1GB.

name: Data Selector
text: Data Selector Data Selector We can consider data selector as a complex object which enables multiple selection. In fact, the data selector is a dialog displaying two-columns list. The first column displays data, and the second one displays the text (data description). Using standard Windows actions (click, Ctrl/Click, Shift/Click) user selects some items and clicks Ok button. All the selected data will be moved into output array. Number of selected items will be stored in appropriate output variable. Later, in our program, we can use these variables in IF statement. To insert new data selector on the form, click button Dataselector in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Data selector. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. Definition dialog appears on the screen. Selector title Column 1 title Column 2 title Column 1 width in percents List type Variable to accept number of selected items Minimum number of selected items Output array

name: Dataselector properties
text: Dataselector properties Dataselector properties Object name When the new data saelector is created, generator automatically assigns the name Dataselector_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed. Background style Background style should be set to normal.
