Background color This attribute is applied only if the background style is normal. Double click brings up a color definition dialog. Foreground color Color of text. Double click brings up a color definition dialog. Font name Choose one from offered font names. Font size Specifies font size of the text. Bold Specify that font weight is 700. Normal weight is 400. Italic Specify whether text is italic. Underline Specify whether text is underlined. Sequence number If the data selector has sequence number 0, click is allowed at all times if the data selector is enabled. If the data selector has nonzero sequence, click will be accepted only if all objects with the smaller sequ...

name: Datasource dialog
text: Datasource dialog Datasource dialog In this dialog we need to specify all the tables and files which will be used in the program. In a program we can use simultaneously RDB tables and flat files. This option is very important if we want to import/export data from/to another applications or if we want to create temporary flat files for later processing. Available tables The list on the left side of the dialog shows all tables defined in the Data Dictionary. At the beginning of the list, RDB tables are displayed. Classical flat files, if exist, are displayed at the end of the list with text (file) appended to the file name. Table selection Click table name and press ">" button -or- Double-click table name. Selected table name appears in the first empty line of the right-side grid. By default, access mode, is set to READ. If we try to selected already chosen table, the message "Table already selected" appears on the screen. How to remove chosen table? - Click side heading of the grid. Whole line becomes selected. Click "<" button -or Press "Delete" key. - Right click side heading of the grid. In context menu select 'Remove table' or 'Remove rest'. How to change table position in th...

name: DATE
description: This function converts a value expression to a date.
examples: !In the next lines we must use DATE function.
text: DATE DATE This function converts a value expression to a date. Format DATE(val_exp) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - val_exp - date literal, alpha literal, numeric literal or alpha field. Note Proper date format (yyyymmdd) must be stored in the value expression. Examples $date_field = date("20051201") !! alpha literal !In next two lines we can use simple assignment because the !left and right operands are compatible. $date_field = '1-dec-2015' $date_field = '2015-12-01' !In the next lines we must use DATE function. $date_field = date(20051101) !! numeric literal $date_field = date('20151301') !! invalid month if $a8 is date $date_field = date($a8) end_if See Also Makedate, Value_date, Date Functions

name: Date Functions
text: Date Functions Date Functions ADD_DAYS adds number of days to a date. ADD_MONTHS adds number of months to a date. ADD_WEEKS adds number of weeks to a date. ADD_YEARS adds number of years to a date. BEGINM returns a date representing beginning of the month of date_exp. BEGINQ returns a date representing beginning of the quarter of date_exp. BEGINW returns a date representing beginning of the week of date_exp. BEGINY returns a date representing beginning of the year of date_exp. CONVERT_DMY_YMD converts a numeric or alphanumeric field to the date value. CONVERT_MDY_YMD converts a numeric or alphanumeric field to the date value. CONVERT_LIT_YMD converts a date literal to the date value. CONVERT_YMD_DMY converts a date value to an alphanumeric one. CONVERT_YMD_MDY converts a date value to an alphanumeric one. DATE converts a numeric or an alphanumeric field to a date. DATETIME returns a datetime field. DAY returns day of a date. DAY_OF_WEEK returns day of the week. DAY_OF_YEAR returns day of the year. DAYS_IN_MONTH returns number of days in a month. DIFF_DATE returns the difference between two date values. DIFF_DAYS returns number of days between two date values. DIFF_DT returns the...

name: DATETIME
description: This function returns a datetime field.
syntax: DATETIME(date_part, time_part) Stores the both parts of the field.
DATETIME(, time_part) Stores time part only. Date part remains unchanged.
DATETIME(date_part) Stores date part only. Time part remains unchanged.
text: DATETIME DATETIME This function returns a datetime field. Some databases use datetime data type to store date and time data. In Oracle, this is date data type, is MS SQL Server datetime, in DB2 timestamp, etc. Format 1 DATETIME(date_part, time_part) Stores the both parts of the field. Format 2 DATETIME(, time_part) Stores time part only. Date part remains unchanged. Format 3 DATETIME(date_part) Stores date part only. Time part remains unchanged. Return Value Type Datetime. Parameters - date_part - date field. - time_part - time field. Note In an assigment ------------------------------------------------------------ datetime_field = date_field ------------------------------------------------------------ program zeroes time part of output datetime_field. Examples $datetime = DATETIME(today,systime) Database_Field = DATETIME(,systime) Database_Field = DATETIME(today) See Also Date Functions

name: DAY
description: This function returns day of a date.
text: DAY DAY This function returns day of a date. Format DAY(date_exp) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date_exp - Note If date = zero_date or date = NULL, result is 0. Otherwise, result is a number in the range 1 to 31. Examples See Also Date Functions

name: DAY_OF_WEEK
description: This function returns day of the week.
text: DAY_OF_WEEK DAY_OF_WEEK This function returns day of the week. Format DAY_OF_WEEK(date_exp) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date_exp - Note Result is a positive number in the range 1 to 7. - 1 represents Monday - 2 represents Tuesday - 3 represents Wednesday - 4 represents Thursday - 5 represents Friday - 6 represents Saturday - 7 represents Sunday Examples See Also DAY_OF_YEAR, Date Functions

name: DAY_OF_YEAR
description: This function returns day of the year.
text: DAY_OF_YEAR DAY_OF_YEAR This function returns day of the year. Format DAY_OF_YEAR(date_exp) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date_exp - Note Result is a positive number in the range from 1 to 366. Examples See Also DAY_OF_WEEK, Date Functions

name: DAYS_IN_MONTH
description: This function returns number of days in a month.
text: DAYS_IN_MONTH DAYS_IN_MONTH This function returns number of days in a month. Format DAYS_IN_MONTH(date_exp) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date_exp - Note Result is a number in range 1 to 31. If date = zero_date or date = NULL, result is 0. Examples $D = DAYS_IN_MONTH("15-JAN-2001") !! result is 31 $D = DAYS_IN_MONTH("15-FEB-2001") !! result is 28 $D = DAYS_IN_MONTH("15-FEB-2000") !! result is 29 See Also

name: DB_DATASOURCES
description: This function stores list of ODBC datasource names into text file.
text: DB_DATASOURCES DB_DATASOURCES This function stores list of ODBC datasource names into text file. Format DB_DATASOURCES(output_file) Result Value Type Numeric. If the function fails result is -1, otherwise, result is 0. Parameters - output_file - output file name, alpha literal or alpha variable. The name can be: absolute path, like c:\temp\tbl.txt, or path with logical name, like work_dir:tbl.txt. The record consists of the two fields: datasource name and datasource description. Field delimiter is TAB (ASCII code 9). In DD, File definition dialog, select 'Variable with field delimiters' option for record format. Note To use this function, your program doesn't need to be connected to a datasource. Examples $status = db_datasources("c:\temp\ds.txt") $status = db_datasources(workdir:ds.txt") See Also DB_EXPORT, DB_TABLES

name: DB_EXPORT
description: This function copies data from an RDB table to output file.
text: DB_EXPORT DB_EXPORT This function copies data from an RDB table to output file. Format DB_EXPORT(table_name, output_file,additional_SQL_criteria,progressbar) Result Value Type Numeric, number of records written to the output file. If schema/table does not exist, result is -1. Result value of -2 indicates error on output file. Result value of -3 indicates other errors. Parameters - table_name -name of table to be copied, alpha literal or alpha variable. If you want to copy a table from your default schema, supply table name only. If you want to use table from another schema, enter the table name in the form SCHEMA.TABLE_NAME. - output_file - output file name, alpha literal or alpha variable. The name can be: absolute path, like c:\temp\t1.dbe, or path with logical name, like export_dir:t1.dbe. - additional_SQL_criteria - additional WHERE, ORDER BY or JOIN clause, optional parameter. Normally, run-time engine generates SELECT * FROM table_name and copies all the records (rows) to the output file. Using this parameter, you can copy just a few records. SQL parameters, like Where fld1 = :$locvar, are not allowed. - progressbar - identifier, name of the progress bar, optional paramete...

name: DB_IMPORT
description: This function copies data from an external file into RDB table.
text: DB_IMPORT DB_IMPORT This function copies data from an external file into RDB table. Format DB_IMPORT(table_name, input_file,delete_flag,commit_interval,skip,ignoredup,progressbar) Result Value Type Numeric, number of imported records. If schema/table does not exist, result is -1. Result value of -2 indicates error on input file. Result value of -3 indicates invalid input file format. Result value of -4 indicates duplicated key (index) value. Parameters - table_name -name of table to be imported, alpha literal or alpha variable. If you want to import a table into your default schema, supply table name only. If you want to use table from another schema, enter the table name in the form SCHEMA.TABLE_NAME. - input_file - input file name, previously created by db_export, alpha literal or alpha variable. The name can be: absolute path, like c:\temp\t1.dbe, or path with logical name, like import_dir:t1.dbe. - delete_flag - delete rows flag, integer, optional parameter. If you want to append new records to existing ones, enter zero (0) into this parameter. If you want to delete all the rows before inserting, specify a non-zero (<>0) value. Default is 1. - commit_interval - number of rec...

name: DB_TABLES
description: This function stores list of tables, views and synonyms into text file.
text: DB_TABLES DB_TABLES This function stores list of tables, views and synonyms into text file. Format DB_TABLES(schema, output_file) Result Value Type Numeric. If the function fails result is -1, otherwise, result is 0. Parameters - schema - name of the schema, alpha literal or alpha variable. If you want to export tables from all schemas, enter empty string or *. - output_file - output file name, alpha literal or alpha variable. The name can be: absolute path, like c:\temp\tbl.txt, or path with logical name, like work_dir:tbl.txt. The record consists of the three fields: table name, table type (TABLE, VIEW, ALIAS or SYNONYM) and schema. Field delimiter is TAB (ASCII code 9). In DD, File definition dialog, select 'Variable with field delimiters' option for record format. Note To use this function, your program has to be connected to a datasource. To enable datasource connection, specify at least one RDB table in Datasource dialog. Only tables from the current connection can be listed. If the program uses multiple connections, the first connection will be used for export. Username, used in connection string, must have privileges to read catalog information from the database. Example...

name: Show Files
text: Show Files Show Files File name This is file name filter. Wildcard characters are: * and ?. Examples: * A* *A *A* ???? AB?? List The list shows already defined files according to the filter. The line contains the following information: file name, file organization, record format, record size (in bytes) and number of keys. Show file Show detailed information about the selected record. Print Print file information or store it into text file. Cross ref Tab List on the left-side of the dialog shows all file definitions in the DD. Click the file name to see cross reference in the right-side list: the list of all the programs which use that file definition. Rebuild programs Rebuild all the programs from the right-side list. This option generates source files for each program in the list and one command file: IAFRBLD.BAT. At the end of this process a message appears on the screen: Launch IAFRBLD.BAT? If our answer is "Yes", the command file will be activated and as a result, executable programs will be generated.

name: Show Records
text: Show Records Show Records Record name This is record name filter. Wildcard characters are: * and ?. Examples: * A* *A *A* ???? AB?? List The list shows already defined records according to the filter. The line contains the following information: record name, number of fields, record size (in bytes), number of keys and date of last modification. Export description Export record definition of the selected record into a text file. Show record Show the structure of the selected record. Print Print record information or store it into text file. Cross ref Tab List on the left-side of the dialog shows all record definitions in the DD. Click the record name to see cross reference in the right-side list: the list of all the files which use that record definition.

name: DEFAULT_VALUE
description: This function returns default value used in CREATE TABLE statement.
text: DEFAULT_VALUE DEFAULT_VALUE This function returns default value used in CREATE TABLE statement. Format DEFAULT_VALUE(field) Return Value Type Alpha, numeric or date. Parameters - field - a table field. Notes If default value does not exist, result is initial value of the field (zero, space, zero_date, zero_time). Example ! Consider the following SQL statement: ! CREATE TABLE tbl ( ! col1 int not null primary key, ! col2 varchar(5) default 'abcd', ! col3 decimal(9,2) default 12.34, ! col4 decimal(9,2) default -1.00, ! col5 varchar(80) default null) $defa = DEFAULT_VALUE(tbl.col2) !!result='abcd' acc_1 = DEFAULT_VALUE(tbl.col3) !!result=12.34

name: Default
text: Default Default Default form Defines default data entry form for the first table from datasource dialog. Access mode for this table should be UPDATE. Advanced Defines default data entry form for any table from datasource dialog. Access mode for this table should be UPDATE. Define text labels: fast definition Define user profile Options

name: Define
text: Define Define global fields As we already said, a global field is a template field defining some attributes. Field name Field name is a standard identifier. Field type Field types are Alpha Stores ANSI alpha characters, 1 character occupies one byte Numeric Numbers Date Date values Time Time values Unicode Stores Unicode UTF-16 alpha characters, 1 character occupies two bytes Size For alpha and Unicode fields, this parameter denotes number of characters. For numeric fields, size specifies number of digits and decimals in the form nn[,dd] where is nn - total number of digits, maximum 18, dd - number of decimals. For example, size 5,2 means: five digits, with two decimals (integer part has three digits). Minimum value that can be stored into this field is -999,99 and maximum value of such field is 999,99. For date fields, size is 8 or 6 specifying 8-digit or 6-digit date. Date is stored in the form YYYYMMDD or YYMMDD if 6-digit, as an unpacked number without sign. For time, size is 6 or 4. Time is stored in the form HHMM[SS], as an unpacked number without sign, ie. size also denotes number of bytes. Additional attributes for alpha and Unicode fields Justification Values: No justif...

name: Define global procedures / functions
description: Define global procedures / functions Define global procedures / functions
text: Define global procedures / functions Define global procedures / functions This dialog enables us to view all global procedures/functions and to select a procedure/function for modification or deleting. Show all Show all global procedures and functions. Show procedures only Show all global procedures. Show functions only Show all global functions. Show versions Show all versions of procedures/functions. As we know, each Save command creates new version of a procedure/function. After DD compressing, all the previous versions will be purged. Modify Modify selected procedure/function. New procedure Create new global procedure. New function Create new global function. Delete Delete selected global procedure/function. After deletion, all the versions are removed from the DD. Just one particular previous version of procedure/function cannot be deleted. Search Searches global procedures/functions according to search criteria, look Search global procedures/functions.

name: Define
text: Define Define Datasource This command activates Datasource definition dialog. Local fields This command activates Local fields definition editor. Strings This command activates Strings definition dialog. Local procedure(s)/ function(s) This command activates Local procedure(s)/function(s) editor. Global procedure(s)/function(s) This command activates Global procedure(s)/function(s) editor. Function keys This command activates function keys editor. Protection This command activates Program protection dialog. Starting actions This command activates Starting actions editor. Ending actions This command activates Ending actions editor. Miscellaneous Subform calculations This command activates Subform calculations editor. Subform calculations execute after last active field on the screen is processed, or when program executes FINISH_WINDOW statement. Help screens Sequence Sequence definition for active objects. Default sequence (resequence) This command sets default sequence for active objects. All active objects (except those with sequence = 0), are sorted from left to the right and from up to down. The first object obtains the sequence number 1, next one number 2, etc. Toolbar A too...

name: DEFINE_LOCAL
description: This statement defines local field in a procedure or function.
text: DEFINE_LOCAL DEFINE_LOCAL This statement defines local field in a procedure or function. Format 1 for alphanumeric, date and time fields DEFINE_LOCAL field_name type size [num_el] [INIT_ALWAYS[=value]] Format 2 for numeric fields DEFINE_LOCAL field_name type num_dig[,num_dec] [data_type] [num_el] [INIT_ALWAYS[=value]] Format 3 for datetime and string fields DEFINE_LOCAL field_name type [num_el] [INIT_ALWAYS[=value]] Where - field_name - a name of the local field (the name has to start with two dollar signs, $$) - type - type of the local field (A - alphanumeric, N - numeric, D - date, T - time, DT - datetime, W - Unicode UTF-16, S - ANSI string, SW - UTF-16 string). - size - size of the local field. If the field is alphanumeric, field size is between 1 and 16000. If the field is date, size has to be 6 or 8, while time field can have size 4 or 6. - num_dig - total number of digits of numeric field. - num_dec - number of decimals of numeric field (must not be greater than the number of digits) - data_type - numeric field data type. Possible values: U (unpacked) is default, P - BCD packed, W - integer word (2 bytes), L - integer longword (4 bytes), Q - integer quadword (8 bytes), F...

name: Define lookup dialog
description: Define lookup dialog Define lookup dialog
text: Define lookup dialog Define lookup dialog Lookup dialog displays rows from a query or elements of arrays. By clicking 'Move' button, one value can be moved to the main program. The LOOKUP dialog can be used in a program, together with a DESTINATIONFIELD, via /LOOKUP qualifier. Objects in the dialog are: list control and 2 buttons (Move and Cancel). Our task is to define dialog title and list control columns. Title Dialog title, an alpha literal or a local field. At run-time, this text is displayed in dialog caption. Select command (query) SQL SELECT commands defines input record stream. The fields from select_list will be displayed in the list control, therefore, number of fields determines number of columns in the list control. There are two ways to enter SELECT command: manually and automatically. - Manually Click the 'SELECT command' edit box and enter your statement in the form SELECT fld1, fld2, ... fldN FROM mytable [WHERE ... ORDER BY ...] When finished, click 'Parse SELECT command' button. If the select list is correct, appropriate number of columns will be created in the list control which is placed on the right side of the dialog. By clicking columns header, you can cu...

name: DEFINE_RECAP_RECORD
description: This statement writes one record into the recapitulation file.
text: DEFINE_RECAP_RECORD DEFINE_RECAP_RECORD This statement writes one record into the recapitulation file. Format DEFINE_RECAP_RECORD RECAP_1 = field1 ... RECAP_N = fieldN END_RECAP_RECORD Where - field - value to be stored into the recapitulation record. Note The block of the statements DEFINE_RECAP_RECORD ... END_RECAP_RECORD fills and writes one record into the recapitulation file. All the field names in this record must start with RECAP_. When the last record from main input record stream is processed, the recapitulation file closes and reopens in read-only mode, becoming the main input file in recapitulation report. Usually, we place the block within a field in subtotal calculations, because the recapitulation displays summary data. But, it is not necessary. If we use this block inside a detail line field, one record from input record stream will generate one record in recapitulation file. If we place DEFINE_RECAP_RECORD ... END_RECAP_RECORD block inside a do_while loop, one recapitulation record will be written for each loop pass. Example DEFINE_RECAP_RECORD RECAP_1 = acc_1 RECAP_2 = fld1 + fld2 RECAP_3 = inptbl.field1 RECAP_4 = total(inptbl.ammount) END_RECAP_RECORD See also ...

name: Delete
text: Delete Delete This method deletes a file from the connected FTP server. Format Delete(filename) Parameters - filename - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the file, with or without path specification. Wildcards not allowed. Return Value Alpha variable. If connecting was successful, value is blank. Otherwise, the variable holds an error message. Applied To Ftp object. Note Examples $msg = Ftp.Delete('file1.txt') !!current directory if $msg <> SPACE Pause 'Error: ' | $msg end_if $msg = Ftp.Delete('/file1.txt') !!root directory $msg = Ftp.Delete('/subdir/file1.txt') !!absolute path $msg = Ftp.Delete('sub/file1.txt') !!relative path Using FTP

name: Deleteallitems
text: Deleteallitems Deleteallitems This method removes all items from an object. Format 1 Deleteallitems() Format 2 Deleteallitems (line) This format is applied to grid combobox only. Parameters - line - grid line, numeric value. The lines are counted from 1. Applied To Combobox, data selector, tree. Notes Format 2 is applied to grid combobox if every line contains comboboxes with different content. Examples ! Empty standard combo-box Combobox_1.Deleteallitems() ! Empty grid combobox in current line and fill it again Combobox_gr.Deleteallitems(current_line) Combobox_gr.InsertItemInLineCombo(current_line,'01','Item 1') Combobox_gr.InsertItemInLineCombo(current_line,'02','Item 2') Combobox_gr.InsertItemInLineCombo(current_line,'03','Item 3') See also Methods InsertItemEx, InsertItem InsertItemInLineCombo

name: DeleteDirectory
text: DeleteDirectory DeleteDirectory This method deletes directory from the connected FTP server. Format Deletedirectory(dirname) Parameters - dirname - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies directory name. Return Value Alpha variable. If connecting was successful, value is blank. Otherwise, the variable holds an error message. Applied To Ftp object. Note Examples $msg = Ftp.Deletedirectory('/dir1/dir2') if $msg <> SPACE Pause 'Error: ' | $msg end_if Using FTP

name: Delete
text: Delete Delete

name: Deleteitem
text: Deleteitem Deleteitem This method removes an item from a tree. Format Deleteitem(level, key) Parameters - level - tree level, integer variable or literal. Allowed values are 1 to 10. - key - item key, alpha variable. This variable represents the concatenation of the all keys stored in the tree, from level 1 up to the current level. Applied To Tree. Example Lets consider the following tree: 001 Item_1 002 Item_2 01 Item_2_1 02 Item_2_2 03 Item_2_3 003 Item_3 001, 002, 003, 01, 02, ... are the keys stored in the tree nodes. To remove Item_2_3 from the tree, we have to do Tree_1.DeleteItem(2,"00203") To delete Item_3, the call is Tree_1.DeleteItem(1,"003") See also Insertitem, Insertitembykey, Modifyitem, Methods

name: DELETE_RECORD
description: This statement removes an existing record from an indexed file or RDB table.
text: DELETE_RECORD DELETE_RECORD This statement removes an existing record from an indexed file or RDB table. Format DELETE_RECORD table_name Where - table_name - name of the indexed file or the name of RDB table from datasource dialog with UPDATE access. Notes The record to be removed must first be read and locked using a READ, SELECT or READ_ALL instruction. If RDB table does not have a primary key, this statement cannot be used. Example Select * from tbl1 Where kseg = :$seg1 If record tbl1 found Delete_record tbl1 End_if ! or Read tbl1 key=$seg1 If record tbl1 found Delete_record tbl1 End_if See also EXECSQL

name: DESTINATION
description: This statement defines data entry field. The destination field object gets the input focus allowing user to enter field via the keyboard.
examples: Destination = fld1/HELP: "This is a numeric field without sign"
text: DESTINATION DESTINATION This statement defines data entry field. The destination field object gets the input focus allowing user to enter field via the keyboard. Format DESTINATION = field [/ASSVAL: assval_expr] [/AUTO_RETURN] [/CHECKED: val_exp] [/UNCHECKED: val_exp] [/CALENDAR] [/COLORDIALOG] [/COND: cond_exp] [/DEFVAL: val_exp] [/DEFVALF: val_exp] [/FILEDIALOG: Title, Initial_directory, Filter (file_extensions)] [/FONTDIALOG] [/HELP: help_name or alpha_literal] [/HELPA: help_name or alpha_literal] [/LIST: list_name] [/LOOKUP: SQL_statement [HEADING alpha_literal] [TITLE title]] [/LOOKUPA: SQL_statement [HEADING alpha_literal] [TITLE title]] or [/LOOKUP: lookup_name] [/LOOKUP_ONLY: lookup_name] [/LOOKUPC: lookup1, lookup2, ..., lookupN cond1, cond2, ..., condN [/MISSING] [/NO_ECHO] [/NO_EXIT] [/PROGRAM: "program_name"] [/PROGRAM_ONLY: "program_name"] [/RIGHTFILL[:character]] Where - field - the field to be entered. What we enter via the keyboard, will be stored into this field. - cond_exp - conditional expression. If TRUE, the field will be entered manually, otherwise value from ASSVAL will be moved to the field and the next object in the sequence becomes the active object. - ...

name: Destination field
text: Destination field Destination field A destination field accepts a value from the keyboard. To insert new destination field on the form, click button Destination Field in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Destination Field. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. A dialog "Destinationfield definition" appears on the screen. Enter the variable name (field to be accepted) or click Browse to choose a variable from a list. Click OK. If the variable exists, object DESTINATIONFIELD will be created: default edit mask is displayed on the screen, and one source line is placed in code buffer. The line is Destination = variable_name See Destination statement. The destination field object has the two components: visual appearance (edit mask on the screen) and code buffer (contains statements defining the output variable, check conditions, calculations, etc.). The edit mask can be changed by context menu command "Change edit mask". To change the code, choose context menu command "Modify code". Maximal number of lines in the code buffer (for one object) is 1000, and only one DESTINATION statement must be encountered in the buffer. Destination = acc_1 check acc_1 > 1000 errmsg...

name: Destination field properties
text: Destination field properties Destination_field properties Object name When the new destination_field is created, generator automatically assigns the name Destinationfield_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Field name (information only, cannot be changed) Name of the destination field. In IAF, data entered via keyboard must be recorded somewhere: into a table field, a local field, a counter, an accumulator. This place we call destination field. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed or initialized. Enabled Determines whether the object is disabled when the subform is first displayed or initialized. When the object is disabled, mouse and keyboard input are ignored. Required Specifies whether the value is required in the field. This means the following: - an alphanumeric field cannot...

name: DETAIL_CALC
description: This statement specifies part of batch calculations which are executed for each input record of the main input file (table).
text: DETAIL_CALC DETAIL_CALC This statement specifies part of batch calculations which are executed for each input record of the main input file (table). Format DETAIL_CALC Example DETAIL_CALC ... TOTAL_CALC ... See also Total calculations

name: DIFF_DATE
description: This function returns the difference between two date values.
text: DIFF_DATE DIFF_DATE This function returns the difference between two date values. Format DIFF_DATE(date1, date2, flag) Result Value Type Numeric, 6 digits. Parameters - date1, date2 - date or datetime fields. - flag - alpha, difference flag. Value '0' denotes that the difference between two equal dates is 000000.Value '1' denotes that the difference between two equal dates is 000001. Note Result is a 6-digit positive number. The first two digits denotes number of years, the second two digits denotes number of months and the two last digits denotes number of days. Examples $n6 = DIFF_DATE("10-feb-2009","1-jan-2009","0") !! result is 000109 $n6 = DIFF_DATE("10-feb-2009","1-jan-2009","1") !! result is 000110 $n6 = DIFF_DATE("31-jan-2009","1-jan-2009","0") !! result is 000030 $n6 = DIFF_DATE("31-jan-2009","1-jan-2009","1") !! result is 000100 See Also

name: DIFF_DAYS
description: This function returns number of days between two date values.
text: DIFF_DAYS DIFF_DAYS This function returns number of days between two date values. Format DIFF_DAYS(date1, date2) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date1, date2 - date or datetime fields. Note Result is a positive number. Examples $n = DIFF_DAYS("1-mar-2009","1-mar-2009") !! result is 0 $n = DIFF_DAYS("2-mar-2009","1-mar-2009") !! result is 1 $n = DIFF_DAYS("1-mar-2009","2-mar-2009") !! result is 1 See Also

name: DIFF_DT
description: This function returns the difference between two datetime expressions in the form: number_of_days/time.
text: DIFF_DT DIFF_DT This function returns the difference between two datetime expressions in the form: number_of_days/time. Formats - DIFF_DT(date1, time1, date2, time2) - DIFF_DT(date_time1, date2, time2) - DIFF_DT(date1, time1, date_time2) - DIFF_DT(date_time1, date_time2) Return Value Type 12-digits alphanumeric, left filled with zeroes. Parameters - date1, date2 - standard date fields - time1, time2 - standard time fields - date_time1, date_time2 - datetime fields Note Result is a 12-digits number: the first 6 digits represent number of days, the second 6 digits represent time. Parameters date1 and time1 must be greater than date2, time2. Examples ! ! $a12 is defined as alpha, 12 characters ! $A12 = DIFF_DT ($D1, $T1, $D2, $T2) $daysdiff = num ($a12[1:6]) $timediff = time($a12[7:6]) ! ! A numeric field can be used as a result. ! In this case, 12-digit unpacked numeric must be used. ! $N12 = DIFF_DT ($D1, $T1, $D2, $T2) $daysdiff = $n12[1:6] $timediff = time($n12[7:6]) See Also

name: DIFF_MONTHS
description: This function returns number of months between two date values.
text: DIFF_MONTHS DIFF_MONTHS This function returns number of months between two date values. Format DIFF_MONTHS(date1, date2) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date1, date2 - date or datetime fields. Note Result is a positive number. Algorithm: Diff_months = abs (12*year(date1) + month(date1) - 12*year(date2) - month(date2)) Examples $n = DIFF_MONTHS('2009-02-01', '2009-01-01') !! result = 1 See Also

name: DIFF_TIME
description: This function returns time difference between two time values.
text: DIFF_TIME DIFF_TIME This function returns time difference between two time values. Format DIFF_TIME(time1, time2) Return Value Type Time, 6 digits, abs(time1 - time2). Parameters - time1, time2 - standard time fields. Examples $time = DIFF_TIME($time1, systime) $time = DIFF_TIME(time(datetime1),time(datetime2)) $time = DIFF_TIME("12:01","12:00") !! result=00:01:00 $time = DIFF_TIME("12:00","12:01") !! result=00:01:00 See Also Time Functions

name: DIFF_WEEKS
description: This function returns number of weeks between two date values.
text: DIFF_WEEKS DIFF_WEEKS This function returns number of weeks between two date values. Format DIFF_WEEKS(date1, date2) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date1 - - date2 - Note Result is a positive integer. Algorithm: Diff_weeks = diff_days / 7 Examples $D = DIFF_WEEKS("7-jan-2001","1-jan-2001") !! result is 0 $D = DIFF_WEEKS("8-jan-2001","1-jan-2001") !! result is 1 $D = DIFF_WEEKS("10-jan-2001","1-jan-2001") !! result is 1 $D = DIFF_WEEKS("1-jan-2001","10-jan-2001") !! result is 1 See Also

name: DIGIT_OF
description: This function returns one digit from a numeric value.
text: DIGIT_OF DIGIT_OF This function returns one digit from a numeric value. Format DIGIT_OF(numeric_exp, digit_index) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - numeric_exp - numeric field - digit_index - digit index Note Digit_index is defined as follows: Numeric value = 1 2 3 4, 5 6 Digit_index 3 2 1 0 -1 -2 The first digit after decimal point has digit_index -1. The first digit left from decimal point has digit_index 0. Examples $D = DIGIT_OF(1024,0) !! result is 4 $D = DIGIT_OF(1024,1) !! result is 2 See Also

name: Directives (pseudo-commands)
text: Directives (pseudo-commands) Directives (pseudo-commands) Directives are used to change default program behavior. All directives start with number sign (#). List of directives: #DONT_USE_LOCAL_FIELDS #DONT_USE_THIS_FUNCTION #USE_DECIMAL #END_DECIMAL #USE_TERMINATOR #END_TERMINATOR #IGNORE_TABLES #END_IGNORE_TABLES #ALTER_DECIMAL_POINT #RESTORE_DECIMAL_POINT #SELECT_BUFFER_SIZE nnnnn Explanations: #DONT_USE_LOCAL_FIELDS - local fields starting with one dollar sign ($XXX) cannot be referenced in a function/procedure. Example Procedure proc #dont_use_local_fields define_local $$x a 80 define_local $$a a 20 $$a = '123' $$x = 'abcd' if $myfld = 'a' !!<--- invalid reference ... end_if end_procedure #DONT_USE_THIS_FUNCTION - a function/procedure which contains this directive cannot be used in a program. Example Procedure proc #dont_use_this_function ... end_procedure #USE_DECIMAL - use decimal arithmetic in formulas. Rounding method is defined in Default/Options dialog. #END_DECIMAL - stop using decimal arithmetic in calculations. The generator normally uses standard C-compiler arithmetic in formula calculations. All decimal operands (unpacked, packed) are converted to double-floating ...

name: Dir
description: This method stores list of the files on the server into a text file on local machine.
examples: $msg = Ftp.Dir('*.exe') !!$msg contains the first file entry
text: Dir Dir This method stores list of the files on the server into a text file on local machine. Format Dir(pattern[, filename]) Parameters - pattern - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies file search pattern with wildcards. - filename - an alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. Specifies text file name. Logical names in file name allowed. If omitted, the first file entry will be stored into the output variable. Record has four fields: - file name - file type : "D" - directory, "F" - file - date modification in the form YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - file size in bytes Fields are separated by tabulator (TAB = CHR(9)). Return Value Alpha variable. If connecting was successful, value is blank. Otherwise, the variable holds an error message. Applied To Ftp object. Note Examples $msg = Ftp.Dir('*.*','c:\temp\list.txt') if $msg <> SPACE Pause 'Error: ' | $msg end_if $msg = Ftp.Dir('*.exe','c:\temp\list.txt') $msg = Ftp.Dir('*.exe','tmpdir:list.txt') !!logical name $msg = Ftp.Dir('*.exe') !!$msg contains the first file entry Using FTP

name: Disable Password Protection
description: Disable Password Protection Disable Password Protection
text: Disable Password Protection Disable Password Protection

name: Display
text: Display Display This method displays value expression. Format 1 Display (val_exp) Format 2 Display (line, column, val_exp) Parameters - val_exp - a value expression to be displayed - line - cell row, integer (format 2). The first line is 1. Top heading has line = -1. - column - cell column, integer (format 2). The first column is 1. Applied To Format 1 applies to an ordinary object. Format 2 applies to a grid cell. Notes 1/ The object must have 'Background style = Normal'. 2/ Numeric, date and time fields will be edited before displaying. 3/ Combo box, check box, radiobutton : val_exp denotes value, not a text. To change checkbox or radiobutton label, use Displaylabel method. 4/ Radio button: this method should be applied to the first radiobutton in the group (the button with the smallest sequence in the group of the buttons). A group of radio buttons is defined implicitly by the screen painter: all radio buttons within one frame belong to one group. This frame can be invisible at run-time, but must exist in the painter because it is the only way to determine group of radio buttons. 5/ FORM: the method changes program caption (title). 6/ Grid, graph with sequence = 0: to display...

name: Displaycomplex
text: Displaycomplex Displaycomplex This method displays a text. Some segments of the text could have various font attributes (size, weight, italic, underline). Format Displaycomplex (field, fontsize, fontweight, fontitalic, fontunderline, ...) Parameters - field - a field to be displayed, one segment of the text. - fontsize - font size, integer. - fontweight - font weight (intensity), an integer from 0 to 1000. - fontitalic - italic flag, integer. Value 0, normal; <> 0, italic. - fontunderline - underline flag, integer. Value 0, normal; <> 0, underline. Applied To Source field. Notes - The object must have 'Background style = Normal'. - Numeric, date and time fields will be edited before displaying. - All the fields will be all-trimmed before displaying. The distance between various segments is the average width of a character. - Font name is defined in Properties dialog. - The height of the object has to be appropriate to accept all the segments heights. - Cannot be used in a grid. Examples sourcefield_1.displaycomplex(f1,14,400,0,0,today,24,700,1,0) sourcefield_1.displaycomplex( \ f1,14,400,0,0, \ acc_1,16,400,1,0, \ today,14,700,0,0, \ systime,14,700,0,1) See also Display, Display...

name: Displayimage
text: Displayimage Displayimage This method displays an image. Format 1 Displayimage(image_path) Format 2 Displayimage(image_field, image_type) Parameters - image_path - alpha literal or alpha variable containing image path. The path can be absolute, relative, with or without logical names. File extension defines image type: ICO, BMP, GIF, JPG (JPEG). - image_field - table field containing image. Field type must be LONGVARBINARY. In MS SQL Server appropriate datatype is 'image', in Oracle 'long raw' or 'blob'. - image_type - specifies image type, integer. Possible values: 0 - Auto. Runtime engine will try to determine image type. 1 - ICO 2 - BMP 3 - GIF 4 - JPG (JPEG) Applied To Sourcefield, Bitmapbutton, Image, DestinationField. Notes Bitmapbutton can display ICO, BMP or GIF images only. To remove picture from a bitmapbutton, use an empty string as an argument. To remove picture from a source field, image or destination field, use Init() method. Examples sourcefield_1.Displayimage('c:\images\img1.bmp') sourcefield_1.Displayimage('myimages:img1.bmp') sourcefield_1.Displayimage(mytbl.pct,0) sourcefield_1.Init() !!remove picture ! BITMAPBUTTON images ! Embedded images (image name is a l...

name: Displaylabel
text: Displaylabel Displaylabel This method changes label text of a checkbox or radiobutton. Format Displaylabel(conc_exp) Parameter - conc_exp - concatenated expression or alpha literal. Applied To Checkbox, radiobutton. Example checkbox_1.Displaylabel("new label") See also Display, Displayimage, Methods

name: DISPLAY_WINDOW_FIELDS
description: This statement displays fields in the subform (fields have to be filled, usually with SELECT or READ statement).
text: DISPLAY_WINDOW_FIELDS DISPLAY_WINDOW_FIELDS This statement displays fields in the subform (fields have to be filled, usually with SELECT or READ statement). Format DISPLAY_WINDOW_FIELDS Note What the DISPLAY_WINDOW_FIELDS does is: - Sets the subform to display mode (stores 1 to IAF_DISPLAYMODE). - Moves from one active object to another, by sequence, executing all the statements. When comes to DESTINATION statement, value from the field buffer displays on the screen and continues. - After the last object in sequence is processed, stores 0 to IAF_DISPLAYMODE and sets the focus to the first active object following the object containing the DISPLAY_WINDOW_FIELDS statement. Example Destination = $seg1 Select * from tbl1 Where kseg = :$seg1 If record tbl1 found display_window_fields End_if See also Implicit record reading (Default reading)

name: IAF Help
description: IAF Help When multiple objects are selected, one of the objects is dominant. Dominant object is selected object with hatched border. Other objects have solid line border.
text: IAF Help When multiple objects are selected, one of the objects is dominant. Dominant object is selected object with hatched border. Other objects have solid line border.

name: DO_WHILE
description: This statement marks beginning of a cycle.
text: DO_WHILE DO_WHILE This statement marks beginning of a cycle. Format DO_WHILE cond_exp ... statement(s) ... END_DO Where - cond_exp - conditional expression. While its value is TRUE, the cycle is performing. When program comes to DO_WHILE, if conditional expression is FALSE, whole block of statements up to END_DO will be skipped. Note DO_WHILE cycle must end with END_DO statement. Example $tot = 0 counter_1 = 0 do_while counter_1 < 10 add 1 to counter_1 add $arr(counter_1) to $tot end_do See also Break Next

name: Download
syntax: Download (URL, localfile [, headers, username, password, override_flag [,progressbar_object]])
text: Download Download This function downloads a file (page) from the Internet using HTTP protocol (GET method) and saves it on the local machine. Format Download (URL, localfile [, headers, username, password, override_flag [,progressbar_object]]) Parameters - URL - universal resource locator, like http://google.com/, http://mysite/files/xx.zip, etc. - localfile - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Local file name. - headers - additional headers to be sent to the HTTP server, optional. Two or more header fields should be separated by CRLF (carriage-return/line feed pair). - username - an alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. If username is not an empty string, the function generates HTTP header field Authorization: Basic username:password - password - an alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. - override_flag - an alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. Override flag: value "O" denotes that local file will be overridden if exists. If the parameter has the value different from "O" and local file exists, error will be reported. Default value = "O". - progressbar_object - an alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. Name of a progress_bar used to display progress of file t...

name: Downloadfile
text: Downloadfile Downloadfile This method downloads file from the connected FTP server and saves it on the local machine. Format Downloadfile (remotefile, localfile [, override_flag [,progressbar_object]]) Parameters - remotefile - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the file on the server, with or without path specification. Wildcards not allowed. - localfile - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Local file name. - override_flag - an alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. Override flag: value "O" denotes that local file will be overridden if exists. If the parameter has the value different from "O" and local file exists, error will be reported. Default value = "O". - progressbar_object - an alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. Name of a progress_bar used to display progress of file transfer. Return Value Alpha variable. If connecting was successful, value is blank. Otherwise, the variable holds an error message. Applied To Ftp object. Note Transfer method is BINARY. Examples $msg = Ftp.Downloadfile('file1.exe','c:\temp\a.exe','O','progress_1') $msg = Ftp.Downloadfile('file1.exe','c:\temp\a.exe') !!override, no progress Using FTP See also Uploadfile Download

name: EDIT_CURRENCY
description: This function returns formatted currency representation of numeric variable. Currency information is stored in Regional settings.
text: EDIT_CURRENCY EDIT_CURRENCY This function returns formatted currency representation of numeric variable. Currency information is stored in Regional settings. Format EDIT_CURRENCY(num_exp) Return Value Type Alpha UTF-16. Parameters - num_exp - input numeric expression. Note Examples $XW = EDIT_CURRENCY(acc_1) See Also Edit mask

name: EDIT
description: This function returns formatted representation of alpha, numeric, date, datetime or time variable.
text: EDIT EDIT This function returns formatted representation of alpha, numeric, date, datetime or time variable. Format EDIT(val_exp, edit_mask) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - val_exp - input value expression - edit_mask - edit mask Note To convert an integer number in the range 1 to 3999 to Roman numerals, use edit mask "R". If the number is less than 1 or greater than 3999, one question mark will be moved to the destination buffer. Examples $X = EDIT(acc_1,"ZZZ.ZZ9,99-") $X = EDIT(counter_1,"R") !! conversion to Roman numerals $X = EDIT(today,"DD/MM/YY") $X = EDIT(today,"YYYY-MM-DD") $x = EDIT(today,'L') !! long date format $X = EDIT(systime,"HH:MM:SS") $X = EDIT($ALPHA,"XXX") !! move first 3 characters to $X $X = EDIT($ALPHA,"XXX-XXX") ! If $ALPHA = "ABCDEF", result will be "ABC-DEF" (7 characters) $X = EDIT($ALPHA,"XXX") ! gives the same result as $X = $ALPHA[1:3] See Also Edit mask

name: Edit mask
text: Edit mask Edit mask Edit mask is a string of characters that determines visual representation of a field on an external device (screen, printer, print file). For destination fields, edit string has control function as well: defines number of digits in a numeric field, allowed characters in an alpha field, etc. Edit mask for source fields Alpha field Editing characters in edit mask for an alpha field are: X, 9, N, U, L. Each editing character is just placeholder for one character from input field. All editing characters have the same meaning: character placeholder. An edit mask can contain and other characters. These characters we consider as formatting characters and they will be moved directly to the output buffer. Examples: Field value Edit mask Result ABCDE XXXXX ABCDE ABcde 99XXX ABcde ABCDE XXX ABC (output length = 3) ABCDE XX/XXX AB/CDE ABcde UUUUU ABcde (not uppercase, the U is just placeholder) Date field Editing characters for a date field are: D, M, Y, W, R and Z. All other characters in the mask are formatting characters. Edit pattern Meaning DD Display number of days, two digits (01, 02, ..., 31) ZD Display number of days, two digits, suppress leading zero (1, 2, ......

name: Editor rules
text: Editor rules Editor rules The maximal number of characters in a line is 128. Each line contains just the one instruction. There is no a termination character. The last non-blank character in the line denotes the end of the instruction. Examples: counter_1 = 10 acc_1 = 100,00 add 10 to acc_1 read_external $loc If an instruction is longer than 128 characters, the continuation sign \ (backslash) must be used: select fld1, fld2, fld3, fld4 from table1, table2 \ where table1.fld1 = table2.fld1 and fld2 < 1000 \ order by fld1, fld2 Also, there is a directive (#use_terminator) which enables the use of statement terminator, see Directives. Instructions are case-insensitive. The next two instructions are the same: acc_1 = 100,00 ACC_1 = 100,00 The only exceptions are alpha literals. The next two instructions gives different results: $x20 = "abcdef" $x20 = "ABCDEF" If the first non-blank character in the line is ! (exclamation sign), that line is considered as a comment line. read_external/name:mysymb $loc ! This is the comment line ! This is the next comment line $loc = $loc + 2 To write comment in the same line, we must use two exclamation signs: read_external/name:mysymb $loc !! readin...

name: Edit
text: Edit Edit Undo Undo last operation. Cut Copies selected objects to the Clipboard and remove them from the screen. Copy Copies selected objects to the Clipboard. Paste Creates new objects if they are previously copied to the Clipboard by Copy or Cut. Place the cursor at the position where we want to create new object and click. This position represents left upper corner of the new object. Choose Edit/Paste menu command or press Ctrl+V. See Insert at last point clicked option in View section. Find Finds a string in code, labels, procedures, etc. Replace Replace one string by another string. Adjust properties to current profile Changes video attributes of all selected objects according to the current profile. Make uppercase Converts all characters to upper case according to object's font. Make lowercase Converts all characters to lower case according to object's font. Make sentence case Converts the first character to the upper case, all other characters to lower case according to object's font. Insert character Inserts a special character from ANSI character set into text label. Select all Selects all objects. Reset screen layout Removes all the objects from the screen.

name: Ellipse
text: Ellipse Ellipse To insert new ellipse on the form, click button Ellipse in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Ellipse. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. Drag to the right and bottom and release the button. New ellipse is placed on the form. Create a circle Click button Ellipse in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Ellipse. Press Shift. Move the cursor to the desired position, click and drag to the bottom. Create ellipse with maximal width Click button Ellipse in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Ellipse. Press Ctrl. Move the cursor to the desired position, click and drag to the bottom. To change ellipse properties double click on the ellipse -or- select the ellipse, right click to display context menu and choose 'Properties'.

name: Ellipse properties
text: Ellipse properties Ellipse properties Object name When the new ellipse is created, generator automatically assigns the name Ellipse_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed. Background style Normal Background is filled with the current background color Transparent Background remains unchanged Background color This attribute is applied only if the background style is normal. Border style Transparent Border is not seen Solid Border is standard line Dots Border is dotted line Dashes Border is dashed line Border color Is applied only if border style is not transparent. Border width Is applied only if border style is not transparent. Possibly values are 0, 1, 2,... pixels. Z-Order Possible values: 0, -1, -2, -3, ... See Z-Order.

name: Enablelinedeleting
examples: Method ENABLELINEDELETING(0) disables deleting of lines displayed in the grid.
text: Enablelinedeleting Enablelinedeleting This method enables or disables line deleting. Format Enablelinedeleting(value) Parameters - value - a numeric variable, integer. Value=0 disables line deleting. Value <> 0 enables line deleting. Applied To Data-entry grid. Note By default, the lines can be deleted. This method is used usually in 'Table starting actions'. Example Table_1.enablelinedeleting(0) !! disable line deleting Table_1.enablelinedeleting(1) !! enable line deleting !! Lets look at the following piece of code !! Table starting actions select_all/load_key * from tbl1 where fld1 = :$Par1 end_select Table_1.Enablelinedeleting(0) !! Loop SELECT_ALL ... END_SELECT reads record set from TBL1, simultaneously building internal table of primary keys (qualifier /LOAD_KEY). Run-time engine uses this internal table to properly display grid lines. Method ENABLELINEDELETING(0) disables deleting of lines displayed in the grid. At the end of 'Table starting actions', run-time goes through the internal table and displays grid lines, one by one. After the last line is displayed, 'Tables ending actions' will be activated, if exist, and finally, the cursor will be positioned in the first de...

name: Enablelinedragging
text: Enablelinedragging Enablelinedragging This method enables or disables line dragging. Format Enablelinedragging (value) Parameters - value - a numeric variable or numeric literal. value = 0, line dragging will be disabled, value <> 0, line dragging will be enabled. Applied To Display-only grid. Notes Example Table_1.Enablelinedragging(0) !! Disables dragging Table_1.Enablelinedragging(1) !! Enables dragging See also Methods

name: Enablenewlines
examples: Method ENABLENEWLINES(0) disables entering new lines displayed into the grid.
The command Table_1.Enablenewlines(1), this is the default, positions the cursor to the first EMPTY line.
text: Enablenewlines Enablenewlines This method enables or disables new line entering into a grid. Format Enablenewlines(value) Parameters - value - a numeric variable or numeric literal. If value = 0, user can modify existing lines only. If value <> 0, user can enter new lines. Applied To Data entry table (grid). Note By default, new lines can be entered. This method is used usually in 'Table starting actions'. Example Table_1.Enablenewlines(0) !! Disable new lines Table_1.Enablenewlines(1) !! Enable new lines !! Lets look at the following piece of code !! Table starting actions select_all/load_key * from tbl1 where fld1 = :$Par1 end_select Table_1.Enablenewlines(0) !! Loop SELECT_ALL ... END_SELECT reads record set from TBL1, simultaneously building internal table of primary keys (qualifier /LOAD_KEY). Run-time engine uses this internal table to properly display grid lines. Method ENABLENEWLINES(0) disables entering new lines displayed into the grid. At the end of 'Table starting actions', run-time goes through the internal table and displays grid lines, one by one. After the last line is displayed, 'Tables ending actions' will be activated, if exist, and finally, the cursor will be...

name: END_
description: This statement ends block of instructions beginning with: IF, DO_WHILE, READ_ALL, DEFINE_RECAP_RECORD and PROCEDURE.
text: END_ END_ This statement ends block of instructions beginning with: IF, DO_WHILE, READ_ALL, DEFINE_RECAP_RECORD and PROCEDURE. Format - END_DO - END_IF - END_PROCEDURE - END_READ - END_RECAP_RECORD Examples

name: Ending actions
text: Ending actions Ending actions This piece of code executes when user clicks Close button, presses ESC key or when program executes STOP_RUN statement. After ending actions, program frees all allocated memory blocks, updates Symntt00.iaf file, rollbacks pending transaction (if exists), closes all opened files and disconnects from database. Maximum number of lines is 10. If this number is too little, PERFORM statement can be used to execute a procedure.

name: ENDM
description: This function returns a date representing end of the month of date_exp.
text: ENDM ENDM This function returns a date representing end of the month of date_exp. Format ENDM(date_exp) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date_exp - input date or datetime. Examples ENDM("12-mar-2001") !! result is 31-mar-2001 See Also BEGINM, ENDW, ENDY, Date Functions

name: ENDQ
description: This function returns a date representing end of the quarter of date_exp.
text: ENDQ ENDQ This function returns a date representing end of the quarter of date_exp. Format ENDQ(date_exp) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date_exp - input date or datetime. Examples ENDQ("12-mar-2001") !! result is 31-mar-2001 See Also BEGINM, BEGINQ, ENDM, ENDW, ENDY, Date functions

name: ENDW
description: This function returns a date representing the end of the week of date_exp. A week ends with Sunday, according to ISO 8601.
text: ENDW ENDW This function returns a date representing the end of the week of date_exp. A week ends with Sunday, according to ISO 8601. Format ENDW(date_exp) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date_exp - input date or datetime. Examples $endweek = ENDW("27-nov-2006") !! result is 3-dec-2006 $endweek = ENDW("28-nov-2006") !! result is 3-dec-2006 See Also BEGINW, ENDM, ENDY, Date Functions

name: ENDY
description: This function returns a date representing end of the year of date_exp.
text: ENDY ENDY This function returns a date representing end of the year of date_exp. Format ENDY(date_exp) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date_exp - input date or datetime. Examples $E = ENDY("12-mar-2001") !! result is 31-dec-2001 See Also BEGINY, ENDM, ENDW, Date Functions

name: ENVVAR
description: This function returns value of specified environment variable.
text: ENVVAR ENVVAR This function returns value of specified environment variable. Format ENVVAR(env_variable_name) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - env_variable_name - name of environment variable, alpha literal or alpha field. If the variable does not exist, result is blank field. Examples $S = ENVVAR('SystemRoot') $S = ENVVAR('USERPROFILE') $ENAME = 'USERPROFILE' $S = ENVVAR($ENAME) See Also String functions

name: ERRMSG
description: This statement displays error message if the CHECK expression value is FALSE, and returns
examples: ERRMSG = "Entered number must not be negative"
text: ERRMSG ERRMSG This statement displays error message if the CHECK expression value is FALSE, and returns control to the last object having input focus (destination field or push button). Format 1 ERRMSG = concatenated_exp Format 2 ERRMSG = function(...) Where - concatenated_exp - concatenation of variables, can be: - an alpha literal, - an alpha variable, - STRING function, - STRING_EDIT function, - any concatenation - function - an user function displaying message text Note ERRMSG is always used together with a check (CHECK, CHECK_RECORD) instruction. We can use CHECK and ERRMSG (or WARMSG) instructions in: - a destination field after DESTINATION instruction to validate entered value, - button click actions to validate all subform fields, - line_ending actions to validate all the fields in one line of the grid. Examples Destination = fld1 Check fld1 >= 0 ERRMSG = "Entered number must not be negative" !! -or- Destination = fld1 Check fld1 >= 0 ERRMSG = String(ids_1) -------------------------------------------------------- Destination = fld1 Check fld1 > 0 ERRMSG = "Value " | fld1 | " is invalid" !! -or- Destination = fld1 Check fld1 > 0 ERRMSG = String_edit(string(ids_2),fld1) wh...

name: ERRWARMSG
description: This statement is a combination of ERRMSG and WARMSG statements. If Registry symbol ERRWAR exists and has value 1 the statement becomes WARMSG, else ERRMSG is applied.
examples: ERRWARMSG = 'Entered number must not be negative' !!WARMSG
ERRWARMSG = 'Entered number must not be negative' !!ERRMSG
text: ERRWARMSG ERRWARMSG This statement is a combination of ERRMSG and WARMSG statements. If Registry symbol ERRWAR exists and has value 1 the statement becomes WARMSG, else ERRMSG is applied. Format 1 ERRWARMSG = concatenated_exp Format 2 ERRWARMSG = function(...) Examples Destination = fld1 Write_external /name: $errwar '1' Check fld1 >= 0 ERRWARMSG = 'Entered number must not be negative' !!WARMSG Destination = fld1 Write_external /name: $errwar '0' Check fld1 >= 0 ERRWARMSG = 'Entered number must not be negative' !!ERRMSG See also CHECK ERRMSG WARMSG

name: Example1
text: Example1 Example1 This piece of code downloads a file from FTP server. ******************************************************** $ftpmsg is an alpha variable, length = 255 bytes. ******************************************************** $ftpmsg = Ftp.Open('myserver','user1','pwd1') !! mode=active if $ftpmsg <> SPACE Pause "Ftp error: " | $ftpmsg else !! Root directory on FTP server is '/' if ftp.Getcurrentdirectory() <> '/' $ftpmsg = ftp.Setcurrentdirectory('/') !! set current dir = ROOT end_if !! download file from root directory $ftpmsg = Ftp.Downloadfile('remotefile.jpg','c:\temp\image1.jpg','O','progress_1') Ftp.Close() end_if Using FTP

name: Example 1
description: Example 1 Example 1 - Copy TBL1 (DSN1) to TBL2 (DSN2)
text: Example 1 Example 1 - Copy TBL1 (DSN1) to TBL2 (DSN2) Data Dictionary actions: Load TBL1 definitions into your Data Dictionary. Load TBL2 definitions into the same DD. Program generator actions: In 'Define datasource' dialog select TBL1 (for read), TBL2 (for write): TBL1, access=read TBL2 /DSN: DSN2;UID=user;PWD=pwd, acess=write Select "Connect to multiple datasources" check box. Program code could be reset tbl2 select_all * from tbl1 tbl2.fld21 = ... ... tbl2.fld2N = ... write TBL2 end_select commit Distributed queries are not allowed (for example, SELECT command from both datasources). See also Example 2

name: Example 2
description: Example 2 Example 2 - Copy TBL1 (DSN1) to TBL1 (DSN2)
text: Example 2 Example 2 - Copy TBL1 (DSN1) to TBL1 (DSN2) Data Dictionary actions: Load TBL1 (DSN1) definitions into your Data Dictionary. Table TBL1 from DSN2 cannot be loaded because table name in the DD must be unique. Case 1: TBL1 (DSN1) and TBL1(DSN2) have the identical structures. In 'Define datasource' dialog we select TBL1 and use alias for TBL1(DSN2) TBL1, access=read TBL1_DSN2 /USE: TBL1 /DSN: DSN2;UID=user;PWD=pwd, acess=write Select "Connect to multiple datasources" check box. Program code could be reset tbl1_dsn2 select_all * from tbl1 tbl1_dsn2 = corr(tbl1) write tbl1_dsn2 end_select commit Case 2: TBL1 (DSN1) and TBL1(DSN2) have similar structures (some columns are the same, some columns are different). In 'Define datasource' dialog we select TBL1 and use alias for TBL1(DSN2) TBL1, access=read TBL1_DSN2 /USE: TBL1 /DSN: DSN2;UID=user;PWD=pwd, acess=write Select "Connect to multiple datasources" check box. In program code we use only the same columns reset tbl1_dsn2 select_all col_1, col_2, ..., col_N from tbl1 tbl1_dsn2.col_1 = ... ... tbl1_dsn2.col_N = ... execsql insert into tbl1_dsn2 (col_1, col_2, ..., col_N) values ... end_select commit Case 3: TBL1 (DSN1) and TB...

name: Close
description: This method discards any changes and stops Excel. After this action, another document can be opened, etc.
text: Close Close This method discards any changes and stops Excel. After this action, another document can be opened, etc. Format Close() Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Excel object. Note This method is usually used after the reading input document. Examples $stat = Excel.Close() Using Excel See also Open

name: Example 1
text: Example 1 Using Excel - Example 1 This piece of code transfers data from file "c:\dat\Test.xls" to a database table. ****************************** $estat is numeric variable. ****************************** $estat = excel.Open("c:\dat\Test.xls","hide") if $estat <> 0 Pause "Excel error: " | $estat else counter_1 = excel.Getnumberrows() counter_2 = excel.Getnumbercols() if counter_1 > 0 init counter_3 do_while counter_3 < counter_1 add 1 to counter_3 fld1 = excel.Getcell(counter_3,1,1250) !!alpha field, codepage=1250 fld2 = excel.Getcell(counter_3,2) fld3 = excel.Getcell(counter_3,3) write file1 end_do end_if $estat = excel.Close() end_if Using Excel

name: Example 2
text: Example 2 Using Excel - Example 2 This piece of code transfers data from database to the file "c:\dat\Test.xls". ****************************** $estat is numeric variable. ****************************** $estat = excel.Open() !!create new, empty document if $estat <> 0 Pause "Excel error: " | $estat else init counter_1 read_all file1 add 1 to counter_1 $estat = excel.Setcell(counter_1,1,fld1,1250) !!alpha field, codepage=1250 $estat = excel.Setcell(counter_1,2,fld2) $estat = excel.Setcell(counter_1,3,fld3) $estat = excel.Setcell(counter_1,4,add_days(fld4,5)) end_read $estat = excel.Run("save_exit", "c:\dat\Test.xls") end_if Using Excel

name: Example 3
text: Example 3 Using Excel - Example 3 This piece of code opens the existing file "c:\dat\Template.xls", fills some cells and saves the document under new name "c:\dat\Out.xls". ****************************** $estat is numeric variable. ****************************** $estat = excel.Open("c:\dat\Template.xls") if $estat <> 0 Pause "Excel error: " | $estat else init counter_1 read_all file1 add 1 to counter_1 $estat = excel.Setcell(counter_1,1,fld1,1250) !!alpha field, codepage=1250 $estat = excel.Setcell(counter_1,2,fld2) $estat = excel.Setcell(counter_1,3,fld3) end_read $estat = excel.Run("save_exit", "c:\dat\Out.xls") end_if Using Excel

name: Getcell
text: Getcell Getcell This method reads a value from MS Excel cell. Format 1 Getcell(range[,codepage]) Format 2 Getcell(line,column[,codepage]) Parameters - range - cell in the form COLUMNline, alpha variable. Examples: A1, A2, B1, X1, AA1, AA2, AA100, ... - line - row, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1, etc. - column - column, numeric value, integer. The first column is 1. Instead of column number, an alpha literal containing column header can be used: 'A', 'B', ..., 'AA', 'AB', ... - codepage - if the cell contains an alpha value, this parameter specifies variable's code page. The parameter is very important because MS Office tools (Excel, Word, ...) use UNICODE to represent text data, while text data in programs use, in most cases, single-byte representation. Default is 1252 (Western). Return Value Value of the cell. Applied To Excel object. Note This method can be used in assignment only, on the right-hand side of the equation. If the cell has NULL value, output variable will be initialized. If line or column are invalid, output variable will be initialized, too. To avoid this situation, use Getnumberrows() and Getnumbercols() methods to get proper intervals. If you do n...

name: Getsheetname
text: Getsheetname Getsheetname This method returns the name of the current sheet. Format Getsheetname() Return Value Sheet name, alpha variable. Applied To Excel object. Example $a100 = Excel.Getsheetname() Using Excel See also Setsheet

name: Introduction
text: Introduction Using Excel - Introduction Programs can transfer data to and from a MS Excel document. For this purpose we use object named Excel and its methods. There is only one Excel object in a program. This means that two documents cannot be opened at the same time. All methods return a value. A program uses COM (OLE) interface to manipulate objects belonging to MS Excel. MS Excel must be installed on local PC. Object name: Excel Methods: Close - discards any changes and stops Excel ExportXML - exports Excel workbook to XML file Getcell - reads a value from MS Excel cell Getnumbercols - returns number of columns in the current sheet Getnumberrows - returns number of rows in the current sheet Getnumbersheets - returns number of sheets in the current workbook Getsheetname - returns name of the current sheet Joincells - combines a range of cells into one large cell. Loadexternal - loads external datasource. Open - starts MS Excel and opens (creates) document Pagesetup - changes page layout parameters Run - saves the document, breaks connection, and gives control to the user Runmacro - runs a procedure or macro Setalignment - aligns text in a cell. Setbackcolor - sets the backgro...

name: Joincells
text: Joincells Joincells This method combines a range of cells into one large cell (Excel). Format 1 Joincells (range) Format 2 Joincells (line1, col1, line2, col2) Parameters - range - range of cells in the form STARTING_CELL:ENDING_CELL, alpha variable. - line1, col1 - starting cell, numeric. - line2, col2 - ending cell, numeric. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Invalid range Applied To Excel. Note To display a text into large cell, use STARTING_CELL as a parameter. Example $status = Excel.Joincells('A1:F1') $lin = 1 $status = Excel.Joincells(compress_trim('A',$lin,':','F',$lin)) $status = Excel.Setcell(1,'A','some text') $status = Excel.Joincells(1,1,1,6) Using Excel See also Joincells (grid)

name: Open
description: This method starts MS Excel and opens (creates) document. Must be invoked before any other Excel method.
examples: Opens new, empty document, Excel is visible
Opens new, empty document, Excel is hidden
text: Open Open This method starts MS Excel and opens (creates) document. Must be invoked before any other Excel method. Format Open([filename [,hide][,password]]) Parameters - filename - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the existing Excel document. If we create new, empty document, filename is empty string. Logical names in filename are allowed. - hide - second parameter, optional, is alpha literal containing text "hide". In that case, Excel is active in background and cannot be seen on the screen. If a lot of data need to be transfered between program and Excel, this parameter is required because it accelerates the transfer. - password - alpha literal or alpha field, optional. If Excel document is password protected, the password should be supplied to automatically open the document. If the password is not supplied and the document is password protected, then the dialog appears at run-time. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Cannot activate Excel -2 Document already opened -3 File not found -4 Logical name not found -5 Invalid password Applied To Excel object. Note To release current Excel document use Close or Run method. Examples V...

name: Run
description: This method saves the document, breaks connection between program and MS Excel, and gives control to the user. After this, another document can be opened, etc.
text: Run Run This method saves the document, breaks connection between program and MS Excel, and gives control to the user. After this, another document can be opened, etc. Format Run([command,filename]) Parameters - command - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Possible values are: "print" - prints current sheet on default printer, "print Xn:Ym" prints range of cells, from cell Xn to the cell Ym, "print X:Y" prints range of columns, from column X to column Y, "save" - invokes 'Save as', "save_exit" - invokes 'Save as' and stops Excel, "save_overwrite" - invokes 'Save', "save_overwrite_exit" - invokes 'Save' and stops Excel. For "save_overwrite" or "save_overwrite_exit", an existing file must be opened by Open method. - filename - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the output file which will be used in 'Save as'. Logical names in filename allowed. If command is "save_overwrite" or "save_overwrite_exit", filename is an empty string. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Invalid command > 0 File errors Applied To Excel object. Note This method is usually used to save results. If we want that Excel stays active after Ru...

name: Setalignment
text: Setalignment Setalignment This method sets cell alignment (Excel). Format 1 Setalignment (range, alignment) Format 2 Setalignment (line, column, alignment) Parameters - range - range of cells in the form STARTING_CELL:ENDING_CELL, alpha variable. ENDING_CELL can be omitted. - alignment - alpha variable, alignment in the form HORIZONTAL = {CENTER | LEFT | RIGHT | JUSTIFY} or VERTICAL = {CENTER | TOP | BOTTOM | JUSTIFY} or ORIENTATION = number_of_degrees {-90 to 90} HORIZONTAL, VERTICAL and ORIENTATION can be abbreviated. - line - row, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1, etc. - column - column, numeric value, integer. The first column is 1. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Invalid range -3 Invalid alignment Applied To Excel object. Examples $status = Excel.Setalignment('A1','H=center') $status = Excel.Setalignment('A1','HORIZ=right') $status = Excel.Setalignment('A1','V=center') $status = Excel.Setalignment('A1','VERT=center') $status = Excel.Setalignment(1,1,'VERT=center') $status = Excel.Setalignment(1,1,'ORIENT=90') $status = Excel.Setalignment(1,1,'ORIENT=45') $status = Excel.Setalignment(1,1,'ORIENT=-45') $status ...

name: Setbackcolor
text: Setbackcolor Setbackcolor This method sets the background color of a range of Excel cells. Format 1 Setbackcolor (range, color) Format 2 Setbackcolor (line, column, color) Parameters - range - range of cells in the form STARTING_CELL:ENDING_CELL, alpha variable. ENDING_CELL can be omitted. - color - a numeric variable with valid RGB color value or one of the predefined colors or RGB function. - line - row, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1, etc. - column - column, numeric value, integer. The first column is 1. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Invalid range -3 Error Applied To Excel object. Examples $status = Excel.Setbackcolor('a1','black') $status = Excel.Setbackcolor('a1:f1','red') $lin = 1 $status = Excel.Setbackcolor(compress_trim('A',$lin,':','F',$lin),'red') $status = Excel.setbackcolor('a1:a1',RGB(255,0,0)) $status = Excel.Setbackcolor('a1:a10',255) $status = Excel.Setbackcolor(1,5,255) $lin = 1 $col = 5 $status = Excel.Setbackcolor($lin,$col,'red') Using Excel See also Setbackcolor

name: Setbordercolor
text: Setbordercolor Setbordercolor This method sets the border color of a range of Excel cells. Format 1 Setbordercolor (range, color) Format 2 Setbordercolor (line, column, color) Parameters - range - range of cells in the form STARTING_CELL:ENDING_CELL, alpha variable. ENDING_CELL can be omitted. - color - a numeric variable with valid RGB color value or one of the predefined colors or RGB function. - line - row, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1, etc. - column - column, numeric value, integer. The first column is 1. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Invalid range -3 Error Applied To Excel object. Examples $status = Excel.Setbordercolor('a1','black') $status = Excel.Setbordercolor('a1:f1','red') $lin = 1 $status = Excel.Setbordercolor(compress_trim('A',$lin,':','F',$lin),'red') $status = Excel.Setbordercolor('a1:a1',RGB(255,0,0)) $status = Excel.Setbordercolor('a1:a10',255) $status = Excel.Setbordercolor(1,5,255) $lin = 1 $col = 5 $status = Excel.Setbordercolor($lin,$col,'red') Using Excel See also Setbordercolor

name: Setcell
text: Setcell Setcell This method stores value to an Excel cell. Format 1 Setcell(range, variable [,codepage]) Format 2 Setcell(line, column, variable [,codepage]) Parameters - range - cell in the form COLUMNline, alpha variable. Examples: A1, A2, B1, X1, AA1, AA2, AA100, ... - line - row, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1. - column - column, numeric value, integer. The first column is 1. Instead of column number, an alpha literal containing column header can be used: 'A', 'B', ..., 'AA', 'AB', ... - variable - value which will be written to the cell. If the value starts by '=' (equal sign) a formula will be stored to the cell. - codepage - if variable is an alpha one, this parameter specifies variable's code page. The parameter is very important because MS Office tools (Excel, Word, ...) use UNICODE to represent text data, while text data in programs use, in most cases, single-byte representation. Default is 1252 (Western). Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Excel object. Note If a formula has more than one argument, arguments are separated by comma: Excel.Setcell(1,1,'=SUMIF(E1:E3,"A",F1:F3)') Excel.Setcell(1,1,'=...

name: Setcolumnwidth
text: Setcolumnwidth Setcolumnwidth This method sets column width (Excel). Format Setcolumnwidth (column, width) Parameters - column - column name, alpha variable. Examples: A, B, AA, AB, BB, ... - width - column width in characters, integer. Standard values: 0 (zero) to 255. If width < 0, autofit operation will be applied. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Invalid column Applied To Excel object. Examples $status = Excel.Setcolumnwidth('A',20) $status = Excel.Setcolumnwidth('A1',20) !! 1 will be ignored $status = Excel.Setcolumnwidth('A',-1) !! autofit Using Excel See also Setcolumnwidth

name: Setsheet
text: Setsheet Setsheet This method changes current sheet. Format 1 Setsheet (numsheet) Format 2 Setsheet (sheet_name) Parameters - numsheet - sheet number, numeric value. - sheet_name - sheet name, alpha literal or variable. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Invalid sheet number Applied To Excel object. Examples $stat = Excel.Setsheet(2) $stat = Excel.Setsheet(counter_1) $stat = Excel.Setsheet("Sheet3") Using Excel

name: Settextcolor
text: Settextcolor Settextcolor This method sets the text color of a range of Excel cells. Format 1 Settextcolor (range, color) Format 2 Settextcolor (line, column, color) Parameters - range - range of cells in the form STARTING_CELL:ENDING_CELL, alpha variable. ENDING_CELL can be omitted. - color - a numeric variable with valid RGB color value or one of the predefined colors or RGB function. - line - row, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1, etc. - column - column, numeric value, integer. The first column is 1. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Invalid range -3 Error Applied To Excel object. Examples $status = Excel.Settextcolor('a1','black') $status = Excel.Settextcolor('a1:f1','red') $lin = 1 $status = Excel.Settextcolor(compress_trim('A',$lin,':','F',$lin),'red') $status = Excel.settextcolor('a1:a1',RGB(255,0,0)) $status = Excel.Settextcolor('a1:a10',255) Using Excel See also Settextcolor

name: EXECSQL
