description: This statement executes a SQL statement.
examples: EXECSQL update tbl1 set fld1=100, fld2='ABC' where keyfld=1
EXECSQL delete from tbl1 where keyfld=1
EXECSQL delete from tbl1 where keyfld= :$key
text: EXECSQL EXECSQL This statement executes a SQL statement. Format 1 EXECSQL SQL_statement Format 2 EXECSQL $local_variable Where - SQL_statement - a SQL statement, text. - $local_variable - an alpha local variable containing SQL statement. Notes A SELECT (SELECT_ALL) statement cannot be executed by EXECSQL. Data manipulation statements (CREATE TABLE, CREATE INDEX, ...) do not support parameters, i.e. we cannot write CREATE TABLE :$tblname (col1 int, col2 int). Examples EXECSQL update tbl1 set fld1=100, fld2='ABC' where keyfld=1 EXECSQL delete from tbl1 where keyfld=1 EXECSQL delete from tbl1 where keyfld= :$key $aloc = "Create table tbl1 (col1 int, col2 char(10))" EXECSQL $aloc

name: EXECUTE
description: This statement executes operating system commands or opens a file or an URL.
text: EXECUTE EXECUTE This statement executes operating system commands or opens a file or an URL. Format EXECUTE [/FILE] command Where - command - literal or alphanumeric field containing the operating system command. If /FILE qualifier is used, this parameter contains file name. Qualifier /FILE opens specified file. If the filename does not contain an absolute path, full path of executable program will be retrieved and used as file folder. Examples EXECUTE "copy c:\tmp\a.seq c:\tmp\b.seq" !! command EXECUTE/FILE "c:\temp\a.doc" !! absolute path EXECUTE/FILE "c:\temp\a.xls" !! absolute path EXECUTE/FILE "a.xls" !! relative path EXECUTE/FILE ".\a.xls" !! relative path EXECUTE/FILE "..\dir1\a.xls" !! relative path EXECUTE/FILE "http://www.mysite.com" !! URL

name: EXIT
description: This statement exits immediately a procedure or function.
text: EXIT EXIT This statement exits immediately a procedure or function. Format EXIT Example Procedure PROC if acc_1 = 0 EXIT end_if ... End_procedure

examples: To expand Item_2 we have to do
To expand Item_2_3, the call is
To expand all items, the call is
text: Expand Expand This method expands a tree node. Format Expand (level, key) Parameters - level - tree level, integer variable or literal. Allowed values are 0 to 10. To expand all tree nodes, set level to 0 (zero). To expand one node only, the level must be in the range 1 to 10. - key - item key, alpha variable. This variable represents the concatenation of the all keys stored in the tree, from level 1 up to the current level. This parameter is ignored if level = 0. Applied To Tree. Note This method is usually used in Post tree-filling actions to expand desired node. Example Lets consider the following tree: 001 Item_1 002 Item_2 01 Item_2_1 02 Item_2_2 03 Item_2_3 1 Item_2_3_1 2 Item_2_3_2 003 Item_3 001, 002, 003, 01, 02, ... are the keys stored in the tree nodes. To expand Item_2 we have to do Tree_1.Expand(1,"002") To expand Item_2_3, the call is Tree_1.Expand(2,"00203") To expand all items, the call is Tree_1.Expand(0,"") See also Insertitem, Insertitembykey, Modifyitem, Methods

description: Export Data From File Export Data From File
text: Export Data From File Export Data From File

name: Export Data From RDB Table

text: Export Data From RDB Table Export Data From RDB Table

name: ExportPDF
text: ExportPDF ExportPDF This method exports a Winword document to a PDF file. Format ExportPDF (pdf_file_name) Parameters - pdf_file_name - output PDF file name, absolute path, alphanumeric. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Word object. Note The document must be opened. Examples ... $stat = Word.Open('c:\temp\File.doc') $stat = Word.ExportPDF('c:\temp\File.pdf') $stat = Word.Close() ... Using Word See also

name: Export Programs
text: Export Programs Export Programs

name: ExportXML
text: ExportXML ExportXML This method exports Excel workbook to XML file. Format ExportXML(xmlmap_name, output_xmlfile) Parameters - xmlmap_name - name of XmlMap, an alpha variable or alpha literal. - output_xmlfile - name of output XML file, alpha literal or alpha variable. Logical names allowed. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Xmlmap not found -2 Logical name not found -3 Option not implemented Applied To Excel object. Example $estat = excel.Open("c:\dat\Test.xls","hide") if $estat = 0 $estat = excel.Exportxml("map1","xmldir:export.xml") Pause "Exportxml status = " | $estat $estat = excel.Close() end_if Using Excel

name: Fields Menu
text: Fields Menu Fields Menu Define Defines new global fields. Show Shows all the global fields defined in the Data Dictionary. Modify Modifies some attributes of global field. Delete Deletes unused global fields.

name: FILE_STATUS
text: FILE_STATUS FILE_STATUS This function returns file status. Format FILE_STATUS(file_name) Return Value Type Alphanumeric. Possible values: Result description Result output format Logical name does not exist -1 Other errors numeric_error;textual description, for example 2;The system cannot find the file specified. Successful 0;file_size;date_created;date_modified, for example 0;14560;20090120101533;20090504125123; Parameters - file_name - name of file to be analyzed, logical names allowed. Note How to check folder existence? Add a file name to folder string, invoke FILE_STATUS and check return value. If the first character of return value is '3', folder does not exist. $newstring = compress_trim($folder_name,"\x.xxx") $fs = file_status($newstring) if $fs[1:1] = "3" Pause "Folder does not exist" end_if Examples $FS = FILE_STATUS("c:\temp\a.tmp") $FS = FILE_STATUS("dat_dir:a.tmp") where $FS is defined as alpha, 128 characters long See Also File_io Split

name: File definition
text: File definition File definition Generally, a file is collection of information. In Iaf, a file is collection of records containing data. File organization - Sequential (flat) files contain set of records. - Indexed (index-sequential) files besides data records contain indexes (keys) which enable fast access to the records. In this chapter we will no consider this type of file organization. Record format - FIXED. Each record in the file has the same length. The fields in such record can be of any type (alpha, integer, floating, packed numeric, etc.). The access to this file can be sequential or random (by record number). - VARIABLE. Each record is ended by record terminator. In our case the terminator is two-byte one: carriage return/line feed. Carriage return has decimal value 13, line feed has value 10. Because of this, fields in such records must be alpha ones: no integers, no BCD packed, no floating numbers. Such files can be viewed by an ordinary text editor. - VARIABLE WITH FIELD DELIMITERS. Each record is ended by record terminator (<CR><LF>) and each field in the record is ended by field delimiter. Delimiter can be any printable character or <TAB> (TABulator has decimal v...

name: FILE_IO
text: FILE_IO FILE_IO This function handles files. Format - FILE_IO("dir", src_file, dest_file[, modifier]) Stores list of files or directiories defined by src_file into the dest_file. Wildcard characters (* and ?) in src_file allowed, for example: *.dat. Allowed modifier values are: "D": list directories. "T": show modification date/time. Field separator is TAB (ASCII code 9). "TC": show creation date/time. Field separator is TAB (ASCII code 9). "S": time component contains seconds and milliseconds. Otherwise, only hours and minutes will be returned. If the function succeeds, the result is zero. If the function fails, result contains error status. - FILE_IO("copy", src_file, dest_file[, modifier]) Copies an existing src_file into a new dest_file. Allowed modifier value is "O": overwrite existing output file. If the function succeeds, the result is zero. If the function fails, result contains error status. - FILE_IO("append", src_file, dest_file) Appends src_file to dest_file. If dest_file does not exist, it will be created. Multiple files in src_file (wildcards) not allowed. If the function succeeds, the result is zero. If the function fails, result contains error status. - FILE_IO("...

name: File Manipulation
text: File Manipulation File Manipulation The list shows already defined files with their characteristics. File name is maximum 30 characters long, the first character must be a letter. Allowed characters: letters A - Z, digits 0 - 9, underline (_) and a dollar sign ($). File name in one Data Dictionary must be unique. Modify To modify an existing file definition, select the file in the list and click Modify -or- Double click the file name in the list. The File definition dialog appears on the screen. New To create new file definition, click button New, enter file name and go through the file definition dialog. Copy To create new file definition based on existing file definition, select the file in the list and click Copy. This option is useful if we have similar file definitions. Delete To delete a file definition from the Data Dictionary, select the file in the list and click Delete. Delete unused files To delete all unused file definitions (the files not used by any program), click Delete unused files. The list of unused files appears on the screen. We can delete all these files or just some of them. Unlock If during file definition (modification) phase Data Dictionary Maintenance ...

name: Introduction
text: Introduction Introduction To manipulate with operating system files, we use an object named FILE. This object enables us to read and write ordinary text files. Text files contain plain, unformatted text. Each record of a text file is ended by two-bytes terminator: carriage return/line feed. All methods return an integer value. There is only one FILE object in a program. Consequently, two files cannot be opened at the same time. Note If we want to define record structure, we must do it using 'Data Dictionary' utility. Such structured files must be declared in Datasource dialog and processed using statements like WRITE, READ_ALL, etc. Object name: File Methods: Open - opens/creates a text file Close - closes the file Rewind - positions the file pointer Reset - resets file (removes all the records) Read - reads next record from the file Write - writes one record to the file Example 1 Example 2

name: File
text: File File Menu Save to recovery file Saves current work to journal file program_name.JOU. If generator abnormally ends, next time when we try to modify this program, the message "Recovery file exists. Use it?" appears. If we click YES, our saved work will be restored. See also Default/Options dialog for autosave interval definition. Run Activate File dialog to choose any executable program. Exit & generate Writes current work to the Data Dictionary and generates executable program. Exit & save Writes current work to the Data Dictionary. Quit Abandons all changes and closes generator.

name: Find Program
text: Find Program Find Program Find a program in Data Dictionary. This option is especially useful if a Data Dictionary contains many applications. Program name Enter the program name with wildcard characters. Wildcard characters are * (replaces many characters), and ? (replaces exactly one character). Examples: * prog* *prog *prog* prog??? ??? Program type Select program type (interactive, report, batch). Note To activate appropriate generator, double click the list.

name: FINISH_WINDOW
description: This statement finishes data entry process and moves program control to subform calculations. In reports and batch programs, this statement finishes program title and starts program cycle.
text: FINISH_WINDOW FINISH_WINDOW This statement finishes data entry process and moves program control to subform calculations. In reports and batch programs, this statement finishes program title and starts program cycle.

name: General
text: General Form painter - General information Form painter enables you to design (paint) forms or reports. To create a form (or report) you place objects to the form. Objects can be modified, moved around the form, resized, removed, etc. Active objects (destination field, combo box, check box, radio button, button, source field) have associated code buffer. Inserting new object to the form - Click appropriate button on the Toolbox. - Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. -or- - Choose menu command Objects/some_object. - Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. -or- - Choose menu command Objects/Field explorer. - Click Sourcefield or Destinationfield on the Toolbox. - Drag a field to the form. Using this method we can create source and destination fields only. Selecting object(s) To select one object, just click it. -or- Click and drag the mouse around the object. Selecting multiple objects: - Select one object, - Ctrl/Click to select next object, and so on. -or- Click and drag the mouse around the objects. To select an object that is behind another object (denoted by blue rectangle), hold down Alt then click inside blue rectangle. If you want to remove on...

name: Frame properties
text: Frame properties Frame properties Object name When the new frame is created, generator automatically assigns the name Frame_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Caption The text which appears on the upper edge of the frame. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed. Background style Normal Transparent Background color This attribute is applied only if the background style is normal. Double click brings up a color definition dialog. Special effects Values: Flat Raised Sunken Etched Bump Shadowed Border style Values: Transparent border is not seen, Solid the border is standard line, Dots the border is dotted line, Dashes the border is dashed line. Border color Valid only if border style is not transparent and special effects flat. Double click brings up a color definition dialog. Border ...

name: Freezecolumns
text: Freezecolumns Freezecolumns This method enables columns on the left side to be visible when scrolling through the grid. Format Freezecolumns (ncolumns) Parameters - ncolumns - number of columns to be frozen, integer. Applied To Grid. Notes To unlock frozen columns enter table_x.Freezecolumns(0). Example Table_1.Freezecolumns(1) !! Locks the first column See also Methods Freezelines

name: Freezelines
text: Freezelines Freezelines This method enables top lines to be visible when scrolling through the grid. Format Freezelines (nlines) Parameters - nlines - number of lines to be frozen (locked), integer. Applied To Grid. Notes To unlock frozen lines enter table_x.Freezelines(0). Example Table_1.Freezelines(1) !! Locks the top line See also Methods Freezecolumns

name: FUNCTION
description: This statement specifies a beginning of a function. The function is a procedure which has arguments and returns a value.
syntax: FUNCTION function_name (arg_1 desc_1 [ARRAY], . . ., arg_n desc_n [ARRAY]) RETURN_VALUE ret_desc
examples: FUNCTION myfnc(CODE A5, FAMMOUNT N15,2, FID N6) RETURN_VALUE A8
text: FUNCTION FUNCTION This statement specifies a beginning of a function. The function is a procedure which has arguments and returns a value. Format FUNCTION function_name (arg_1 desc_1 [ARRAY], . . ., arg_n desc_n [ARRAY]) RETURN_VALUE ret_desc ... statements ... END_FUNCTION Where - arg_1,..., arg_n - names of formal arguments (maximum number of arguments is 30) - desc_1,..., desc_n - argument description represented in a form: An n = length of ANSI alpha field Nnn[,dd] nn = total number of digits, dd = number of decimals Dn n = length of date field (6 or 8) Tn n = length of time field (4 or 6) DT datetime field Wn n = length of unicode UTF-16 field If the input argument is an array, use keyword ARRAY. To determine real number of array elements, use NUMBER_OF_ELEMENTS function. If an array argument is omitted, number of elements is 0 (zero). - ret_desc - description of return value, format is the same as argument description. All the statements between FUNCTION and END_FUNCTION represent the function body. At least one statement in the body must be an assignment with function name on the left-hand side. In this way the return value will be established. Examples FUNCTION myfnc(COD...

name: Function key handlers
description: Function key handlers Function key handlers
text: Function key handlers Function key handlers A function key event handler is a procedure activated as the result of pressing a function key. The function keys are F1, F2, F3, ..., F12 with modifiers Ctrl+, Shift+ and Alt+. Each event handler has the form ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE OnKeyPress(functionkey_identifier) ... event handler code ... END_PROCEDURE On_event_procedure and OnKeyPress are keywords denoting the start of the handler. End_procedure denotes end of the particular handler. Functionkey_identifier defines function key, case-insensitive, and has the form [Ctrl+][Shift+][Alt+]function_key Examples: F5 Ctrl+F5 Shift+F5 Ctrl+Shift+F5 Alt+F5 Ctrl+Shift+Alt+F5 Code examples: ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE OnKeyPress(F5) ... event handler code ... END_PROCEDURE ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE OnKeyPress(Ctrl+F5) ... event handler code ... END_PROCEDURE ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE OnKeyPress(Ctrl+Shift+F5) ... event handler code ... END_PROCEDURE ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE OnKeyPress(Alt+F5) ... event handler code ... END_PROCEDURE See also On_event_procedure

name: Getbackcolor
text: Getbackcolor Getbackcolor This method returns background color of a cell in current sheet. Format Getbackcolor(line, column) Parameters - line - row, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1, etc. - column - column, numeric value, integer. The first column is 1. Instead of column number, an alpha literal containing column header can be used: 'A', 'B', ..., 'AA', 'AB', ... Return Value Background color of a cell, RGB value, integer. Applied To Excel object. Note To get background color of current tab, set line and column to -1. Examples $color = Excel.Getbackcolor(1,1) !!line=1, col=1 $color = Excel.Getbackcolor(1,2) !!line=1, col=2 $color = Excel.Getbackcolor(1,'B') !!same as above $color = Excel.Getbackcolor(-1,-1) !! tab color Using Excel, Grid Getcell

name: Getcell
text: Getcell Getcell This method reads a cell contents from a grid. Format Getcell(line,column) Parameters - line - grid line, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1, etc. Special value is CURRENT_LINE. The current line is the selected one in display-only grid, or a line containing the cursor in data-entry grid. - column - grid column, numeric value, integer. The first column is 1. Instead of numeric value, column identifier can be used. Column identifier is name of the column field. Return Value Value of the cell. Applied To Grid. Notes If you want to read cell value as an alphanumeric one, just enter the command in the form $alpha_variable = Table_n.Getcell(line,column) In that case, output alpha variable contains the value you see in the grid, like 1.000,00 or 01/01/2015 or 10:15:00, etc. But, if you know that a particular column contains numeric value, and you want to read this value in numeric form suitable for calculations, the command is $num_variable = Table_n.Getcell(line,column) If line or column is invalid (< 1 or > number of lines / columns) or type incompatibility occurs (for example, try to move date cell into a numeric variable), output variable will be initialize...

name: Getcolor
text: Getcolor Getcolor This method returns color of the object. Format Getcolor (flag) Parameters - flag - numeric value, integer. This parameter defines which color to be returned. flag = 0, initial back color flag = 1, current back color flag = 2, current text color Return Value Numeric, RGB color. If object does not exist, function returns -1. If 'flag' is invalid, function returns -1. Example ! Let's $$color defined as define_local $$color n 9 L !! numeric, integer $$color = button_1.Getcolor(1) !! get current back color See also Setbackcolor Settextcolor Setbordercolor Methods

name: Getcurrentdirectory
text: Getcurrentdirectory Getcurrentdirectory This method returns current directory from the server. Format Getcurrentdirectory() Parameters Return Value Alpha variable. If connecting was successful, value is blank. Otherwise, the variable holds an error message. Applied To Ftp object. Note Examples $cdir = Ftp.Getcurrentdirectory() Using FTP

name: Getidentity
text: Getidentity Getidentity This method returns next identity value of a grid column. Format Getidentity(column) Parameters - column - grid column, numeric value, integer. The first column is 1. Return Value If current line is 1, result is 1. if current line > 1, result = value_of_previous_cell + 1. Applied To Data-entry grid. Examples if current_line > table_1.Getnumberrows() out_field = table_1.Getidentity(1) end_if See also Methods

name: Getline
text: Getline Getline This method reads one line from a grid. All parameters except the first one are output ones. Format Getline(line, field1, field2, ..., fieldN ) Parameters - line - grid line, numeric value, integer, required parameter. The first line is 1, etc. To specify current row, we can use the internal variable CURRENT_LINE or star (*). Also, the constant ENTIRE_GRID can be used to denote all rows in the grid. - field1 - content of the first column, can be omitted. If ENTIRE_GRID is specified as the first argument, array qualifier is expected, i.e. field1(). - field2 or field2()- content of the second column, can be omitted. - fieldN or fieldN() - content of the the last column, can be omitted. Applied To Grid. Notes If we use ENTIRE_GRID constant, one column will be stored in an array. If number of rows is bigger than number of array elements, the first N rows will be read. Remain rows (from N+1 up to end of the grid) will be ignored. Examples Table_1.Getline(current_line, counter_1, acc_1) Table_1.Getline(*, counter_1, acc_1) Table_1.Getline(current_line, counter_1,, counter_3) Table_1.Getline(5, counter_1,, counter_3) Table_1.Getline($line, counter_1,, counter_3) !! Read...

name: Getnextselected
examples: $sellin = table_1.Getnextselected(-1) !! Index of the first selected line
text: Getnextselected Getnextselected This method returns index of the next selected line in a grid (table). Format Getnextselected (line) Parameters - line - a numeric variable or numeric literal, integer. Index of the line to begin the searching with, or -1 to find the first line. The specified line is excluded from search. The first line has index 1. Return Value Index of the next selected line (1, 2, ...), or -1 if there is no next selected line. Applied To Grid. Examples if table_1.getselectedcount() > 0 $sellin = table_1.Getnextselected(-1) !! Index of the first selected line do_while $sellin <> -1 ... $sellin = table_1.Getnextselected($sellin) !! Next selected end_do end_if See also GetSelectedCount Methods

name: Getnumbercols
text: Getnumbercols Getnumbercols This method returns number of columns in the grid. Format Getnumbercols() Return Value Number of columns, numeric variable. Possible values: >= 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Grid. Examples counter_1 = Table_1.Getnumbercols() See also Getnumberrows

name: Getnumbercols
text: Getnumbercols Getnumbercols This method returns number of columns in the current sheet. Format Getnumbercols() Return Value Number of columns, numeric variable. Possible values: >= 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Excel object. Examples counter_1 = Excel.Getnumbercols() Using Excel See also Getnumberrows, Getnumbersheets

name: Getnumberrows
text: Getnumberrows Getnumberrows This method returns number of the filled rows in the grid (table). Format Getnumberrows() Return Value Number of rows, numeric variable. Possible values: >= 0 Ok -1 Object not found Applied To Grid. Examples counter_1 = table_1.Getnumberrows() See also Methods Getnumbercols

name: Getnumberrows
text: Getnumberrows Getnumberrows This method returns number of rows in the current sheet. Format Getnumberrows() Return Value Number of rows, numeric variable. Possible values: >= 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Excel object. Examples counter_1 = Excel.Getnumberrows() Using Excel See also Getnumbercols, Getnumbersheets

name: Getnumbersheets
text: Getnumbersheets Getnumbersheets This method returns number of sheets in the current workbook. Format Getnumbersheets() Return Value Number of sheets, numeric variable. Possible values: >= 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Excel object. Examples counter_1 = Excel.Getnumbersheets() Using Excel See also Getnumbercols, Getnumberrows

name: Getposition
text: Getposition Getposition This method returns position and size of the object. Format Getposition() Return Value Alpha field containing values in the form: Xpos;Ypos;Width;Height (the delimiter is semicolon). If object does not exist, function returns blank value. Example $alphares = table_1.Getposition() if $alphares <> space $arr() = split(';',$alphares) $xpos = value($arr(1)) $ypos = value($arr(2)) $width = value($arr(3)) $height= value($arr(4)) end_if See also Setposition, Methods

name: GETPRINTERNAME
description: This function returns printer name from list of available printers.
text: GETPRINTERNAME GETPRINTERNAME This function returns printer name from list of available printers. Format GETPRINTERNAME(index) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - index - index in the list of printers, integer. Index starts with 1. This parameter must be in the range from 1 to number of available printers. Note Result is blank string if index <= 0, or index > number_of_available_printers. Examples !! Read names of all available printers counter_1 = 0 do_while 1=1 add 1 to counter_1 $printer = getprintername(counter_1) if $printer = SPACE !! End of list break end_if ... ... !! do something with $printer end_do See also Internal fields (DEFAULT_PRINTER)

name: GETSCRIPT
description: This function tries to determine script used in an Unicode text.
text: GETSCRIPT GETSCRIPT This function tries to determine script used in an Unicode text. Format GETSCRIPT(Unicode_text) Result Value Type Numeric. Values: 0 - Basic Latin 1 - Latin 1 supplement 2 - Latin Extended A 3 - Latin Extended B 4 - Greek 5 - Cyrillic 6 - Armenian 7 - Georgian 8 - Hebrew 9 - Arabic -1 - Unknown Parameters - Unicode_text - utf-16 alpha field. Note Examples $res = GETSCRIPT($utf16) See Also

name: Getselectedcount
text: Getselectedcount Getselectedcount This method returns number of selected lines in a grid (table). Format Getselectedcount() Return Value Number of selected lines, numeric variable. Possible values: >= 0 Ok -1 Object not found Applied To Grid. Examples counter_1 = table_1.Getselectedcount() See also GetNextSelected Methods

name: Gettopline
text: Gettopline Gettopline This method returns first visible line in a grid (table). Format Gettopline() Return Value Ordinal number of the first visible line, numeric variable, starting with 1. Possible values: >= 1 Ok -1 Object not found Applied To Grid. Examples counter_1 = table_1.Gettopline() See Settopline Methods

name: Gettreedata
text: Gettreedata Gettreedata This method returns data stored in the selected tree node. Format Gettreedata() Return Value Applied To Tree. Note Internal variable TREELEVEL denotes level of the selected node (1, 2, ...). Examples $data = tree_1.Gettreedata() See also Insertitem Insertitembykey Methods

name: Global procedures
description: Global procedures Global procedures / functions definition
text: Global procedures Global procedures / functions definition Global procedure(s)/function(s) are subroutines defined at Data Dictionary level. Each program in the Data Dictionary can use these procedures/functions, see Local procedure(s)/ function(s). See also Define global procedures/functions

name: Graph
description: We use a graph to display data graphically.
examples: Values = $nbooks, where is $nbooks numeric array, number of elements=12.
Values = $nbooks_cy, where is $nbooks_cy numeric array, number of elements=12.
Values = $nbooks_py, where is $nbooks_py numeric array, number of elements=12.
To change graph properties double click on the graph
select the graph, right click to display context menu and choose Properties.
text: Graph Graph We use a graph to display data graphically. To insert new graph on the form, click button Graph in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Graph. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the top left corner of the graph. Drag to the right and bottom and release the button. Graph definition dialog appears on the screen. Graph type We can plot three types of graphs: histogram (bar chart), pie chart or line chart. Histogram plots data as vertical columns with two axes: X and Y. X axis is called Category axis. Y axis is called Values axis. Chart source data In this dialog we specify data to be displayed. Data series is a group of related data. In our case, all data belonging to one data series are stored in one array. Each data series has two components: data series name and data series values. Data series name is an alpha literal or alpha variable. Data series values represent a numeric array containing values. To define data series, specify name, the array containing values and click Add. The series name appears in the list. To change a defined data series, select appropriate series in the list. Change name or/and values and click Update. To remove a...

name: Graph properties
text: Graph properties Graph properties

name: Group box
text: Group box Group box The group box is a rectangle with some text located on the top edge. To insert new group box on the form, click button Group box in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Group box. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the top left corner of the group box. Drag to the right and bottom and release the button. New group box with default text "Group box" is placed on the form. To change group box properties double click on the frame edge -or- select the group box, right click to display context menu and choose 'Properties'.

name: Group of objects
description: Group of objects Group of objects
text: Group of objects Group of objects A group is not object itself. The group is, simply, a set of objects. Create new group Select objects, then choose menu command Objects/Groups/Create group. Enter group name in the dialog which appears on the screen. The group name is maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. One object can be member of one group only. Remove an existing group Choose menu command Objects/Groups/Ungroup and select a group from the list. The following methods can be used on groups: Init Setbackcolor Settextcolor Setfont Setvisible Setenable Setreadonly

name: GZIP_COMPRESS
description: This function creates a gzip (.gz) compressed file.
text: GZIP_COMPRESS GZIP_COMPRESS This function creates a gzip (.gz) compressed file. Format GZIP_COMPRESS(input_file, output_file) Return Value Type Numeric. If the function fails result is -1, otherwise, result is 0. Parameters - input_file - input (uncompressed) file, alpha literal or alpha variable. - output_file - output GZIP file (.gz), alpha literal or alpha variable. Notes GZIP_COMPRESS uses ZLIB library written by Jean-Loup Gailly and Mark Adler. Wildcard characters (* and ?) cannot be used in input_file, because GZIP file format supports only one file. If you want to store many input files in one output archive, use any of free archiver programs with command line, like 7-ZIP. Every executable program can be activated by EXECUTE or CALL_PROGRAM /NONIAF. Examples $numstat = GZIP_COMPRESS("a.txt", "a.gz") See also GZIP_DECOMPRESS

name: GZIP_DECOMPRESS
text: GZIP_DECOMPRESS GZIP_DECOMPRESS This function decompresses gzip (.gz) file. Format GZIP_DECOMPRESS(input_file, output_file) Return Value Type Numeric. If the function fails result is -1, otherwise, result is 0. Parameters - input_file - input GZIP file (.gz), alpha literal or alpha variable. - output_file - output (decompressed) file, alpha literal or alpha variable. Note GZIP_DECOMPRESS uses ZLIB library written by Jean-Loup Gailly and Mark Adler. Examples $numstat = GZIP_DECOMPRESS("a.gz","a.txt") See also GZIP_COMPRESS

name: HASH
description: This function calculates hash value of a data.
examples: ! Calculate hash value sha_256 for a file
text: HASH HASH This function calculates hash value of a data. Format HASH(algid, field [, field_length]) Return Value Type Alpha. For MD5, result length is 32 characters. For SHA1, result length is 40 characters. For SHA_256, result length is 64 characters. Parameters - algid - algorithm identifier, numeric literal or numeric field. Possible values: 1 for MD5, 2 for SHA1, 3 for SHA_256. - field - input field, alpha or memo. - field_length - length of input field, optional. If omitted, whole input field will be processed. Note Hash value is fixed-length binary data. For example, MD5 is an 128-bit binary field (16 bytes). Generally, hash value is displayed in readable form as a hexadecimal number. Therefore, MD5 hash value is 32 characters (hexadecimal digits) long. Examples $a64 = HASH(3,$inp,str_length($inp)) !!calculate sha_256 $a64 = HASH(3,$inp_str) !!calculate sha_256 ! Calculate hash value sha_256 for a file ! $s - memo field ! $a64 - alpha ANSI field, field length is 64 characters $status = LOAD_FILE("c:\test\a.txt", $s) if $status = 0 $a64 = HASH(3, $s) init $s !!Release allocated memory end_if See Also

name: Hidecolumn
text: Hidecolumn Hidecolumn This method hides columns of a grid. Format Hidecolumn(column1, column2, ..., columnN) Parameters - column1, column2, columnN - a numeric variable or numeric literal, integer. Applied To Grid. Note Column = -1, hides side heading. Column = 1, 2, ... hides a column. The first column is 1. Display-only grid This method simply hides one or more columns. Data-entry grid If a column denotes source field, this method hides the column only. If a column denotes one of active fields (destination field, combo box or check box), this method hides the column. Also, statements from the 'Destination' up to the end of the field buffer will be ignored. Variable in the 'Destination' statement will have unknown value. Field code: ... before_instructions ... Destination = my_variable ... after_instructions ... last_instruction_in_the_buffer All the statements from Destination to the last_instruction_in_the_buffer will be ignored. Variable my_variable has unknown value. Example Table_1.Hidecolumn(5) Table_1.Hidecolumn(5,6,10) Table_1.Hidecolumn(5,counter_1,counter_5) See also Unhidecolumn, Methods

name: HOUR
description: This function returns hours of a time.
text: HOUR HOUR This function returns hours of a time. Format HOUR(time_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - time_exp - input time expression Note Result is a number in the range from 0 to 23. Examples $hour = HOUR(systime) See Also Time Functions

name: Hyperlink
text: Hyperlink Hyperlink Hyperlink in reports performs the same function as a hyperlink in a html page. We can create hyperlink to a web page, an email address or a file. Hyperlink options Email address Hyperlink contains email address Web page Hyperlink contains web page name File Hyperlink contains file name Tooltip text Tooltip is a text that will be displayed when the cursor hovers above the hyperlink. Tooltip can be an alpha literal, like "This is tooltip" or an alpha variable. If we use variable, its value must be set before object printing. Example: $alphatt = "This is tooltip for " | afield Hyperlink address (email address, web page name, file name) This field contains actual hyperlink: email address, web page name or file name. We can specify alpha literal or alpha variable. Of course, value of the variable must be set before object printing. File name can be absolute or relative. Examples: "emaddress@provider.com" "http://www.acompany.com" "c:\data\myfile.xls" !! absolute path "myfile.xls" !! same directory as print file's dir ".\myfile.xls" !! same as above "subdir\myfile.xls" !! relative: printfile_dir\subdir\... "..\subdir\myfile.xls" !! relative: the same level as print...

name: IDIVIDE
description: This function returns result from the integer division.
text: IDIVIDE IDIVIDE This function returns result from the integer division. Format IDIVIDE(dividend, divisor) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - dividend - - divisor - Examples $N = IDIVIDE(10,3) !! result is 3 $N = IDIVIDE(20,5) !! result is 4 See Also MOD

name: IF
description: Conditional execution of block of the statements.
text: IF IF Conditional execution of block of the statements. Format IF cond_exp ... [ELSE_IF cond_exp ...] [ELSE_IF cond_exp ...] [ELSE ...] END_IF Where - cond_exp - conditional expression. Note ELSE_IF and ELSE parts are optional, END_IF keyword is required. Examples IF acc_1 = 1 ADD 1 TO counter_1 ELSE_IF acc_1 = 2 ADD 2 TO counter_1 ELSE_IF acc_1 = 3 ADD 3 TO counter_1 ELSE ADD 100 to counter_1 END_IF IF acc_1 = 1 add 1 to acc_2 END_IF See also Comparison operators, Logical operators, Conditional expression

name: Image
description: To insert new image on the form, click button Image in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Image. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. File open dialog appears on the screen, current folder is iafdir\iafres. All the images which we intend...
text: Image Image To insert new image on the form, click button Image in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Image. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. File open dialog appears on the screen, current folder is iafdir\iafres. All the images which we intend to use in our applications, should be copied to this folder. Iaf can display ICO, BMP, JPG and GIF images, including GIF animations. Select an image, clik Open. New image is placed on the form. To change image properties double click on the image -or- select the image, right click to display context menu and choose Properties. How run-time engine processes the image? If Picture type is embeded, the image is linked to the executable program. If Picture type is linked, the image file must be copied together with executable program to each PC running the program. Programs with embeded images are simpler to deployment, but their size is bigger.

name: Image properties
text: Image properties Image properties Object name When the new image is created, generator automatically assigns the name Image_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Picture Specifies the path of the picture file. Double-click brings up file dialog. The default folder is IAFRES. You should copy all the pictures, which will be used in application, to the IAFRES folder. Only ICO, BMP, JPEG and GIF images can be displayed. GIF animations are allowed, too. If you use a GIF animation, the form painter will display just the first image (frame), but at run-time real animation will be displayed. The # sign in the first position of the path (for example, #myimage.gif) denotes IAFRES folder. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed. Picture type Embeded Whole picture is stored into the EXE as a reso...

name: Import Data into File
description: Import Data into File Import Data into File
text: Import Data into File Import Data into File

name: Import Data Into RDB Table
description: Import Data Into RDB Table Import Data Into RDB Table
text: Import Data Into RDB Table Import Data Into RDB Table

name: Import Programs
text: Import Programs Import Programs

name: INDEX
description: This function compares a field to array elements, beginning from the first element. If an array element is equal to the field, function returns index of that element.
text: INDEX INDEX This function compares a field to array elements, beginning from the first element. If an array element is equal to the field, function returns index of that element. Format INDEX(array, field) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - array - an array of elements, alpha, numeric, date, time, datetime. Array specification () can be used with this parameter. - field - any field. Field type must be the same as type of array elements. Note The first element has index 1. If the field was not found in the array, result is -1. Examples counter_1 = index($array(), fld) !! with () counter_1 = index($array, fld) !! without () if counter_1 = -1 Pause "Error" end_if ! Let $array has values 100, 200, 300, 400, 500 counter_1 = index($array,400) !! result is 4 counter_1 = index($array,250) !! result is -1 See Also INDEXGE, INDEXLE, Numeric Functions

name: INDEXGE
description: This function compares a field to array elements, beginning from the first element. If an array element is greater or equal to the field, function returns index of that element.
text: INDEXGE INDEXGE This function compares a field to array elements, beginning from the first element. If an array element is greater or equal to the field, function returns index of that element. Format INDEXGE(array,field) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - array - an array of elements, alpha, numeric, date, time, datetime. Array specification () can be used with this parameter. - field - any field. Field type must be the same as type of array elements. Notes The first element has index 1. If the field was not found in the array, result is -1. The array elements must be sorted in ascending order. Examples ! Let $array has values 100, 200, 300, 400, 500 counter_1 = indexge($array,400) !! result is 4 counter_1 = indexge($array,350) !! result is 4 counter_1 = indexge($array,50) !! result is 1 counter_1 = indexge($array,600) !! result is -1 See Also INDEX, INDEXLE, Numeric Functions

name: INDEXLE
description: This function compares a field to array elements, beginning from the first element. If an array element is less or equal to the field, function returns index of that element.
text: INDEXLE INDEXLE This function compares a field to array elements, beginning from the first element. If an array element is less or equal to the field, function returns index of that element. Format INDEXLE(array,field) Return Value Type - array - an array of elements, alpha, numeric, date, time, datetime. Array specification () can be used with this parameter. - field - any field. Field type must be the same as type of array elements. Notes The first element has index 1. If the field was not found in the array, result is -1. The array elements must be sorted in descending order. Examples ! Let $array has values 500, 400, 300, 200, 100 counter_1 = indexle($array(),400) !! result is 2 counter_1 = indexle($array(),350) !! result is 3 counter_1 = indexle($array(),50) !! result is -1 counter_1 = indexle($array(),600) !! result is 1 See Also INDEX, INDEXGE

name: INIT_WINDOW_FIELDS
description: This statement visually initializes fields in the subform from the current field up to the end of subform.
text: INIT_WINDOW_FIELDS INIT_WINDOW_FIELDS This statement visually initializes fields in the subform from the current field up to the end of subform. Format INIT_WINDOW_FIELDS Note INIT_WINDOW_FIELDS and DISPLAY_WINDOW_FIELDS statements replace default reading algorithm. Fields with sequence zero will not be initialized. Example Destination = $seg1 Select * from tbl1 Where kseg = :$seg1 If record tbl1 found display_window_fields else init_window_fields End_if See also DISPLAY_WINDOW_FIELDS Implicit record reading (Default reading)

name: Init
description: This method initializes object on the screen.
text: Init Init This method initializes object on the screen. Format Init() Parameters None. Applied To All objects. Note The initialization is just visual. For combobox, Init() will remove any text from the screen, but the refilling is not necessary because the internal structures (data and text) keep previous values. For tab control, all the fields in current page will be initialized. All data-entry objects (check box, radio button, destination field, combo box, data-entry grid, data selector) have 'field_processed' flag. This flag is set when the data is entered into the field. The Init() method resets this flag as well, but memory buffer remains unchanged. Examples combobox_1.Init() form.Init() See also Methods INIT statement

name: INITCAP
description: This function converts the first letter of each word in uppercase, and all other letters converts in lowercase.
text: INITCAP INITCAP This function converts the first letter of each word in uppercase, and all other letters converts in lowercase. Format INITCAP(alpha_exp) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - Note Words are delimited by space. Example $a = INITCAP("abcd abcd") !! result is "Abcd Abcd" See Also

name: INITCAP_NLS
description: This function converts the first letter of each word in uppercase, and all other letters converts in lowercase.
text: INITCAP_NLS INITCAP_NLS This function converts the first letter of each word in uppercase, and all other letters converts in lowercase. Format INITCAP_NLS(alpha_exp, code_page) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - code page - a valid code page Note Words are delimited by space. Example $a = INITCAP_NLS("abcd š���",1250) !! result is "Abcd Š���" See Also

name: INIT_RECORD
description: This statement fills all fields in record with initial values (ZERO, SPACE, ZERO_DATE, ZERO_TIME).
text: INIT_RECORD INIT_RECORD This statement fills all fields in record with initial values (ZERO, SPACE, ZERO_DATE, ZERO_TIME). Format INIT_RECORD file[.record] Where - file - a name of the file (table) from datasource dialog. - record - a name of the record, if the file has many record definitions. Note For RDB tables, INIT_RECORD moves default values (if exist) into record buffer if access mode is 'update' or 'write'. Example: if table TBL1 was created using SQL command CREATE TABLE TBL1 (col1 varchar(5) default 'abcd') after INIT_RECORD field 'COL1' will contain 'abcd'. Example INIT_RECORD tbl1 INIT_RECORD file1.redef2

name: INPUTBOX
description: This function displays prompt in a dialog box, waits for the user to input text or click a button, and returns string containing the contents of the text box.
text: INPUTBOX INPUTBOX This function displays prompt in a dialog box, waits for the user to input text or click a button, and returns string containing the contents of the text box. Format INPUTBOX(prompt[, title] [, default] [, xpos] [, ypos] [,passlength]) Return Value Type Text. Parameters - prompt - Text to be displayed in the dialog box. - title - Text to be displayed in the title bar of the dialog box. - default - Text to be displayed in the edit box of the dialog as a default value. - xpos - x position (pixels) of the dialog box. - ypos - y position (pixels) of the dialog box. - passlength - if a password to be entered, this parameter specifies maximum numbers of the characters. If this parameter is omitted, normal text will be entered (with echo). Note To omit some arguments, you must include the corresponding comma delimiter. Examples $text = inputbox("Enter your ID") $text = inputbox("Enter your ID",,"1") $passw= inputbox("Enter password",,,,,8) See Also MESSAGEBOX

name: Insertitem
text: Insertitem Insertitem This method inserts an item into an object. Format Insertitem(text_label, data) Parameters - text_label - text to be displayed. - data - data to be stored into this node. When user selects an item, the data will be transferred to appropriate variable. Applied To ComboBox, data selector, tree (initial filling). Note Each item has two parts: text and associated data. All texts are displayed by an object. When user selects an item, associated data will be moved to program variable. The method is obsolete for ComboBox, use InsertItemEx. Example ! Example 1: Fill-in a combo box combo_1.Insertitem("Item 1","01") combo_1.Insertitem("Item 2","02") combo_1.Insertitem("Item 3","03") combo_1.Insertitem("Item 4","04") combo_1.Insertitem("Item 5","05") ! At run-time, if user selects "Item 3", value "03" will be moved to ! the variable defined by "Destination = prog_var" ! Example 2: fill-in combo box from the table combo_1.Deleteallitems() select_all * from tbl1 where fld1=100 combo_1.InsertItem(tbl1.text, tbl1.data) end_read See also Deleteitem, Deleteallitems, Insertitembykey, InsertItemEx, Modifyitem, Methods

name: Insertitembykey
text: Insertitembykey Insertitembykey This method randomly inserts an item into a tree. Format Insertitembykey(level, text_label, data, key) Parameters - level - tree level, integer variable or literal. Allowed values are 1 to 10. - text_label - text to be displayed. - data - data to be stored into this node. When user selects the node, this value will be stored into TREEDATA internal variable. - key - concatenation of the all keys stored in the tree, from level 1 up to the parent level. If we try to insert an item into the first level, this parameter can be omitted, because there in no parent item. Applied To Tree. Note When we fill a tree initially, we use Insertitem method (Level NN, Insert actions). But, if we want to add an item later-on, we should use this method. Example Lets consider the following tree: 001 Item_1 002 Item_2 01 Item_2_1 02 Item_2_2 03 Item_2_3 01 Item_2_3_1 003 Item_3 To insert item [004 Item_4], we write tree_1.Insertitembykey(1,"Item_4","004","") To insert a child item [04 Item_2_4], we write tree_1.Insertitembykey(2,"Item_2_4","04","002") To insert a child item [02 Item_2_3_2], we write tree_1.Insertitembykey(3,"Item_2_3_2","02","00203") See also Deleteitem...

name: InsertItemEx
text: InsertItemEx InsertItemEx This method inserts an item into a combobox, listbox or dataselector, enabling multi-column listbox portion of the combobox. Format InsertItemEx(data, field1, field2, ..., fieldN) Parameters 1. data - data to be stored in this node. When user selects an item, the data will be transferred to appropriate variable. Some special data values enable us to perform commands such as: set listbox headings. This value is: "%heading%"". 2. field1, field2, ..., fieldN - these fields are displayed in the listbox portion of the combobox, when user clicks combobox down arrow or presses any key on the keyboard. Numeric and date fields will be edited using default edit mask. Column widths are defined automatically at run-time. Default column alignment is: LEFT for alpha and date fields, RIGHT for numeric ones. Applied To Combo box, Data selector. Note If you use special value %HEADING% to set listbox headings, the first text denotes heading for data, next one for column 1, etc. If data is not visible (look Combo box properties, attribute Display data and text), heading text for data will be ignored. It is possible to display checkbox in a column of listbox. To setup this...

name: Insertiteminlinecombo
text: Insertiteminlinecombo Insertiteminlinecombo This method inserts an item into a grid combobox if comboboxes in every line have different content. Format InsertItemInLineCombo(line, data, text_label) Parameters - line - grid line containing combobox, integer. Lines are counted from 1. - data - data to be stored into this combobox. When user selects an item, the data will be moved to appropriate variable. - text_label - text to be displayed. Applied To ComboBox in grid. Note Every item has two parts: text and associated data. All texts are displayed by the combobox. When user selects the item, associated data will be moved to program variable. Example ! Example 1: Fill-in a combo box in the first line combo_col1.InsertItemInLineCombo(1,'01','Item 1') combo_col1.InsertItemInLineCombo(1,'02','Item 2') combo_col1.InsertItemInLineCombo(1,'03','Item 3') combo_col1.InsertItemInLineCombo(1,'04','Item 4') combo_col1.InsertItemInLineCombo(1,'05','Item 5') ! At run-time, if user selects "Item 3", value "03" will be moved to ! the variable defined by "Destination = prog_var" ! Example 2: fill-in combo box from the table combo_col1.Deleteallitems(1) select_all * from tbl1 where fld1=100 combo_...

name: Insertrow
text: Insertrow Insertrow This method inserts an empty row into opened MS Word table. Format Insertrow (below_row) Parameters - below_row - row, numeric value. New empty row will be inserted below the one defined by the parameter. If 'below_row' is greater than number of the rows in the table, new line will be added at the end of the table. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Table not opened Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Opentable("#1",0,0) if $stat = 0 $stat = Word.Insertrow(1) !! insert row 2 (below row 1) $stat = Word.Closetable() end_if Using Word See also Closetable, Opentable

name: INTEREST
description: This function calculates the interest payment for a given period.
text: INTEREST INTEREST This function calculates the interest payment for a given period. Format INTEREST(principal,rate,date_from,date_to,flag1,type) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - principal - amount - rate - interest rate - date_from - beginning of the period - date_to - end of the period - flag1 - 0 or 1, see formula below - type - 0 for monthly interest, 1 for annual interest Formula Interest = principal * (((1+(rate/100)) ^ (diff_days(date_to,date_from)/period))-1) Period calculates as: if flag1 = 1 if type = 1 period = day_of_year(endyr(datum_from)) else period = days_in_month(datum_from) end_if else if type = 1 period = day_of_year(endyr(datum_to)) else period = days_in_month(datum_to) end_if Examples See Also

name: Internal Fields
syntax: ACC_1 to ACC_20 have 2 decimals (integer part=16 digits, decimal part=2 digits)
examples: IAF_SQLERROR contains five-characters ODBC error code if SQL statement ends with an error. If SQL statement ends successfully, the variable contains SPACE. This variable need to be tested only if WHENEVER SQLERROR CONTINUE is in effect.
text: Internal Fields Internal Fields Internal fields are predefined fields that can be used in each program. List of all internal fields: ACC_nn CMD_PARAM COUNTER_nn CSV_COUNTER CSV_FILENAME CSV_LINE CSV_PARAM CSV_STATUS CURRENT_COLUMN CURRENT_LINE CURRENT_MOVE_COL CURRENT_MOVE_LINE CURRENT_MOVE_NAME CURRENT_OBJECT_NAME CURRENT_OBJECT_FULLNAME CURRENT_PRINT_POS CURRENT_PROCEDURE DEFAULT_IMAGES DEFAULT_PRINTER END_OF_PAGE EOF IAF_CURRENTLANG IAF_DISPLAYMODE IAF_PRINTMODE IAF_ROWCOUNT IAF_SQLERROR IAF_SQLNATIVE IAF_TABLEMODE NUMBER_LABELS PAGE_COUNTER PAGE_OVERFLOW REPETITION_LINE REPORT_ID STARTING_LABEL TOTAL_PAGES TOTAL_PAGES_ON_PAGEBREAK TREELEVEL WEBSERV_STATUS Some of these fields are read-write: can be changed and used as work variables (ACC_nn, COUNTER_nn, EOF, IAF_PRINTMODE, NUMBER_LABELS, PAGE_COUNTER, STARTING_LABEL). The other fields are read-only. For detailed explanation of these internal fields see below: - ACC_nn Accumulators are signed numeric 18-digit fields. Number of accumulators is 40. Format is the following: ACC_1 to ACC_20 have 2 decimals (integer part=16 digits, decimal part=2 digits) ACC_21 to ACC_30 have 0 decimals (integer part=18 digits) ACC_31 to ACC_35 ha...

name: International support
text: International support International Support International support (multilinguality) enabled If you plan to create a multilingual application, this option should be checked. Default language This is the language you use to develop the applications. If you do not want multilingual applications, leave this field empty or enter the text just for your information. But, if you intend to develop multilingual applications, this field is required. In that case, you can use English name for your language (English, France, German, ...) or enter your local name, for example 'Deutsch' (for German). This parameter can be changed at any stage of application development.

name: Program name
text: Program name Program name This is the first dialog of program generator. In this dialog, we choose program name and an operation which will be performed (modify, delete, rename, ...). Application This combobox shows all applications in Data Dictionary. Current application is displayed in the edit part of the combobox. If DD has one application only, combobox is disabled, else current application can be changed. Program name If we click list of programs, this edit box displays selected program. After that, we can click any of enabled buttons (modify, delete, generate, ...). If we enter program name (with possible wildcards) and press Enter, then this string becomes the filter for the list. Allowed wildcards characters are: * and ?. Examples: * All programs A* Program names starting with letter A *A Program names ending with letter A *PRG* Program names containing PRG PRG? Program names exactly four characters long, starting with PRG List List on the right-side of the dialog shows all interactive programs in this application. Naturally, when we enter this generator for the first time, this list will be empty. List options Names Displays program name, last version number, user and ...

name: Introduction to IAF
description: Introduction to IAF Introduction to IAF
text: Introduction to IAF Introduction to IAF Activation To activate generator, just enter IAF in Run dialog and click OK. Main program form appears on the screen. The folder containing iaf.exe have to be defined by PATH environment variable. - If you use Iaf for the first time, select "New Data Dictionary" menu command to create and open Data Dictionary and application. - Otherwise, open DD/Application by "Open Data Dictionary" menu command or select a DD/Application from MRU list. Command line activation Command line has the format IAF [dd_name[ application]] DD_name, application - optional parameters. If dd_name contains blanks, use question marks to surround the parameter. If the application is omitted, the first created application will be opened. Examples: Iaf Iaf c:\my_dddir\mydd Iaf c:\my_dddir\mydd.dd1 Iaf c:\my_dddir\mydd myappl Iaf c:\my_dddir\mydd.dd1myappl Iaf "c:\my dddir\mydd.dd1" Iaf "c:\my dddir\mydd.dd1" myappl See Main program

name: Introduction
text: Introduction Introduction Data Dictionary utility enables you to define DD entities: global fields, records, files, strings and applications. Also, RDB table definitions can be loaded from an ODBC datasource into the DD. A global field is a template which defines data type (alpha, numeric, date, time), size, default edit-mask and other attributes. We have to define global fields prior to move to Record definition. When the new DD has been created, several standard global field definitions are stored in it. Record is a structured set of fields. Besides global attributes, each field can have local attributes valid in that definition only: column header, help text, default value, range control and the description. File is collection of records containing data. Usually, one file has one record structure. Sometimes, we need more that one record structure in a file. Up to 8 (eight) redefinitions are supported. RDB table is a table stored in a database, like MS SQL Server, Oracle, DB2, MySQL, etc. Prior to use tables in our programs, we have to load their definitions into the DD. String is any text displayed on the screen in a message box, as text label, etc. Application is set of prog...

name: Introduction
text: Introduction Introduction Object FTP allows client access to a FTP server. By using this object, we can download files from the server, upload files to the server, etc. All methods return a value. Return value is an alpha field. If return value is blank, method has successfully ended, otherwise, return value holds an error message. Object name: Ftp Methods: Close Closes connection to the server Createdirectory Creates remote directory Delete Deletes remote file Deletedirectory Deletes remote directory Dir Gets list of files Downloadfile Gets a file from the server (download) Getcurrentdirectory Gets current directory Open Establishes connection to a FTP server Rename Renames remote file Setcurrentdirectory Changes the directory on the server Uploadfile Stores a file to the server (upload) Example 1 Download a file

name: Introduction
text: Introduction Using multiple datasources - Introduction Suppose we have defined two datasources on our PC: DSN1 points to a MS SQL Server database. This is our main (default) data source. DSN2 points to a DB2 database. This is secondary data source. How to execute commands against tables on both databases in the same program? If our main database is MS SQL server, we could create a linked server to DB2 database and use one datasource only (DSN1). This is the simplest option. But, what if we cannot create linked server? Or if we use databases which do not have such functionality. Let's consider two examples. Table TBL1 belongs to DSN1 (MS SQL Server). Table TBL2 belongs to DSN2 (DB2). Datasource DSN2 (DB2) has own table TBL1. Example 1 Copy rows from TBL1 (DSN1) to TBL2 (DSN2) Example 2 Copy rows from TBL1 (DSN1) to TBL1 (DSN2)

name: Introduction
text: Introduction Introduction

name: Introduction
text: Introduction Introduction XML file is a text file combining data and tags like HTML. Such files can be edited by any text editor. In Data Dictionary, we can define these files as "sequential, variable record format". Therefore, instructions like WRITE, READ_ALL can be used to process XML files. To make processing these files easier, we use object named Xml and its methods. There is only one Xml object in a program. This means that two files cannot be opened at the same time. In this case, XML file doesn't need to be defined in the Data Dictionary. All methods return a value. Object name: Xml Methods: Open Close SetPrefix SetXmlDecPoint WriteText WriteNode WriteEndNode WriteElement Example

name: ISAM files
text: ISAM files ISAM files Record Manipulation Operations on records: create new definition, modify existing definition, delete a record definition, copy one definition to another, export/import record definition. Show Records Shows all the records defined in the Data Dictionary. File manipulation Operations on files: create new file definition, modify existing file definition, delete file definition. Show files Shows all the files defined in the Data Dictionary.

name: Isenabled
text: Isenabled Isenabled This method returns the value of enable state of an object. Format Isenabled() Return Value Type Numeric. Return Value If the object is enabled, result is 1, otherwise result is 0. Parameters None. Applied To All objects. Examples $status = button_1.Isenabled() if button_3.Isenabled() = 1 Pause "Enabled !" end_if $objname = 'image_1' $status = $objname.Isenabled() See also Methods Setenable Setvisible Isvisible

name: IsLineModified
text: IsLineModified IsLineModified This method determines whether the grid current line has been modified. Format IsLineModified() Return Value Type Numeric. Return Value If a new line is entered, result is 2. For existing lines, if any field is modified, result is 1, otherwise 0. Applied To Data-entry grid. Note This function is usually used in 'Line ending actions' of the grid. The function takes into account all the fields (columns) except 'Source fields'. Examples $status = Table_1.IsLineModified() if $status = 2 Pause 'New line' else_if $status = 1 Pause 'Modified !' end_if See also Methods Isenabled IsVisible IsLineModifiedExclude

name: IsLineModifiedExclude
text: IsLineModifiedExclude IsLineModifiedExclude This method determines whether the grid current line has been modified. Columns in the list are excluded from checking. Format IsLineModifiedExclude (list_of_columns_to_be_excluded) Parameters - list_of_columns - comma separated list of column names to be excluded from checking. Column name is the name of the field in particular column. Return Value Type Numeric. Return Value If a new line is entered, result is 2. For existing lines, if any field is modified, result is 1, otherwise 0. Applied To Data-entry grid. Note This function is usually used in 'Line ending actions' of the grid. The function takes into account all the destination, combobox and checkbox fields except these in 'list_of_columns'. Examples $status = Table_1.IsLineModifiedExclude(col1, col2) if $status = 2 Pause 'New line' else_if $status = 1 Pause 'Modified !' end_if See also Methods IsLineModified

name: Isvisible
text: Isvisible Isvisible This method returns the state of visibility of an object. Format Isvisible() Return Value Type Numeric. Return Value If the object is visible, result is 1, otherwise result is 0. Parameters None. Applied To All objects. Examples $status = button_1.Isvisible() if button_3.Isvisible() = 1 Pause "Visible !" end_if $objname = 'image_1' $status = $objname.Isvisible() See also Methods Setvisible Setenable Isenabled

name: Joincells
examples: Table_1.Joincells(1,1,5,10) !! the first line, columns 5 thru 10
text: Joincells Joincells This method combines many adjacent cells into one large cell. Format Joincells(line1, line2, column1, column2) Parameters - line1 - starting line, a numeric variable or numeric literal. - line2 - ending line, a numeric variable or numeric literal. - column1 - starting column, a numeric variable or numeric literal. - column2 - ending column, a numeric variable or numeric literal. Applied To Display-only grid. Note To display a text into large cell, use (line1, column1) as parameters. Example Table_1.Joincells(1,1,5,10) !! the first line, columns 5 thru 10 Table_1.Display(1,5,'New large text') See Methods Excel.Joincells

name: JSON_ARRAY
description: This function reads one element from a JSON array.
text: JSON_ARRAY JSON_ARRAY This function reads one element from a JSON array. Format JSON_ARRAY(json_variable, array_name, index) Result Value Type Alpha field or alpha string. Parameters - json_variable - alpha variable containing JSON structure. Variable type is CLOB, longchar (type = S). - array_name - array name, alpha literal or alpha variable. - index - index, integer. The first element has index=1. Note If index < 1 or index > number_of_elements, result is an empty string. Examples $fld = json_array($json,'array_1',1) $fld = json_array($json,'array_1',counter_1) ! Read all elements ($s is string, type=S) $index = 0 do_while $index < 99999 !! 99999 is an arbitrary large number add 1 to $index $s = json_array($json,'array_1',$index) if str_length($s) = 0 break end_if ... do something with string $s ... end_do See also XML_GET_FIELD XML_ADD_FIELD XML_DATASET_FIELD JSON_GET_FIELD

name: JSON_GET_FIELD
description: This function reads one field from a JSON structure.
text: JSON_GET_FIELD JSON_GET_FIELD This function reads one field from a JSON structure. Format JSON_GET_FIELD(json_variable, field_name) Result Value Type Any field. Parameters - json_variable - alpha variable containing JSON structure. Variable type is CLOB, longchar (type = S). - field_name - field name, alpha literal or alpha variable. Note Examples $fld = json_get_field($json,'field_1') See also XML_GET_FIELD XML_ADD_FIELD XML_DATASET_FIELD

name: LEFT
description: This function extracts a substring beginning with the first character in alpha_exp.
text: LEFT LEFT This function extracts a substring beginning with the first character in alpha_exp. Format LEFT(alpha_exp,count) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - count - number of characters to extract, integer literal or integer variable. Examples $res = Left($inp, 5) See also Mid RIGHT

name: LETTERS
description: This function returns textual representation of a number.
text: LETTERS LETTERS This function returns textual representation of a number. Format LETTERS(numeric_exp, language) Return Value Type Letters. Parameters - language - alphanumeric literal denoting language. Allowed values are: "ENG" for english, "MAK" for macedonian, "SLO" for slovenian, "SRB" for serbian. Examples $out80 = letters(acc_1, "eng") !! If acc_1 contains 1234,50 result is: !! "one thousand two hundred thirty four 50/100" See Also

name: Limittext
examples: DestField_1.LimitText(40) !! maximum 40 characters can be entered
text: Limittext Limittext This method limits amount of the text, in characters, that can be entered into an alphanumeric destination field. Format 1 Limittext (numchars) Limits text of a field Format 2 Limittext (line,column,numchars) Limits text of a grid cell Parameters - numchars - numeric variable or numeric literal greater than zero. - line - grid line, numeric value. The lines are counted from 1.To specify current line, use the internal variable CURRENT_LINE or star (*). - column - grid column, numeric value. The columns are counted from 1. Applied To Destination field, field type is alphanumeric. Note - Number of input characters is defined by edit mask, see Edit mask. - If IgnoreEditMask attribute is set, edit mask is ignored and number of input characters is equal to field length. To change number of input characters dynamically, this method should be used. However, this method can't increase number of input characters defined by painter. For example, if edit mask is XXXXXXXX (allows eight characters), then any value less than 8 can be used as parameter. To limit the text in entire grid column, use ENTIRE_COLUMN constant in the form Table_1.Limittext (ENTIRE_COLUMN,column,num...

name: Line
description: To insert new line on the form, click button Line in toolbox or choose Objects/Line. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the beginning of the line. Drag to the desired end of the line and release the button. New line is placed on th...
text: Line Line To insert new line on the form, click button Line in toolbox or choose Objects/Line. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the beginning of the line. Drag to the desired end of the line and release the button. New line is placed on the form. Create horizontal line Click button Line in toolbox or choose Objects/Line. Press Shift. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the beginning of the line. Drag to the right or left, release the mouse button and release the shift key. Create vertical line Click button Line in toolbox or choose Objects/Line. Press Shift. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the beginning of the line. Drag to the top or bottom, release the mouse button and release the shift key. Create maximal horizontal line Click button Line in toolbox or choose Objects/Line. Press Ctrl. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. To change line properties (style, width, color) double click on the line -or- select the line, right click to display context menu and choose 'Properties'.

name: Line properties
text: Line properties Line properties Object name When the new line is created, generator automatically assigns the name Line_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed. Line style Transparent Line is invisible Solid Line is standard, solid, line Dots Dotted line Dashes Dashed line Line color Line color. Line width Possible values: 0, 1, 2, ... pixels. Special effects If line width >= 2 and y_size = 0, or x_size = 0, ie. line is horizontal or vertical one, you can choose one of the following: Flat Standard line Raised Line with raised effects Sunken Line with sunken effects X position, Y position Change line position on the screen. X size, Y size Change line size. If x_size = 0, the line is vertical; if y_size = 0, the line is horizontal one. Note: this jo...

name: Lineset properties
text: Lineset properties Lineset properties Print page header on the first page only? Valid for page header only. If you want to print page header only once at the beginning of the program, select this check box. Print on page overflow? Valid for sub-headers only. If you want the sub-header to be printed always when the skip to the new page occurs, select this check box. This option is recommended in order to reach better report layout. Next lineset overlaps this one? Valid for sub-headers only. Generally, after printing of a sub-header, print position (vertical offset from the top of the page) increases by sub-header height. Next lineset starts from new print position. If this options is checked, next lineset starts from the same print position as this subheader. Is this lineset repetitive? Generally, one input record generates one detail line, or activates break which causes printing one group lineset. In some cases, we need to repeat a lineset many times with various values. Example: a customer takes loans from a bank in various currencies. In detail line we can print every loan (amount, currency, dates, etc), but in subtotal line we want to print total amounts per each currency. T...

name: LIST

An explicit group can be defined via 'Objects/Groups/Create group' command. To initiate a group operation, invoke GROUPBOX_NAME.method(), or EXPLICIT_GROUP_NAME.method(). name: Loadexternal
text: Loadexternal Loadexternal This method loads external datasource into Excel worksheet. Format 1 Loadexternal (textfile_source, destination_cell) Format 2 Loadexternal (database_source, destination_cell, sql_command) Parameters - textfile_source - file name in the form: 'TEXT;path'. Keyword TEXT is required. - destination_cell - destination cell in the form 'Xn'. This is starting cell where the resulting set will be placed. - database_source - database connection string in the form 'ODBC;DSN=dsn;UID=uid;PWD=password'. Keyword ODBC is required. - sql_command - SQL command to be run on the ODBC datasource.. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Invalid range Applied To Excel. Note Examples $status = Excel.Loadexternal('TEXT;c:\temp\File1.csv','A1') $status = Excel.Loadexternal('ODBC;DSN=mydsn;UID=myuid;PWD=mypwd', \ 'A1', \ 'Select * from tbl') ! Example $status = Excel.Open() if $status = 0 $status = Excel.Loadexternal('TEXT;c:\temp\File1.csv','A1') $status = Excel.Run() end_if Using Excel

name: LOAD_FILE
description: This function loads a file into an alpha field or a long field (LONGVARCHAR or LONGVARBINARY) of an RDB table. Result is a numeric_exp containing status. If successful, status = 0.
text: LOAD_FILE LOAD_FILE This function loads a file into an alpha field or a long field (LONGVARCHAR or LONGVARBINARY) of an RDB table. Result is a numeric_exp containing status. If successful, status = 0. Format LOAD_FILE(file_name,field) Return Value Type Numeric. Possible values: 0 load successful, 2 error: no such file or directory 13 error: permission denied Parameters - file_name - file name, alpha literal or alpha variable. Logical names allowed. - field - result field. If the field is alpha one and its length is less than file size, truncation occurs. If the field is LONG, its length will be adjusted automatically to include whole file. Example ! !This function loads file c:\test\a.txt into the field MEMO ! ! $status is defined as numeric, without decimals. ! $status = LOAD_FILE("c:\test\a.txt",tbl1.memo) if $status <> 0 pause "Load error" | $status end_if !This piece of the code inserts one row into the RDB table tbl1: tbl1.col1 = 1 tbl1.col2 = today tbl1.col3 = program_name $status = LOAD_FILE("c:\test\a.txt",tbl1.memo) if $status <> 0 pause "Load error" | $status tbl1.memo = NULL !! null value end_if write tbl1 See Also Save_file, Displayimage

name: Load table definitions from ODBC datasource into the DD
description: Load table definitions from ODBC datasource into the DD Load table definitions from ODBC datasource into the DD
text: Load table definitions from ODBC datasource into the DD Load table definitions from ODBC datasource into the DD To use database tables in your programs, you must load their definitions into the Data Dictionary.

name: Local fields definition
description: Local fields definition Local fields definition
text: Local fields definition Local fields definition Local fields are variables defined in a program and can be used in that program only. These variables can be seen from any part of the program (field calculations, subform calculations, local procedures, global procedures, ...). Local fields are usually used as work variables, to save temporary results, etc. Maximum number of local fields in one program is 500. Each editor line defines one local field. The line format is: local_field_name field_type size [data_type] [occurence] [!!comment] local_field_name = local field name is maximum 60 characters long, the first character must be a dollar sign ($). field_type = field type mnemonic. field_type mnemonic field type A alpha ANSI: 1 character occupies 1 byte, max length 32767 N numeric D date T time W alpha, UTF-16: 1 character occupies 2 bytes, max length 32767 DT datetime S memo ANSI SW memo unicode utf-16 memo: variable length alpha field. size = field length. For numeric fields this parameter is in the form tot_digits[,dec_digits]. data_type = applies to numeric fields only. data_type mnemonic data type U unpacked numeric (default) P BCD packed numeric (obsolete) W integer, 2 byt...

name: Local procedures
description: Local procedures Local procedures / functions definition
text: Local procedures Local procedures / functions definition Local procedure(s)/function(s) are subroutines defined in a program and can be used in this program only, see Global procedure(s)/ function(s). A procedure starts with PROCEDURE keyword and ends with END_PROCEDURE. A function starts with FUNCTION keyword and ends with END_FUNCTION. All the local procedures/functions are defined in the same place. In procedure/function definition we can use DEFINE_LOCAL statement to define local fields which can be seen just from this procedure/function. Note: if we want to call one local procedure from another local procedure, called procedure must be defined at the beginning of the buffer. Examples: Procedure proc1 ... perform proc2 !!invalid, proc2 not defined yet end_procedure Procedure proc2 ... perform proc1 !!ok, proc1 defined before proc2 end_procedure Function fnc1() return_value A5 ... end_function Function fnc2(arg1 n3) return_value N1 ... end_function

name: Logical Name Table Maintenance
description: Logical Name Table Maintenance Logical Name Table Maintenance
text: Logical Name Table Maintenance Logical Name Table Maintenance Logical names table establishes connection between logical name and physical path of a file. To avoid absolute file references, like c:\data\file1.txt, we use logical names like datdir:file1.txt where datdir is a logical name.
