At run-time, a program reads logical names table (this is Logntt00.iaf file at default directory) and creates absolute file name.By simple changing just one line in logical name table, you can redirect your file at any folder. Record format of Logntt00.iaf file is: DEFINE logical_name physical_folder where is DEFINE mandatory keyword logical_name your logical name physical_folder absolute path. If the path contains blanks, physical_folder must be surrounded by quotation marks. There are two special folders: . (dot) denotes current folder. ..\dir denotes a folder parallel to current folder. Logntt00.iaf is an ordinary ANSI text file and can be created/modified by any text editor. Also, this file can be created by a program, or via Iaf dialog. Examples: DEFINE datdir c:\mydata DEFINE prtdir c:\myreports DEFINE prgdat "c:\program files\myappl" DEFINE myfile c:\mydata\file1.txt DEFINE wrkdir . DEFIN...

name: Logical operators
text: Logical operators Logical operators Logical operators let us join two or more conditional expressions. Logical operators are AND combines two conditions and returns TRUE when both conditions are TRUE. OR combines two conditions and returns TRUE when either of conditions are TRUE. NOT reverses value of a Boolean expression, unary operator. Examples if a = b AND c = d if a = b OR c = d if a = b AND (c = d OR e = f) if NOT a = b if a = b AND NOT c = d Logical operators precedence (order of evaluation) List of operators with decreasing precedence is NOT AND OR The precedence of NOT is greater than that of AND, etc. To avoid confusion, use parentheses ! Example 1 if a = b AND c = d OR e = f is the same as if (a = b AND c = d) OR e = f Example 2 if a = b OR c = d AND e = f is the same as if a = b OR (c = d AND e = f) Example 3 if a = b OR c = d AND e = f OR g = h is the same as if a = b OR (c = d AND e = f) OR g = h Truth table for: condexp1 AND condexp2 OR condexp3 condexp1 condexp2 condexp3 result T True T F True T F T True T F False F T True F T F False F T True F False Truth table for: condexp1 OR condexp2 AND condexp3 condexp1 condexp2 condexp3 result T True T F True T F T True T...

name: Lookup dialog
text: Lookup dialog Lookup dialog /LOOKUP qualifier displays lookup dialog at run-time. Format: Destination = field/LOOKUP: SQL_statement [HEADING heading_text] [TITLE title_text] Destination = field/LOOKUPA: SQL_statement [HEADING heading_text] [TITLE title_text] or Destination = field/LOOKUP: lookup_name Destination = field/LOOKUPA: lookup_name Lookup name is name of lookup dialog defined by Define lookup dialog painter command. This dialog enables us to define complex lookup dialog. If you use /LOOKUP, the dialog will be displayed if you click appropriate magnifier or press CTRL+A when the field has the input focus. If you use /LOOKUPA, the dialog will be displayed automatically when the field gets input focus. SQL_statement is a standard SELECT statement, specifying fields to be displayed in the dialog. SELECT fld1, fld2 FROM tbl ORDER BY fld1 SELECT DISTINCT fld1, fld2 FROM tbl ORDER BY fld1 SELECT fld1, fld2 FROM tbl WHERE fldx=1 ORDER BY fld1 SELECT fld1, fld2 FROM tbl WHERE fldx=:$parameter ORDER BY fld1 SELECT fld1, fld2 FROM tbl WHERE fldx=:$parameter group by fld1,fld2 ORDER BY fld1,fld2 SELECT fld1, fld2,count(*) into :$cnt FROM tbl group by fld1,fld2 ORDER BY fld1,fld2 Ta...

name: LOWER
description: This function converts an alpha field to lower case.
text: LOWER LOWER This function converts an alpha field to lower case. Format LOWER (alpha_exp) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - alpha field, ANSI or UTF-16. Note If alpha field is ANSI one, the conversion is performed according to the current code page. Examples $res = Lower('ABCD') !! Result = abcd See Also LOWER_NLS, UPPER, UPPER_NLS, INITCAP, INITCAP_NLS

name: LOWER_NLS
description: This function converts all characters in alpha_exp to lower case.
text: LOWER_NLS LOWER_NLS This function converts all characters in alpha_exp to lower case. Format LOWER_NLS(alpha_exp, code_page) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - - code_page - a valid code page Examples See Also LOWER, UPPER, UPPER_NLS, INITCAP, INITCAP_NLS String functions

name: Close
description: This method closes printer port opened by Open method. After this, another port can be opened, etc.
text: Close Close This method closes printer port opened by Open method. After this, another port can be opened, etc. Format Close() Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Port was not open Applied To Lineprinter object. Example $lpstat = Lineprinter.Close() See also Open Direct printing

name: Introduction
text: Introduction Introduction To avoid windows spooler and directly print plain text to a printer on a PC, LINEPRINTER object can be used. Printer can be locally connected to the PC's port, or can be shared printer on local area network. If the printer is shared resource, redirection must be performed via NET USE command, for example: NET USE LPT2: \\server\printer1. All methods return an integer value. Object name: Lineprinter Methods: Open - opens a printer port Write - writes a text to the printer Close - closes the printer port Example $lpstat = Lineprinter.Open('LPT1:') if $lpstat = 0 $lpstat = Lineprinter.Write('Some text') $lpstat = Lineprinter.Write(crlf) $lpstat = Lineprinter.Write(compress_trim('Some text',crlf)) $lpstat = Lineprinter.Write(compress_trim($escseq1,'Some text',$escseq2,crlf)) $lpstat = Lineprinter.Close() end_if

name: Open
text: Open Open This method opens printer port. Format Open(printer_port) Parameters - printer_port - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the printer port, such as LPT1:, LPT2:, COM1:, ... Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Printer port already open > 0 An error Applied To Lineprinter object. Note Examples $lpstat = Lineprinter.Open('LPT1:') $lpstat = Lineprinter.Open('COM2:') $portname = 'LPT2:' $lpstat = Lineprinter.Open($portname) if $lpstat = 0 ... end_if See also Close Direct printing

name: Write
description: This method writes text to the printer port.
text: Write Write This method writes text to the printer port. Format Write(field[,code_page]) Parameters - field - buffer containing text, an alpha variable or alpha literal. - code_page - code page, optional parameter. If omitted, no conversion will be performed. Allowed values: 852 (convert 'field' to code page 852), 437 (convert 'field' to code page 437) and 255 (remove diacritics). Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok. -1 Port was not open Applied To Lineprinter object. Note If you want to move print head to the next line, CRLF must be appended to the text. Example $lpstat = Lineprinter.write($text) $lpstat = Lineprinter.write($text,852) $lpstat = Lineprinter.write(compress_trim($text,crlf)) See also Direct printing

name: LPAD
description: This function returns an alpha field, left-padded with another string.
text: LPAD LPAD This function returns an alpha field, left-padded with another string. Format LPAD(alpha_exp, padstr) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - field to be left-padded, alpha literal or alpha field. - padstr - pad character or pad string, alpha literal or alpha field. Note Input field is aligned to the right and all left spaces are replaced by padstr. Examples $Outstr = LPAD($INPSTR,'0') $Outstr = LPAD($INPSTR,'abc') $Outstr = LPAD($Outstr,' ') $Outstr = LPAD($Outstr,$PAD) ! Fill a field by a character init $Outstr $Outstr = lpad($Outstr,'0') ! Suppose that $a10 is defined as alpha, length = 10. $a10 = 'A' $a10 = LPAD($a10,'0') !! result is: '000000000A' See Also String Functions RPAD

name: LTRIM
description: This function removes white space from the start of alpha_exp.
text: LTRIM LTRIM This function removes white space from the start of alpha_exp. Format LTRIM(alpha_exp) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - input alpha field. Examples $res = LTRIM($inp) $res = LTRIM($res) See Also ALLTRIM, RTRIM String functions

name: MAIL
text: MAIL MAIL This statement sends a mail. Format MAIL address,subject,body,attachment[,"NODIALOG"] Where - address - recipient's address - subject - subject text (can be empty string) - body - message text (can be empty string) - attachment - files to be sent as attachment, separated by semicolon (can be empty string) - NODIALOG - enables to send email without the dialog box prompting the user for sending options. Can be abbreviated to "N". If this parameter is omitted, the dialog box appears on the screen. Note This statement will launch default e-mail client on local PC. A program simply uses set of functions exported by MAPI32.DLL to prepare and send a mail. MAPI32.DLL must be installed. Examples $address = "COMPANY@XXX.NET" $body = "message text, ..." $attach = "c:\a1.txt; c:\a2.txt" MAIL $address, "subject text", $body, $attach MAIL "COMPANY@XXX.NET", "subject text", $body, $attach MAIL $address, "subject text", $body, $attach,"NODIALOG" MAIL $address, "subject text", $body, $attach,"N" MAIL $address, "subject text", $body, "","N"

name: Mailing label format
description: Mailing label format Mailing label format
text: Mailing label format Mailing label format Top margin (mm) The distance from the top edge of the paper to the first label in millimeters. Side margin (mm) The distance from the left edge of the paper to the first label in millimeters. Width (mm) The width of the label in millimeters. If there is horizontal gap between labels, the gap size should be added to the width. Height (mm) The height of the label in millimeters. If there is vertical gap between labels, the gap size should be added to the height. Number across Number of labels in a row. Number down Number of rows. Page size Type of the paper (A4, A5, Letter, ...). If 'Custom' is selected, form name should be defined too (see Form name bellow). Draw in physical units (mm) at run-time If pre-printed forms are used, this option enables printing text or graphics to exact position, for example, 5 mm from the left edge of the label. Draw cross lines to separate labels If ordinary, clear paper is used, lines between labels can be drawn for easier paper cutting. Number of copies Numeric literal or numeric variable. If blank, 1 is assumed. This parameters is valid only if we choose direct printing in Print dialog at run-time. Form n...

name: MAKEDATE
description: This function returns date value calculated from the year, month and day.
text: MAKEDATE MAKEDATE This function returns date value calculated from the year, month and day. Format MAKEDATE(year,month,day) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - year - numeric_exp - month - numeric_exp (from 1 to 12) - day - numeric_exp (from 1 to number of days in the month) Note If month or day arguments are invalid, result is zero_date. Examples $Date = makedate(2014,1,1) !! result is '1-jan-2014' $Date = makedate(2014,0,1) !! result is zero_date $Date = makedate(4,1,1) !! result is '1-jan-0004' See Also Date Value_date Date Functions

name: MAKETIME
description: This function returns time value calculated from the hour, minute and second.
text: MAKETIME MAKETIME This function returns time value calculated from the hour, minute and second. Format MAKETIME(hour,minute,second) Return Value Type Time. Parameters - hour - numeric_exp (from 0 to 23) - minute - numeric_exp (from 0 to 59) - second - numeric_exp (from 0 to 59) Note If arguments are invalid, result is zero_time. Examples $time6 = maketime(10,1,50) !! result is "10:01:50" $time6 = maketime(25,0,1) !! result is zero_time See Also Time Functions

name: Mathematical operators
text: Mathematical operators Mathematical operators Mathematical operators are + addition - subtraction, unary minus * multiplication / division ^ exponent For integer division see IDIVIDE , MOD.

name: MAX
description: This function returns the highest of the input values for all processed records between two break fields.
text: MAX MAX This function returns the highest of the input values for all processed records between two break fields. Format MAX(val_exp) Return Value Type Any type. Parameters - val_exp - variable from main table Notes This function can be used in reports and batch programs only. For alpha fields (ANSI or UTF-16), this function finds the highest value using binary comparison. Examples Source = MAX(alpha_field) See Also COUNT, AVG, MIN, TOTAL

name: Mergeclose
text: Mergeclose Mergeclose This method closes mailmerge data file. Atfer this, the template document can be opened and mailmerge invoked. Format Mergeclose() Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Mergeclose() Using Word See also Mergeopen

name: Mergedata
text: Mergedata Mergedata This method stores one field into the mailmerge record buffer. Format Mergedata(variable [,code_page]) Parameters - variable - value which will be stored into the record buffer. - codepage - if variable is an alpha_exp, this parameter specifies variable's code page. The parameter is very important because MS Office tools (Excel, Word, ...) use UNICODE to represent text data, while text data in programs use, in most cases, single-byte representation. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Data file not opened Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Mergedata($fld) Using Word

name: Mergeopen
text: Mergeopen Mergeopen This method creates new mailmerge data file. Format Mergeopen(filename) Parameters - filename - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the mailmerge data file. Logical names in filename are allowed. We suppose that there exists a Word document which use this file for mailmerge. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Mergeopen("c:\tmp\mm.txt") Using Word

name: Mergewrite
text: Mergewrite Mergewrite This method writes one record into mailmerge data file. Format Mergewrite() Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Mergewrite() Using Word

name: MESSAGEBOX
description: This function displays a message in a dialog box, waits for the user to click a button, and returns an integer indicating which button the user clicked.
text: MESSAGEBOX MESSAGEBOX This function displays a message in a dialog box, waits for the user to click a button, and returns an integer indicating which button the user clicked. Format MESSAGEBOX(prompt[, buttons] [, title]) Return Value Type Numeric. Possible values: 1 OK clicked 2 Cancel 3 Abort 4 Retry 5 Ignore 6 Yes 7 No Parameters - prompt - Text to be displayed in the dialog box. Maximum length of the text is approximately 1024 characters, depending on the width of the character used. If the text consists of more than one line, you can separate the lines using a carriage return/linefeed character combination (CHR(13) | CHR(10)) between each line. - button - numeric_exp Sum of values specifying the number and type of buttons to display, the icon style to use, the identity of the default button, and the modality of the message box. If ommited, the default value for button is 0. Values: 0 Display OK button only. 1 Display OK and Cancel buttons. 2 Display Abort, Retry, and Ignore buttons. 3 Display Yes, No, and Cancel buttons. 4 Display Yes and No buttons. 5 Display Retry and Cancel buttons. 16 Display Critical Message icon. 32 Display Warning Query icon. 48 Display Warning Messa...

name: Methods
text: Methods Methods Methods are functions that operate on objects. An object represents an element of a form, or report, such as label, source field, destination field, combobox, checkbox, button, form itself, subform, status bar, etc. Object must be identified by its name. The object name is a standard identifier, like Label_1, Table_1, Subform_1. If an object belongs to a tabcontrol, identifier is in the form tab.tabpage.object_name, like Tab_1.tabPage_1.Label_1. Object name can be seen in form(report) painter selecting 'Show/Object names' in main menu, or activating 'Property dialog' of the object. In this dialog, object name can be changed, too. Syntax: If the method does not return a value, the statement format is Object_name.method(arg1, arg2, ..argN) Example Button_1.Setvisible(0) If the method returns a value, the statement format is output_value = Object_name.method(arg1, arg2, ..argN) Example $result = Table_1.Getcell($line, $column) $result = Label_1.Objvalue() Object_name is an identifier or local field name. Examples Sourcefield_1.Display(acc_1) Sourcefield_1.Display(Compress(fld1,fld2)) Sourcefield_1.Display(alltrim(fld1) | ',' | alltrim(fld2)) Table_1.Display(current_...

name: MID
description: This function extracts specified number of characters from an alpha field.
examples: $res = Mid($inp, 1, 0) !! is the same as init $res
text: MID MID This function extracts specified number of characters from an alpha field. Format MID(alpha_field, start[, count]) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_field - source alpha field, ANSI or UTF-16. - start - starting position of the substring, integer literal or integer variable. The leftmost character has position 1. - count - number of characters to extract, integer literal or variable. Optional argument. If this argument is omitted, than the rest of the string is extracted. Note If start <= 0 or start > field_length or count <= 0, output string will be initialized. Examples $res = Mid($inp, 5, 3) $res = Mid($inp, 5) $res = Mid($inp, 0, 5) !! error, start = 0 $res = Mid($inp, 1, 0) !! is the same as init $res See also LEFT RIGHT String functions

name: MIN
description: This function returns the smallest of the input values for all processed records between two break fields.
text: MIN MIN This function returns the smallest of the input values for all processed records between two break fields. Format MIN(val_exp) Return Value Type Any type. Parameters - val_exp - variable from main table Notes This function can be used in reports and batch programs only. For alpha fields (ANSI or UTF-16), this function finds the smallest value using binary comparison. Examples Source = MIN(alpha_field) See Also COUNT, AVG, MAX, TOTAL

name: MINUTE
description: This function extracts minutes of a time.
text: MINUTE MINUTE This function extracts minutes of a time. Format MINUTE(time_exp) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - time_exp - Note Result is a number in the range 0 to 59. Examples See Also Time Functions

name: MOD
description: This function returns remainder from the integer division.
text: MOD MOD This function returns remainder from the integer division. Format MOD(num1, num2) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - num1 - integer, numeric_exp - num2 - integer, numeric_exp Note If num1 < 0, result is negative. Examples $N = mod (10,3) !! result is 1 See Also IDIVIDE

name: Modify
text: Modify Modify

name: Modifyitem
text: Modifyitem Modifyitem This methods modifies text label of a tree item. Format Modifyitem(level, text_label, key) Parameters - level - tree level, integer variable or literal. Allowed values are 1 to 10. - text_label - text to be displayed. - key - concatenation of the all keys stored in the tree, from level 1 up to this level. Applied To Tree. Note To refill whole tree, we can use: Tree_1.Init() Tree_1.Setfocus() But, if we want to change text label of one item, we can use this method. Example Tree_1.ModifyItem(1,"New text label",$key) Lets consider the following tree: 001 Item_1 002 Item_2 01 Item_2_1 02 Item_2_2 03 Item_2_3 003 Item_3 If we want to change text of Item_2_3, we should write Tree_1.ModifyItem(2,"Item_2_3 changed","00203") See also Insertitem, Insertitembykey, Deleteitem, Methods

name: Modifystring
text: Modifystring Modify string An identifier is a sequence of English letters, digits, underscore character(_) and dollar sign ($). The first character must be a letter (A to Z). Maximum number of characters in an identifier is 30. Identifiers are case-insensitive, so IDS_1 and ids_1 denotes the same string. Examples: IDS_1 IDS_2 ids_3 ids_nosuchrecord ident_invrange Text is any text stored in Unicode UTF-8 format. Examples: This is message 1 Invalid date Invalid range These strings can be displayed using STRING function. There are two special characters: % (parameter placeholder) and \ (escape character). The parameter placeholder enables inserting program variables into a text using STRING_EDIT function. The escape character allows including into a text some characters which cannot be typed in directly. Parameter placeholder The percent sign (%) is placeholder Invalid value, must be in the range % to % Such strings can be displayed using STRING_EDIT function. Special characters Line feed \n Carriage return \r Tabulator \t Backslash \\ Percent sign \% Example: if we want to display two-lines message, we can enter Two-lines message\r\nThis is the second line. At run-time, the ERRMSG...

name: MONTH
description: This function returns month of a date.
text: MONTH MONTH This function returns month of a date. Format MONTH(date) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date - date or datetime field. Note If date = zero_date or date = NULL, result is 0. Otherwise, result is a number in the range from 1 to 12. Examples $n2 = month(today) See Also Date Functions

name: Multiple columns
text: Multiple columns Multiple columns This option applies to 'Detail' lineset only. Normally, a detail line prints below the previous one, down the paper. But, if the detail line contains few fields, we can define columns to save the paper. Number of columns Enter number of columns. Iaf automatically calculates largest number of columns regarding to the current width of the detail line. To disable columnar report, enter 0 or 1. Print direction Across then down - print detail line in a column, then move to the next (right-hand) column and so on, up to the most right-hand column. After that, move down the page, print detail line in the first column, etc. Down then across - print detail line in a column, move down the page, and so on up to the overflow position. After that, move to the right-hand column, etc.

name: NEWGUID
description: This function generates universally unique identifier.
text: NEWGUID NEWGUID This function generates universally unique identifier. Format NEWGUID() Result Value Type Alpha. Parameters None. Note Resulting alpha field, 36 characters long, has the form 12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012 Examples $a36 = newguid()

name: NEXT
description: This statement starts next iteration of immediately innermost loop (DO_WHILE , READ_ALL or SELECT_ALL). All the statements between NEXT and end of loop will be skipped.
text: NEXT NEXT This statement starts next iteration of immediately innermost loop (DO_WHILE , READ_ALL or SELECT_ALL). All the statements between NEXT and end of loop will be skipped. Format NEXT Notes If the NEXT is used in Additional view actions outside of a loop, next record from the input record stream will be read. Example Select_all tbl If tbl.fld1 = 100 Next End_if ... End_read counter_1 = 0 do_while counter_1 < 10 add 1 to counter_1 if counter_1 = 6 or counter_1 = 8 Next end_if ... end_do See also Do_while Read_all Select_all Break

name: NEXT_WORKING_DAY
description: This function returns next working day.
text: NEXT_WORKING_DAY NEXT_WORKING_DAY This function returns next working day. Format NEXT_WORKING_DAY(date_exp) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date_exp - date or datetime field. Note Working days are all days except sunday, and dates defined with IAF_HOLIDAYS symbol. Examples $d8 = next_working_day($d8) See Also Date Functions

name: NUM
description: This function converts alpha field to numeric field. Also, this function can be used to cast date, time and datetime fields to numeric.
text: NUM NUM This function converts alpha field to numeric field. Also, this function can be used to cast date, time and datetime fields to numeric. Format NUM(value_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - value_exp - a value expression: alpha, date, time, datetime or numeric. Note If 'value_exp' is an alpha field, it must have all numeric characters (digits). The NUM function is a redefinition: if we know that an alpha variable contains all digits, then with NUM function we declare that variable as numeric. This is why we cannot use this function with partially numeric field. For instance, if $a6 is an alpha field whose length is 6 characters and contains '123', we cannot use NUM($a6). Instead, VALUE function should be called. Examples if $a8 is numeric $num_field = num($a8) end_if ! if $a8 is numeric $res = num($a8) + 5 else $res = 5 end_if ! $num_field = num(today) $num_field = num(timestamp) See Also VALUE , Comparison operators

name: NUMBER_OF_ELEMENTS
description: This function returns number of elements of the array.
text: NUMBER_OF_ELEMENTS NUMBER_OF_ELEMENTS This function returns number of elements of the array. Format NUMBER_OF_ELEMENTS(array_name) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - array_name - array identifier. Notes Example counter_1 = number_of_elements($arr)

name: Numeric Functions
text: Numeric Functions Numeric Functions ABS returns absolute value. CEIL rounds a numeric value. DIGIT_OF returns one digit from a numeric value. IDIVIDE returns result from the integer division. INDEX compares a field to array elements. INDEXGE compares a field to array elements. INDEXLE compares a field to array elements. MOD returns remainder from the integer division. NUM converts alpha field to numeric field. NUMRECORDS returns number of records in a file or in an RDB table. ROUND rounds numeric field. SQRT returns square root of a numeric value. SUM returns sum of elements of a numeric array. SYMBOL returns value of a symbol. TRUNC truncates numeric field. VALUE converts an alpha literal to the numeric value.

name: NUMRECORDS
description: This function returns number of records in a file or in an RDB table.
text: NUMRECORDS NUMRECORDS This function returns number of records in a file or in an RDB table. Format NUMRECORDS(table_name) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - table_name - alpha literal containing table name from 'Datasource' dialog. Notes If table_name denotes an RDB table, the function calculates result using SQL statement SELECT count(*) from table_name If table_name denotes an indexed Btrieve file, program calculates result using Btrieve function BSTAT. If table_name denotes a sequential file with fixed record length, program calculates number of records as numrecords = file_size / record_length Example counter_1 = numrecords('tbl1') See Also

name: Objects with sequence number equal zero
description: Objects with sequence number equal zero Objects with sequence number equal zero
text: Objects with sequence number equal zero Objects with sequence number equal zero Destination field, check box, radio button and combo box with sequence = 0 Processing of these objects is little bit specific regarding to those objects having sequence <> 0. The program code, supporting these objects, usually has the following form: ... before_instructions ... Destination = variable ... after_instructions ... - All the instructions before the 'Destination' statement will be ignored. - 'Destination' stores value to the variable. - The instructions after the 'Destination' denotes click actions. This piece of code will be activated each time when user clicks the object.

name: Objvalue
text: Objvalue Objvalue This method gets value of an object. Format Objvalue() Parameters - none Applied To Sourcefield, destinationfield, label. Note If object is not visible, its value is space. Example acc_1 = df_1.Objvalue() $a = label_1.Objvalue() See also Methods

name: ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE
description: This statement declares beginning of the event handler.
text: ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE This statement declares beginning of the event handler. Format ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE event_name([identifier]) Where - event_name - the name of the event: OnClickMenuItem and OnRClicked for context menu, or OnKeyPress for function keys. - identifier - the name of the identifier: function key or menu item identifier. Note An event handler must be ended by End_procedure statement. OnRClicked handler will be executed after pressing the right mouse button and before menu displaying. This handler is usually used to adjust menu items (setenable(0), etc). Examples !Event handler for F5 function key: ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE OnKeyPress(F5) form.display("F5 key") END_PROCEDURE !Event handler for Ctrl+F5 function key: ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE OnKeyPress(Ctrl+F5) form.display("Ctrl F5 key") END_PROCEDURE !Event handler for context menu right click: ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE OnRClicked() form.display("Rclick") END_PROCEDURE !Event handler for menu item CMITEM_1: ON_EVENT_PROCEDURE OnClickMenuItem(cmitem_1) form.display("cmitem_1 selected") END_PROCEDURE See also Function key handlers, Context menu item handlers

name: OPEN
description: This statement opens a file or establishes connection to a database.
text: OPEN OPEN This statement opens a file or establishes connection to a database. Format 1 OPEN file Format 2 OPEN /DATABASE connection_string Where - file - the name of the file from Define datasource dialog. - connection_string - ODBC connection string, alpha literal or alpha variable, in the form: odbc_datasource;UID=username;PWD=password. By default, a program automatically connects to a database at the very beginning of the program. But, if checkbox 'Open database manually' in 'Define datasource dialog' is checked, this command must be used to connect to the database. Note All the files, except these ones with /MANUAL qualifier, are opened at the very beginning of the program. Only /MANUAL files have to be opened by OPEN statement later-on in the program. Physical file name will be taken from the file definition in the DD. If you want to override this name, use /SPEC qualifier. But, if you want to supply physical file name at run-time, use an assignment statement with FILE_NAME keyword on the left-hand side, see example below. In reports and batch programs, files with WRITE and WRITE_NEW access mode, will be opened (created) just after program title, and before the program cyc...

name: Open Application
text: Open Application Open Application

name: Open Data Dictionary
description: Open Data Dictionary Open Data Dictionary
text: Open Data Dictionary Open Data Dictionary

name: Open
description: This method opens/creates a text file.
text: Open Open This method opens/creates a text file. Format Open (filename, openmode [,texttype]) Parameters - filename - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the text file. Logical names in filename are allowed. - openmode - mode, alpha variable or alpha literal. Possible values are: "new", "append", "read_only". New - Creates a new, empty file. Append - Opens an existing file and positions file pointer to the end. If file does not exist, it will be created. Read_only - Opens file for reading. The file must exist. - texttype - type of text, alpha variable or alpha literal. Possible values: "ansi", "utf-8", "utf-16", "utf-16big". Default is "ansi". Valid only if openmode="new". After file creation, byte-order mark will be inserted at the beginning of the file, if texttype is "utf-8", "utf-16" or "utf-16big". If openmode="append" or "read_only", this parameter is ignored. Ansi - Ordinary ANSI text, one character is one byte long. UTF-8 - Unicode text, a character is represented by multi-byte sequence. UTF-16 - Unicode text, each character is two bytes long. UTF-16BIG - Unicode text, each character is two bytes long, big endian. Byte-order marks are: for UTF-8 0xEF 0x...

name: Open
description: This method establishes connection to a FTP server. Must be invoked before any other Ftp method.
text: Open Open This method establishes connection to a FTP server. Must be invoked before any other Ftp method. Format Open(server, username, password [, mode [, port]]) Parameters - server - an alpha variable or alpha literal, required. Specifies IP address or name of the FTP server. - username - name of the user, alpha variable or alpha literal, required. - password - password, alpha variable or alpha literal, required. To specify blank password, enter empty string. - mode - mode, alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. Two values are allowed: "active" or "passive". If omitted, default value is "active". If you want to avoid client's firewall problems, use PASSIVE mode. - port - TCP/IP port to use on the server, integer, optional. Default value is 21. Return Value Alpha variable. If connecting was successful, value is blank. Otherwise, the variable holds an error message. Applied To Ftp object. Note Example $msg = Ftp.Open('myserver','user1','pwd1') if $msg <> SPACE Pause 'Error: ' | $msg else Ftp.Close() end_if Using FTP

name: Opentable
text: Opentable Opentable This method opens existing table or creates new one. The method enables table filling. Format Opentable(bookmark, numrows, numcolumns) Parameters - bookmark - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the bookmark in Word document. This bookmark is placeholder for new table. If the bookmark has the form "#1", "#2", etc., program opens existing table in the document, where "#1" denotes the first table, "#2" denotes second one, etc. - numrows - number of rows in new table. If we open an existing table, this parameter will be ignored. - numcolumns - number of columns in new table. If we open an existing table, this parameter will be ignored. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Bookmark or table not found Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Opentable("table1",100,5) $stat = Word.Opentable("#1",0,0) Using Word See also Closetable, Setcell

name: Open
description: This method creates an empty XML file and opens it.
text: Open Open This method creates an empty XML file and opens it. Format Open (xml_filename) Parameters - xml_filename - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the XML file. Logical names in filename are allowed. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Logical name not found -2 File already opened > 0 File input/output error Applied To Xml object. Note To release current XML file use Close method. Examples $stat = Xml.Open("c:\xml\test.xml") $stat = Xml.Open("xmldir:test.xml") $stat = Xml.Open($file_name) if $stat = 0 ... end_if See also Close Using XML

name: Options
examples: SELECT TOP 1 * FROM mytable WITH(UPDLOCK) WHERE ...
text: Options Options Save current work Defines autosave time interval: how often we want the painter to save the program. The form painter writes all the information about the program in recovery file named program_name.JOU. If the painter crashes, this file will be used for recovery when we try to modify this program later. If the painter successfully exits, this file is deleted. Store text labels as If we select ANSI, labels will be stored as ANSI text: one byte represents one character. Only characters from selected code page can be stored to the label. If we select Unicode, labels will be stored in UTF-8 character set: one character is represented by multiple bytes. In this case, we can use any character in a label. Code-page for literals, program title, etc Program generator is not an Unicode application itself. All the literals in program code are stored as ANSI text, according to the current code-page for the system. Number of tooltext lines If 'View/Show tooltip' option is checked, number of tooltip text lines is 10, by default. To see larger piece of the code, increase this value. Show field name / code By default, tooltip text shows field name only in objects like 'Destinat...

name: Pagesetup
text: Pagesetup Pagesetup This method changes some page layout parameters. Format Pagesetup (parameters) Parameters - parameters - parameters to be changed, alpha literal or alpha variable. A parameter is text in the form: keyword = value. If more keywords are used in the same function call, they should be separated by semicolon (;). The keywords, implemented so far are: ORIENTATION (changes page orientation), BOTTOMMARGIN (changes bottom margin), LEFTMARGIN (changes left margin), RIGHTMARGIN (changes right margin), TOPMARGIN (changes top margin), ZOOM (changes zoom), GRIDLINES (print with gridlines). ORIENTATION can have two values: xlLandscape or xlPortrait. BOTTOMMARGIN, LEFTMARGIN, RIGHTMARGIN, TOPMARGIN are decimal numbers representing centimeters. ZOOM is an integer. GRIDLINES values: 1 or Y (set gridlines); 0 or N (no gridlines). Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Invalid parameter Applied To Excel object. Examples $stat = Excel.Pagesetup('Orientation=xlLandscape') $stat = Excel.Pagesetup('Orientation=xlPortrait') $stat = Excel.Pagesetup('Leftmargin=2.0') !! margin=2cm $stat = Excel.Pagesetup('Leftmargin=1') !! margin=1cm $sta...

name: PAUSE
description: This statement pauses a program displaying a message box.
text: PAUSE PAUSE This statement pauses a program displaying a message box. Format PAUSE [conc_exp] Where - conc_exp - a literal, variable or concatenated expression, which will be displayed in the message box. Example Pause !! displays text "Pause" Pause "Text to be displayed" Pause "Today is " | today Pause "Ammount = " | acc_1 See also Messagebox

name: PDF417 barcode
description: PDF417 is a two-dimensional bar code consisted of multiple rows. The symbol can store up to 1100 bytes, or 1800 ASCII characters or 2700 digits. The maximum recommended amount of data is 800 characters. To encode letters from any code page, data should be i...
text: PDF417 barcode PDF417 barcode PDF417 is a two-dimensional bar code consisted of multiple rows. The symbol can store up to 1100 bytes, or 1800 ASCII characters or 2700 digits. The maximum recommended amount of data is 800 characters. To encode letters from any code page, data should be in UTF-8 format. Encoding mode Binary mode allows encoding all 256 bytes (0 to 255). Text mode allows encoding all printable ASCII characters and some control characters: TAB, line feed and carriage return. Error correction level Error correction algorithm allows the barcode to avoid various damages of the symbol. <auto> : error level will be calculated at run-time. 0 - 8 : nine error correction levels. Recommended minimum value is 2. Number of data columns <auto> : number of columns will be calculated at run-time. The barcode may have 1 to 30 data columns. Recommended maximum value is 20. Number of rows <auto> : number of rows will be calculated at run-time. The barcode may have 3 to 90 rows. Warning: the combination number_of_columns=<auto> and number_of_rows=<auto> is not recommended. Usually, one parameter is defined and the other one is calculated. Aspect ratio Aspect ratio is the ratio of the...

name: PERFORM
description: This statement executes local or global procedure.
text: PERFORM PERFORM This statement executes local or global procedure. Format PERFORM procedure Where - procedure - the name of the procedure which is called upon Example Perform myproc See also Procedure

name: Predefined colors
text: Predefined colors Predefined colors Predefined color is one of the following alpha literals: "BLACK", "BLUE", "GREEN", "CYAN", "RED", "MAGENTA", "YELLOW", "WHITE", "DARKBLUE", "DARKGREEN", "DARKCYAN", "DARKRED", "DARKMAGENTA", "DARKYELLOW", "DARKGRAY", "LIGHTGRAY", "MONEYGREEN", "SKYBLUE", "CREAM", "MEDIUMGRAY"

name: PREVIOUS
description: This function returns a field from previous record (table) buffer.
text: PREVIOUS PREVIOUS This function returns a field from previous record (table) buffer. Format PREVIOUS(field) Return Value Type Any type. Parameters - field - a field from main input table Note This function can be used in reports or batch programs only. Examples See Also

name: PROCEDURE
description: This statement declares a beginning of a procedure.
text: PROCEDURE PROCEDURE This statement declares a beginning of a procedure. Format PROCEDURE procedure ... statements ... END PROCEDURE Where - procedure - the name of the procedure - statements - IAF instructions which are used to define a procedure Note The procedures can be local and global ones. The local procedures can be used only in the program in which they are defined. A programmer defines these procedures by choosing Define/Local procedure(s), function(s) in main menu. The global procedures are visible from all the programs in the Data Dictionary and can be defined by Define/Global procedure(s), function(s). Example Procedure myproc init acc_2 read file1 key=1 if record file1 found acc_2 = 100,00 * acc_1 / (215,00 - fld1) end_if End_procedure See also Function, Perform

name: New program name dialog
description: New program name dialog New program name dialog
text: New program name dialog New program name dialog This dialog appears on the screen if we create new program. Enter program name and click OK. Program name is maximum 9 characters long. Allowed characters: letters A-Z, digits 0-9, underline (_) and dollar sign ($). The first character cannot be dollar sign. Error messages Invalid program name Program already exists Non-unique program name To return to the painter, press ESC or click Cancel.

name: Program properties
text: Program properties Program properties Program, Version, Date created / modified: information only. Caption This text appears in the program title, maximum 80 characters. Icon Small icon displayed in the upper-left corner of the program's frame. Background color Background color of the program title (reports, batch) or main form if an interactive program has no subforms. Visibility Initial visibility of the program: normal, minimized, maximized. Run mode Not implemented yet. Border style Resizing: frame can change its size at run-time. Thin: frame cannot change its size at run-time. Title bar When not checked, the program has no program title. Default: checked. Control box When not checked, the program has no caption buttons: "X", minimize, maximize. Default: checked. Maximize box When checked, the maximize button appears on the frame. Minimize box When checked, the minimize button appears on the frame. By clicking this button, system reduces window to the size of icon. Status bar When checked, the program will create status bar at the bottom of the frame. Reports always have status bar. To display a text in the status bar, use Display method, object name is STATUS_BAR. X positio...

name: Program protection
text: Program protection Program protection Each Iaf program can be protected from unauthorized access. There are two kinds of protection: by password and by username. Protection by password To access a program, user must enter a password. We define password prompt, ie. text which will be displayed on the screen, and password itself, ie. string that has to be entered. If the user enters wrong password, program stops its execution. Program protection dialog appears at program initialization phase before database connection. Protection by username We can allow up to 10 users to access the program. Only users logged under these usernames can activate this program.

name: Program type
text: Program type Program type In this dialog we define program type: report or batch. A report has program title (optional) and program cycle which generates report. A batch program has program title (optional) and program cycle which performs some database updates. If we select Report, report type need to be defined. Standard The report of this type has standard elements: report header, page header, subheaders, detail line and subtotal lines. The main characteristic is: one record from the input record stream generates one detail line. Label The main characteristic of labels: one record from the input record stream generates one label. Form The form looks like a pre-printed form. Maximum number of pages in the form is 20. The report painter has good set of commands to draw rectangles, lines, frames; to connect lines to other objects, etc. The main characteristic of forms: one record from the input record stream generates one form.

name: Progress bar
text: Progress bar Progress bar To insert new progress bar on the form, click button Progress in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Progress. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the top left corner of the progress bar. Drag to the right and bottom and release the button. New progress bar is placed on the form. To change progress bar properties double click on the progress bar -or- select the progress bar, right click to display context menu and choose Properties. How run-time engine processes the progress bar? There are two methods to display progress of a lengthy operation. The first, Setrange defines lower and upper limits of the range. After that, we repeatedly call Setpos to indicate current position.

name: Progressbar properties
text: Progressbar properties Progressbar properties Object name When the new progress bar is created, generator automatically assigns the name Progress_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed. Style Standard Smooth Gradient Show percent Start color End color Background style Background style should be set to normal. Background color This attribute is applied only if the background style is normal. Double click brings up a color definition dialog. Special effects Flat Border is standard line Raised Border with raised edge Sunken Border with sunken edge Etched Border is etched line Bump Border is bumped line Shadow Shadow below and to the right of the rectangle Border style Transparent Border is not seen Solid Border is standard line Dots Border is dotted line Dashes Border is dashed line Border color Is applied only if border style is not transparent. Double click brings up a color definition dialog. Border width Is applied on...

name: QR-barcode
text: QR-barcode QR barcode QR (Quick Response) is a two-dimensional bar code. A QR code consists of black modules (square dots) arranged in a square grid on a white background. Minimum barcode size is 21 x 21 modules, maximum one is 177 x 177 modules. The symbol can store up to 2953 bytes, or 4296 alphanumeric characters, or 7089 digits. To encode letters from any code page, data should be in UTF-8 format. Error correction level Error correction algorithm allows the barcode to avoid various damages of the symbol. Low - 7% of codewords can be restored Medium- 15% of codewords can be restored Quartile- 25% of codewords can be restored High - 30% of codewords can be restored Default value: Medium. Fixed dimensions - On a label or pre-printed form barcode dimensions should be fixed. - If data field length does not change considerably, barcode dimensions should be fixed as well. Example: data field contains 60 digits, error correction level = High. QR algorithm discovers that number of modules is 33. If module width is 1mm, total width of barcode in painter should be 33mm. Such barcode will be perfectly scanned. - If data field length changes significantly, barcode dimensions should be va...

name: QUARTER
description: This function returns a quarter of a year.
text: QUARTER QUARTER This function returns a quarter of a year. Format QUARTER (date) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date - date expression (date literal, date variable, date function). Note If date = zero_date or date = NULL, result is 0. Otherwise, result is a number in the range 1 to 4. If date between 1-JAN and 31-MAR, result is 1. If date between 1-APR and 30-JUN, result is 2. If date between 1-JUL and 30-SEP, result is 3. If date between 1-OCT and 31-DEC, result is 4. Examples counter_1 = quarter("1-jun-2007") !! result = 2 counter_1 = quarter("2007-06-01") !! result = 2 See Also

name: Radio button
text: Radio button Radio button We can consider radio button as a destination field which stores one of many values: 0 or 1 or 2 or 3, "A" or "B" or "C" or ... To insert new radio button on the form, click button Radio button in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Radio button. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. Definition dialog appears on the screen. One radio button stores one value into the destination field. Because of this, we always create radio buttons in a group, where each radio button stores different value into the destination field. The group of buttons is defined implicitly: all buttons inside a frame represent one group. This means that the frame around the group is required at paint-time. If we do not want to see the frame at run-time, we simply set the Visible attribute to No. Initially, maximum 10 radio buttons can be defined. Later on, if we want to add new radio button in the group, we can use Copy/Paste operation. All radio buttons in a group have successive sequence numbers. The object with smallest sequence number is the first element in the group. Frame text Frame label. Variable Destination variable. Value This value will be moved into des...

name: Radiobutton properties
text: Radiobutton properties Radiobutton properties Object name When the new radiobutton is created, generator automatically assigns the name Radiobutton_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Caption The text which appears on the button. If you want to define one letter as a prefix (mnemonic) character, precede it with ampersand (&). At run-time, that letter will be underlined and user can click that button by pressing Alt + letter. If you want to display single ampersand in the caption, enter two consecutive ampersands. Within one subform, all prefix characters must be different. Visible Determines whether the radiobutton is visible when the subform is first displayed. Enabled Determines whether the radiobutton is displayed as disabled when the subform is first displayed. When the radiobutton is disabled, mouse clicks are ignored. Ba...

name: RDB tables
description: Load table definitions from an ODBC datasource into the DD
text: RDB tables RDB tables Load table definitions from an ODBC datasource into the DD To use database tables in our programs, we have to load their definitions into the DD. Synchronize table definitions Compare table structures in the Data Dictionary to table structures in the database. Tables (synonyms, views, indexes, ...) in database are created by a Database administration utility. To use these tables, we load definitions into the DD. If someone later changes table structure, our DD definition will be out of date. This is the reason why we need synchronization from the time to time. Show Shows all the tables loaded into the Data Dictionary and their structure. Create SQL script from record definition Creates SQL script for table creation from record definition. Create SQL script from an ODBC datasource Creates SQL script for table creation from datasource. Create record definition from an ODBC datasource Creates record definition from a table in datasource. Manipulation Enables us to modify some fields attributes, to delete table definitions from the DD and enter description of a table. Show stored procedures / functions Shows stored procedures / functions in a datasource.

name: READ
description: This statement reads one record from a file or RDB table by a key.
text: READ READ This statement reads one record from a file or RDB table by a key. Format READ [/nohold][/trim][/return_lock] file_name [PART_]KEY[n] = key [WHERE cond_exp] Where - file_name - the name of the file (table) from datasource - key - concatenation. Number of segments in concatenation must be equal to the number of key segments - cond_exp - IAF conditional expression, which must have TRUE value Qualifiers /NOHOLD reads record without locking if indexed file is open for update (UPDATE). While record is locked other programs can read it, but can't erase or change it. Locked record is released after update or after the other record is being read. /TRIM reads record with partial key (only for alphanumeric segments of the key. Key size is determined by deleting all blanks at the end of the last segment. /RETURN_LOCK does not waits for releasing of locked record, but returns status immediately into the main program. Status can be checked with IF or CHECK (check record xxx not_locked). Note This statement is mainly intended to use with index-sequential files like Btrieve. If we use this statement to read a record from a RDB table, /NOHOLD and /TRIM can not be used. This statement ...

name: READ_ALL
description: This statement reads group of records from a file or RDB table in a cycle.
examples: SELECT_ALL * FROM tbl Where ... ORDER BY ...
text: READ_ALL READ_ALL This statement reads group of records from a file or RDB table in a cycle. Format 1 READ_ALL [/load_key][/nohold][/trim] file [[KEY[n] = key]] [WHERE cond_exp] ... END_READ Format 2 (for ISAM files only) READ_ALL [/load_key][/nohold][/trim] file [[STARTING_KEY[n] = key [ENDING_KEY[n] = key]] [WHERE cond_exp] ... END_READ Format 3 (for XML dataset) READ_ALL XML_DATASET(xml_variable[, node_name]) ... END_READ Where - file - the name of the file (table) from datasource dialog - key - concatenation of key segments - cond_exp - conditional expression. Expression after WHERE serves for record selection - xml_variable - alpha variable containing XML dataset. Variable type is CLOB, longchar. - node_name - alpha variable or alpha literal containing initial node name. If omitted, default value is 'Newdataset.Table'. Allowed qualifiers /LOAD_KEY prepare data-entry grid /NOHOLD read an ISAM record without locking /TRIM trim last key segment (ISAM files only) Notes Cycle ends with END_READ statement. In format 3, the first call moves internal pointer to the first occurrence of node defined by NODE_NAME. If the node does not exist, program leaves READ_ALL ... END_READ cycle ...

name: READ_EXTERNAL
text: READ_EXTERNAL READ_EXTERNAL This statement reads symbol. Format 1 READ_EXTERNAL /NAME: symbol_name field1 [, ..., fieldN] Format 2 READ_EXTERNAL /VARNAME: alpha_variable field1 [, ..., fieldN] Where - symbol_name - the name of a symbol. The first character in the name must not be underline (_). If the first character is a dollar sign ($), symbol will be read from the system registry. Otherwise, symbol will be read from symntt00.iaf file. If the symbol name contains spaces, an apostrophe or quotation mark has to be used as delimiter. - alpha_variable - variable containing symbol name. - field1, ..., fieldN - list of output fields: the fields where read value will be stored. Qualifiers /NAME defines symbol name. /VARNAME defines an alpha variable containing symbol name. Notes - Registry key is HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Sing\IAFW\RT\symbol_name. - Also, you can read any symbol from HKEY_CURRENT_USER branch. Symbol name has to be in the form HKEY_CURRENT_USER\sub_key1\sub_key2\...\symbol_name. - If symbol does not exist, output fields will be initialized. Examples ! Read SYM1 from Symntt00.iaf READ_EXTERNAL/NAME: sym1 counter_1 ! Read from Registry, key=hkey_current_user\software\s...

name: Read
description: This method reads next record from a text file.
text: Read Read This method reads next record from a text file. Format Read(field) Parameters - field - buffer for data, an alpha variable. If text file is utf-16 type, this variable must be a UTF-16 one. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: >=0 Record length. For ANSI and UTF-16 files, number of characters. For UTF-8, number of bytes. -1 End of file -2 Read error -3 File not opened Applied To File object. Example init $stat do_while $stat >= 0 $stat = File.Read($rec) if $stat >= 0 ... do something ... end_if end_do See also Write Working with files READ

name: READ_LE
description: This statement reads a record from an indexed file which key is less than or equal to given key.
syntax: READ_LE file KEY[n] = key DECREMENT field
text: READ_LE READ_LE This statement reads a record from an indexed file which key is less than or equal to given key. Format READ_LE file KEY[n] = key DECREMENT field Where - file - the name of the file from datasource. File has to be indexed one. - key - concatenation - field - one of the fields listed in key. It has to be numeric, date or time type. Because we currently deal with RDB tables and sequential files, this statement will not be further explained.

name: READ_NEXT
description: This statement reads next record from a sequential file.
examples: IF EOF = 0 !! if not end of file
text: READ_NEXT READ_NEXT This statement reads next record from a sequential file. Format READ_NEXT file Where - file - the name of the file from datasource dialog. File must be sequential. Note To read group of records, we use READ_ALL statement. READ_NEXT is used under very special circumstances. Example REWIND file1 READ_NEXT file1 IF EOF = 0 !! if not end of file ... do_something ... END_IF

name: Recapitulation record definition
description: Recapitulation record definition Recapitulation record definition
text: Recapitulation record definition Recapitulation record definition Using recapitulation file, we have possibility to print two reports in one program. The mechanism is the following: during main pass of the report, we write records into the recapitulation file. At the end of the main report, recapitulation report starts with the recapitulation file as the main one. Usually, we store some totals in the recapitulation record, but it is not necessary. Everything we store in this file will be printed in the recapitulation report. Maximum number of the fields in the recapitulation record is 50. Field attributes: name, type, size To specify a field, just enter its name in the first column. Field name is maximum 30 characters long starting with RECAP_, allowed characters are: A - Z, 0 - 9, _ (underline) and $ (a dollar sign). In the next column select appropriate field type, and in the third column enter its size. For alpha, UTF16, date and time field, the size is an unsigned number. Numeric field size is in the form nn[,dd] where is nn - total number of digits, maximum 18, dd - number of decimals. Example RECAP_1 Alpha 10 RECAP_2 Num 11,2 RECAP_3 Num 5 RECAP_4 Date 8 RECAP_SUM Num 15,2...

name: Record definition
text: Record definition Record definition A record consists of fields. Maximum number of the fields in a record is 255. To specify a field, just enter its name in Field name column. Field name is maximum 30 character long, allowed characters are: A - Z, 0 - 9, _ (underline) and $ (a dollar sign). Field name must not be the same as the record name. This name we will use later in our programs. In the next column (Global field name) select one of global (template) fields we already defined. The global field defines characteristics (type, size, data type, edit mask, etc) of 'Field name'. Note: If we enter name of a global field in Field name column, the second column will be skipped. If our field is a vector, enter number of occurences in the third column. Space (or value 1) in this column denotes an ordinary field. The last column (Key) applies to index-sequential files (Btrieve, etc.). Because we use database in our applications, this check box should be unchecked. This means that we create sequential files only. These files are usually used as working or temporary files. Each field has local attributes (unlike global attributes defined by global field): - Column header (used in default...

name: Records manipulation
text: Records manipulation Records manipulation The list shows already defined records. One line contains the following information: record name, number of fields, record size (in bytes), number of keys and date of last modification. Record name is maximum 30 characters long, the first character must be a letter. Allowed characters: English letters A - Z, digits 0 - 9, underline (_) and a dollar sign ($). Record name in one Data Dictionary must be unique. Modify To modify an existing record definition, select the record in the list and click Modify -or- Double click the record name in the list. The record definition dialog appears on the screen. New To create new record definition, click button New and go through the record definition dialog. Copy To copy a record definition to another record, select the record in the list and click Copy. This option is very useful if we have similar record definitions. Export description Export record definition to a text file (default file extension is RDS). Such text file could be copied to another PC. Import description Import record definition from a text file previously created by Export description. Analyze data If you have a text file (*.txt, ...

name: Rectangle
text: Rectangle Rectangle To insert new rectangle on the form, click button Rectangle in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Rectangle. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the top left corner of the rectangle. Drag to the right and bottom and release the button. New rectangle is placed on the form. Create a square Click button Rectangle in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Rectangle. Press Shift. Move the cursor to the desired position, click and drag to the bottom. Create rectangle with maximal width Click button Rectangle in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Rectangle. Press Ctrl. Move the cursor to the desired position, click and drag to the bottom. To change rectangle properties double click on the rectangle edge -or- select the rectangle, right click to display context menu and choose 'Properties'.

name: Rectangle properties
text: Rectangle properties Rectangle properties Object name When the new rectangle is created, generator automatically assigns the name Rectangle_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed. Rectangle style Standard Rectangle has standard corners Rounded corners Rectangle has rounded corners Background style Normal Background is filled with the background color Transparent Background remains unchanged Background color This attribute is applied only if the background style is normal. Special effects Flat Border is standard line Raised Border with raised edge Sunken Border with sunken edge Etched Border is etched line Bump Border is bumped line Shadow Shadow below and to the right of the rectangle Border style Transparent Border is not seen Solid Border is st...

name: Remove Application
text: Remove Application Remove Application

name: Removeline
text: Removeline Removeline This method removes one line from a grid. Format Removeline(line ) Parameters - line - line number, integer Applied To Grid. Note If you work with data-entry grid, and you enter data into a table or file, you have to delete appropriate record explicitly. Removeline method just visually removes the line from the grid and adjusts the internal key table. As you know, there is a built-in action 'line deleting'. To interactively remove line from a data-entry grid, the following procedure must be performed: click side heading to select whole line (only current line can be selected). Press 'Delete' key. Message box appears. Click 'Yes'. This built-in action deletes current record, throws out record key from the internal key table, removes current line from the grid and calls 'Line deleting' procedure. Examples Table_1.removeline(counter_1) Table_1.removeline(2) Table_1.removeline(current_line) See Also Addline, Enablelinedeleting, Methods

name: Rename
text: Rename Rename This method renames a file on the connected FTP server. Format Rename(oldname, newname) Parameters - oldname - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies current file name. - newname - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies new file name. Return Value Alpha variable. If connecting was successful, value is blank. Otherwise, the variable holds an error message. Applied To Ftp object. Note Examples $msg = Ftp.Rename('file1.txt','file2.txt') !!current directory if $msg <> SPACE Pause 'Error: ' | $msg end_if Using FTP

name: Repeatheader
text: Repeatheader Repeatheader This method repeats table heading on all pages. This is valid only for tables that are divided into several pages. Format Repeatheader (rep_flag) Parameters - rep_flag - numeric. Possible values: 0 or 1. Value 1 forces table heading printing on the next pages. Value 0 removes table heading from the next pages. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Word object. Note Table heading is the first row of the table. Examples ... $stat = Word.Opentable("#1",0,0) ! ... Fill opened table $stat = Word.Repeatheader(1) $stat = Word.Closetable() ... Using Word See also Opentable, Closetable

name: REPLACE
description: This function replaces all occurrences of one string by another string.
text: REPLACE REPLACE This function replaces all occurrences of one string by another string. Format REPLACE(alpha_exp, from_str, to_str [,collation]) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - input field, alpha. - from_str - string to be replaced. - to_str - string that replaces from_str. - collation - alpha literal denoting collation. This parameter is mainly used to specify case-insensitive option. Note Output string could be truncated if the output buffer is too small. Examples $outstr = REPLACE($INPSTR,'xx','xyz') !! replace xx by xyz If the $INPSTR contains 'abcxxyy', the result will be 'abcxyzyy' $outstr = replace($inpstr,chr(9),' ') !! replace TABs by spaces $outstr = replace($inpstr,alltrim($from),alltrim($to)) ! FROM_STR is case-insensitive: abc, ABC, Abc, etc. $outstr = replace($inpstr,'abc','123','english_CI') See Also String Functions

name: Report parameters
text: Report parameters Report parameters Paper dimensions Page width Page height Page overflow is the position where the skip to new page occurs. Based on these dimensions (width, height) report painter creates work area: 1 mm = 3 pixels. Orientation Portrait Landscape This parameter defines work area appearance. Drawing area Default is 1 page. If a report has many subheaders and subtotals which cannot be fitted into one page, increase working area to two pages. Default orientation at run-time Portrait Landscape This parameter defines default orientation in Print dialog at run-time. Orientation can change at run-time If this option is not checked, the paper orientation cannot be changed at run-time. Draw in physical units (mm) at run-time This option is available for the form report type only. Number of copies Numeric literal or numeric variable. If blank, 1 is assumed. This parameters is valid only if we choose direct printing in Print dialog (click OK). Form name This parameter is mainly used for nonstandard paper size. Run-time engine try to sets paper size to "form name" if the default printer supports such paper. If this parameter is blank, default paper will be determined accor...

name: Program name
text: Program name Program name This is the first dialog of program generator. In this dialog, we choose program name and an operation which will be performed (modify, delete, rename, ...). Application This combobox shows all applications in Data Dictionary. Current application is displayed in the edit part of the combobox. If DD has one application only, combobox is disabled, else current application can be changed. Program name If we click list of programs, this edit box displays selected program. After that, we can click any of enabled buttons (modify, delete, generate, ...). If we enter program name (with possible wildcards) and press Enter, then this string becomes the filter for the list. Allowed wildcards characters are: * and ?. Examples: * All programs A* Program names starting with letter A *A Program names ending with letter A *PRG* Program names containing PRG PRG? Program names exactly four characters long, starting with PRG List List on the right-side of the dialog shows all interactive programs in this application. Naturally, when we enter this generator for the first time, this list will be empty. Program type options Defines type of programs to be displayed in the lis...

name: RESET
description: This statement closes, deletes, re-creates and opens empty file. Also, it deletes all records from an RDB table.
text: RESET RESET This statement closes, deletes, re-creates and opens empty file. Also, it deletes all records from an RDB table. Format RESET file Where - file - the name of the file (table) from datasource dialog. The access mode must be one of WRITE, WRITE_NEW, UPDATE. Note RESET rdb_table_name is the same as EXECSQL DELETE FROM rdb_table_name Example RESET file1 See also OPEN File.Reset

name: Reset
description: This method truncates the file, i.e. sets file size to zero.
text: Reset Reset This method truncates the file, i.e. sets file size to zero. Format Reset() Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 File not opened Applied To File object. Example $stat = File.Reset() See also RESET Working with files

name: REWIND
description: This statement positions on a beginning of a file.
text: REWIND REWIND This statement positions on a beginning of a file. Format REWIND file Where - file - the name of the file (table) from datasource dialog. Example REWIND file1

name: Rewind
text: Rewind Rewind This method repositions file pointer. Format Rewind([seek_flag]) Parameter - seek_flag - new value of file pointer, alpha literal or alpha variable. Possible values: "begin", "end". Default value is "begin". Begin - positions file pointer to the beginning of the file. End - positions file pointer to the end of the file. Example $stat = File.Rewind() $stat = File.Rewind('begin') $stat = File.Rewind('end') See also Working with text files

name: RGB
description: This function returns an integer representing RGB color value.
text: RGB RGB This function returns an integer representing RGB color value. Format RGB(red,green,blue) Parameters - red - red component of the color, an integer in the range 0 to 255 - green - green component of the color, an integer in the range 0 to 255 - blue - blue component of the color, an integer in the range 0 to 255 Return Value Type Integer. Note This function can be used in SETBACKCOLOR and SETTEXTCOLOR methods. Examples obj_1.settextcolor( rgb(0,0,255) ) table_1.setbackcolor( *, 1, rgb(0,0,0) ) obj1.settextcolor("RED") !!is the same as obj1.settextcolor(RGB(255,0,0))

name: RGB color values
text: RGB color values RGB color values RGB color value can be calculated as: ------------------------------------------------------------------ color_value = 65536 * Blue_component + 256 * Green_component + Red_component ------------------------------------------------------------------ where Red, Green, Blue components lays in the range 0 to 255. Examples $red_value = 255 $blue_value = 65536 * 255 $light_gray = 65536 * 192 + 256 * 192 + 192

name: RIGHT
description: This function extracts a substring beginning with the rightmost character in alpha_exp.
text: RIGHT RIGHT This function extracts a substring beginning with the rightmost character in alpha_exp. Format RIGHT(alpha_exp, count) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - input field, alpha - count - number of characters to extract, numeric. Note To extract the characters from the actual string, use RIGHT(RTRIM(alpha_exp), count). Examples $outstr = right('ABCD ',2) !! result is 'D ' $outstr = right(rtrim('ABCD '),2) !! result is 'CD' See Also LEFT, MID, RTRIM, String functions

name: ROLLBACK
text: ROLLBACK ROLLBACK This statement cancels transaction. Format ROLLBACK Note If program uses classic files only (Btrieve, RMS, seq), this instruction will be ignored. Example ROLLBACK See also COMMIT

name: ROUND
description: This function rounds a numeric value.
text: ROUND ROUND This function rounds a numeric value. Format ROUND(numeric_exp, numdec) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - numeric_exp - numeric value to be rounded. - numdec - number of resulting decimals, numeric. - If numdec is greater than zero, then it represents number of decimals. - If numdec is 0, input number will be rounded to integer. - if numdec is less than zero, then the input number is rounded to the left of the decimal point. Note Numdec is defined as follows: Numeric value 1 8 2, 5 6 Numdec -3 -2 -1 0 1 2 Algorithm: If the digit at round position has value less than 5, it will be rounded to 0. All digits right from this position will be set to 0. All digits left from this position stay unchanged. If the digit at round position has value greater or equal 5, it will be rounded to 0, but first digit left from this position will be incremented by 1. Examples acc_31 = 157,567 $C = ROUND(acc_31,2) !! result is 157,57 $C = ROUND(acc_31,1) !! result is 157,6 $C = ROUND(acc_31,0) !! result is 158 $C = ROUND(acc_31,-2) !! result is 200 acc_31 = 157,127 $C = ROUND(acc_31,2) !! result is 157,13 $C = ROUND(acc_31,1) !! result is 157,1 $C = ROUND(acc_31,0) !! result is 157 $...

name: RPAD
description: This function returns the alpha_exp, right-padded with another alpha_exp.
text: RPAD RPAD This function returns the alpha_exp, right-padded with another alpha_exp. Format RPAD(alpha_exp, padstr) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - field to be right-padded, alpha. - padstr - pad character or pad string, alpha. Note All white space characters at the end of alpha_exp will be replaced by padstr. Examples $Outstr = RPAD($INPSTR,'%') $Outstr = RPAD($INPSTR,'abc') $Outstr = RPAD($Outstr,'%') $Outstr = RPAD($Outstr,$PAD) ! Fill a field by a character init $Outstr $Outstr = rpad($Outstr,'0') See Also String Functions LPAD

name: RT_REPLACE
description: This function is used to compose a SQL statement at run-time.
examples: Execsql Insert into tbl values ('abc', rt_replace($a80))
text: RT_REPLACE RT_REPLACE This function is used to compose a SQL statement at run-time. Format RT_REPLACE(alpha_variable) Return Value Type None. Parameters - alpha_variable - value of this variable will be inserted into a SQL statement at run-time. The value replaces RT_REPLACE(alpha_variable). Examples 1/ Execsql Insert into tbl values ('abc', rt_replace($a80)) If $a80 contains text 'value xyz', the above statement at run-time will be Insert into tbl values ('abc', 'value xyz') The same result can be reached by using parameter: Execsql Insert into tbl values ('abc', :$a80) but there are situations where parameters cannot be used. Notes: If $a80 contains text value xyz, the run-time statement will be Insert into tbl values ('abc', value xyz) !!invalid If $a80 contains text "value xyz", the run-time statement will be Insert into tbl values ('abc', "value xyz") !!invalid 2/ Execsql Insert into tbl (col1, col2) Select rt_replace($a80), fld2 from tbl2 In above example, we can not use parameter, because SQL parser does not allow parameter in select_list. At run-time, this statement will be Insert into tbl (col1, col2) Select 'value xyz', fld2 from tbl2

name: RTRIM
description: This function removes white space from the end of alpha_exp.
text: RTRIM RTRIM This function removes white space from the end of alpha_exp. Format RTRIM(alpha_exp) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - alpha field. Examples $res = RTRIM($inp) See Also ALLTRIM, LTRIM String functions

name: Runmacro
text: Runmacro Runmacro This method runs a MS Word macro and, optionally, stores VBA (Visual Basic for Applications) code to Word document. Format Runmacro(macro_name [,VBA_filename][,arg1]) Parameters - macro_name - name of a macro, an alpha variable or alpha literal. The macro must be stored in file IAFMACRO.DOT. If macro_name is prefixed by DOCUMENT:, a macro from current document will be run. Prefixed form is "DOCUMENT:macro_name". - VBA_filename - name of text file containing VBA procedure(s), alpha variable or alpha literal. - arg1 - value of the first argument, alpha variable or alpha literal. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Macro does not exist Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Runmacro('macro1') !! macro from IAFDIR:IAFMACRO.DOT $stat = Word.Runmacro('DOCUMENT:macro1') !! macro from current document $macro = 'my_macro' $stat = Word.Runmacro($macro) ! Run VBA procedure declared as 'Sub Mysub(arg1 as String)' $stat = Word.Runmacro('Mysub',,'abcd') ! Store VBA code from c:\temp\vbacode.txt into open document ! and, after that, execute myproc1. $stat = Word.Runmacro('myproc1','c:\temp\vbacode.txt') Using Word See ...

name: Runmacro
text: Runmacro Runmacro This method runs an Excel macro, procedure or function and, optionally, stores VBA (Visual Basic for Applications) code to Excel workbook. Format Runmacro(module_name [,VBA_filename]) Parameters - module_name - name of a macro, procedure or function, an alpha variable or alpha literal. - VBA_filename - name of text file containing VBA procedure(s), alpha variable or alpha literal. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Excel document not opened -2 Procedure/macro does not exist -3 VBA file does not exist -4 Logical name not found -5 Other errors Applied To Excel object. Note If VBA_filename parameter exists, the order of execution is - Store VBA file into open workbook, - Run module 'module_name'. Examples $stat = Excel.Open('c:\exc\test.xls') $stat = Excel.Runmacro('macro1') !!macro1 already exists in test.xls ! Open CSV file $stat = Excel.Open('c:\exc\test.csv') ! Store VBA code from c:\temp\vbacode.txt into open workbook ! and, after that, execute myproc1. $stat = Excel.Runmacro('myproc1','c:\temp\vbacode.txt') ! Save as .XLS $stat = Excel.Run('save', 'c:\exc\newtest.XLS') Using Excel See also Runmacro Word

name: Run-time symbols
examples: ODBC datasource used to connect to a database.
text: Run-time symbols Run-time symbols Using symbols, the behavior of Iaf programs can be changed at run-time. All symbols are placed in the system Registry, under the key HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Sing\Iafw\Rt as string values. Symbols can be created manually using REGEDIT program. Also, IAFUTIL can be used. To define a run-time symbol in Iaf program, we use WRITE_EXTERNAL statement where symbol name starts with a dollar sign: WRITE_EXTERNAL/NAME: $symbol_name symbol_value Examples: Write_external/name: $iafmenun "mymenu.mnu" Write_external/name: $iafworkd "c:\appl" Write_external/name: $odbc_trace "1" ACTIVECOLOR This symbol specifies border color of active (current) field. If the symbol does not exist, border color is red. If the symbol has value -1 (minus 1), there is no active border coloring.
