In other cases, symbol has the RGB format: RED,GREEN,BLUE where red,green,blue components are in the range 0 - 255. Examples 255,0,0 red 0,255,0 green 0,0,255 blue 0,0,0 black 255,255,255 white -1 no coloring ESC_ALLOWED Normally, a user can use the ESC key to exit current program. If ESC_ALLOWED is defined and has the value 0 (zero), ESC can't be used. DEFAULT_FONT Font used in "List" an...

name: SAVE_FILE
description: This function stores a long field, string, (LONGVARCHAR or LONGVARBINARY, CLOB, BLOB) into a file. Result is an integer containing status.
text: SAVE_FILE SAVE_FILE This function stores a long field, string, (LONGVARCHAR or LONGVARBINARY, CLOB, BLOB) into a file. Result is an integer containing status. Format SAVE_FILE(field,file_name) Return Value Type Numeric. Possible values: 0 successful -1 The file name is too long 3 No such directory > 0 other file errors Parameters - field - long field (ANSI or UTF-16). - file_name - output file name, logical names allowed. Note Example ! $status is defined as numeric, without decimals. ! This function stores PCT field into c:\test\a.bmp if tbl1.pct is not NULL $status = SAVE_FILE(tbl1.pct,"c:\test\a.bmp") if $status <> 0 pause "Save_file error=" | $status end_if See Also Load_file

name: Search Data Dictionary
description: Search Data Dictionary Search Data Dictionary
text: Search Data Dictionary Search Data Dictionary We use this dialog to search for string(s) in programs, global procedures/functions and menus. Search for string(s) Enter the text string(s) you want to search for. String separator is COMMA, for example string1, string2, string3 If a particular string contains spaces, quotation marks have to be used: "string1 with spaces", string2, "string3 xxx" If the string contains leading or trailing spaces, use quotation marks: " leading spaces", "trailing spaces " If the string contains a TAB character (tabulator), use escape character in the form \t (backslash lower-t): "\tleading_tab" Boolean expression OR Search is successful if any of entered strings exists. AND Search is successful if all the strings exist. NOT Search is successful if entered string does not exist. In this case, only one string can be entered. AND NOT Search is successful if the first string exists and the second string does not exist. In this case, exactly two strings have to be entered. Match case If this check box is not selected, all strings are converted to uppercase before comparison. Search in Select appropriate program type, procedures/functions or menus. Note To ...

name: Search global procedures / functions
description: Search global procedures / functions Search global procedures / functions
text: Search global procedures / functions Search global procedures / functions In this dialog you can enter search criteria. Procedure/function name Enter procedure/function name, wildcards characters (* and ?) allowed. Examples: * All procedures A* Procedure names starting with letter A *A Procedure names ending with letter A *PRC* Procedure names containing PRC PRC? Procedure names exactly four characters long, starting with PRC User Enter * for all users or a user name. Search string Enter the text which will be searched for. Look in Select one of options: procedures and functions, procedures only, functions only. Match whole word only A word is a text preceded and followed by space, tab or a punctuation character. Match case When selected, searching is case sensitive.

name: SECOND
description: This function returns seconds of a time.
text: SECOND SECOND This function returns seconds of a time. Format SECOND(time_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - time_exp - Note Result is a number in the range 0 to 59. Examples See Also Time Functions

name: SELECT
description: This statement retrieves one record (row) from a RDB table.
text: SELECT SELECT This statement retrieves one record (row) from a RDB table. Format SELECT select_statement Example SELECT * FROM tbl1 Where PKID=1 See Also Select statement

name: SELECT_ALL
description: This statement retrieves many records (rows) from a RDB table(s) in a cycle. The cycle ends with END_SELECT.
examples: SELECT_ALL * FROM tbl1 Where PKID < 100 order by PKID
SELECT_ALL * FROM tbl1 WHERE PKID < :$PARAM order by 1,2
text: SELECT_ALL SELECT_ALL This statement retrieves many records (rows) from a RDB table(s) in a cycle. The cycle ends with END_SELECT. Format SELECT_ALL select_statement Note If we use this statement in 'Table starting actions' to prepare internal tables in a data-entry grid, we must use /LOAD_KEY qualifier. In this case, all the fields of the primary key must be listed in select_list. To correctly order records in the grid, ORDER BY clause must be used. SELECT_ALL/LOAD_KEY select_statement ... END_SELECT The same goal can be reached by READ_ALL/LOAD_KEY, but SELECT_ALL enables the usage of complex selection criteria, joins, etc. If we use many tables in FROM clause, the first table is the main one. See also READ_ALL. Example SELECT_ALL * FROM tbl1 Where PKID < 100 order by PKID ... END_SELECT SELECT_ALL * FROM tbl1 WHERE PKID < :$PARAM order by 1,2 ... END_SELECT See Also Select statement

name: Select datasource
text: Select datasource Select datasource List List on the left-side of the dialog shows all ODBC datasources defined on your PC, including User DSN and System DSN. Database Database shows default database type (MS SQL Server, Oracle, DB2, ...) used in your application. DB2 schema If we select DB2 database, a schema for tables loading can be entered. If this parameter is empty, the default schema defined by login username will be used. Include all users Ignore login username or DB2 schema and store all the tables from the database into the list. In this case, the system tables will be included too. Include synonyms Show synonyms in the list. Include views Show views in the list. Create trace file If this check-box is checked, a trace file will be created during loading phase. This file can be used for debugging purposes only. File name is DDLOADTRAC.LOG and resides on default directory. This check-box is visible in "Load table definitions" dialog in Data Dictionary utility.

name: Selectitem
text: Selectitem Selectitem This method selects one line in a display-only grid. Format Selectitem (line) Parameters - line - line number, integer. The first line has number 1, etc. There are two special values: 0 - clears selection, -1 - selects all lines. Applied To Grid. Note Examples Table_1.Selectitem(counter_1) Table_1.Selectitem(1) !!Select line 1 Table_1.Selectitem(0) !!Clear current selection Table_1.Selectitem(-1) !!Select all lines See Also Methods

name: SELECT_START
description: This statement retrieves many records (rows) from a RDB table(s) in a cycle. The statement is used to fill a display-only grid. The cycle starts with this statement in 'Table_starting' actions and ends implicitly when the input stream is exshausted. After t...
examples: SELECT_START * FROM tbl1 Where PKID < 100 order by PKID
text: SELECT_START SELECT_START This statement retrieves many records (rows) from a RDB table(s) in a cycle. The statement is used to fill a display-only grid. The cycle starts with this statement in 'Table_starting' actions and ends implicitly when the input stream is exshausted. After that, program transfers control to the 'Table_ending' actions. Format SELECT_START select_statement Note The block diagram is the following: 'Table_starting' actions: SELECT_START prepares input stream. cycle: Program fetches next record from the stream. If not end_of_stream if not termination_condition if not suppressline_condition Display next line in the grid. Perform 'line_ending' actions. end_if Goto cycle end_if Perform 'table_ending' actions. ... Termination_condition and suppressline_conditions are grid attributes. Example SELECT_START * FROM tbl1 Where PKID < 100 order by PKID See Also Select statement

name: Select statement
text: Select statement Select statement This is a classic SQL statement used to retrieve rows from a RDB table: [DISTINCT] select_list FROM table(s) [WHERE search_condition] [GROUP BY group_list] [HAVING search_condition] [ORDER BY orderby_list] [UNION SELECT ...] Select_list specifies columns to be returned by the query. The list consists of fields, formulas and functions. If a function or formula has been used, the destination in the form 'INTO :parameter' must be specified. Select statement without parameters This is the simplest form of the select statement. SELECT * FROM tbl SELECT fld1,fld2 FROM tbl SELECT tbl.* FROM tbl SELECT * FROM tbl ORDER BY fld1 SELECT fld1,fld2 FROM tbl GROUP BY fld1,fld2 ORDER BY fld1,fld2 SELECT * FROM tbl WHERE fld1 > 100 ORDER BY fld1 SELECT fld1 AS alias1,fld2 AS alias2 FROM tbl Select statement with parameters A parameter is a variable which does not belong to the table(s), participating in the select statement. The parameter marker is a colon sign in front of the variable. At run-time, these parameters will be replaced by their values. SELECT * FROM tbl WHERE fld1 > :acc_1 In above statement, :acc_1 is a parameter, because acc_1 is an internal fie...

name: Setalignment
text: Setalignment Setalignment This method sets text alignment. Format 1 Setalignment (alignment) Sets alignment of an object Format 2 Setalignment (line, column, alignment) Sets alignment of a grid cell Parameters - alignment - alpha variable, alignment in the form HORIZONTAL = {CENTER | LEFT | RIGHT} or VERTICAL = {CENTER | TOP | BOTTOM} HORIZONTAL and VERTICAL can be abbreviated. - line - grid line, numeric value. The lines are counted from 1.To specify current line, we can use the internal variable CURRENT_LINE or star (*). - column - grid column, numeric value. The columns are counted from 1. Notes To align entire grid line, use ENTIRE_LINE constant in the form table_1.Setalignment(line,ENTIRE_LINE,alignment) To align entire grid column, use ENTIRE_COLUMN constant in the form table_1.Setalignment(ENTIRE_COLUMN,column,alignment) Examples label_1.Setalignment('HOR=LEFT') !! horiz. alignment=left label_1.Setalignment('HOR=CENTER') !! horiz. alignment=center label_1.Setalignment('HOR=RIGHT') !! horiz. alignment=right label_1.Setalignment('VERT=CENTER') !! vert. alignment=center table_1.Setalignment(ENTIRE_COLUMN,1,'HOR=RIGHT') See also Excel.Setalignment

name: Setbackcolor
text: Setbackcolor Setbackcolor This method sets the background color of an object or grid cell. Format 1 Setbackcolor (color) Sets background color of an object Format 2 Setbackcolor (line,column,color) Sets background color of a grid cell Parameters - color - a numeric variable with valid RGB color value or one of the predefined colors or RGB function. - line - grid line, numeric value. The lines are counted from 1.To specify current line, we can use the internal variable CURRENT_LINE or star (*). - column - grid column, numeric value. The columns are counted from 1. Notes To color entire grid line, use ENTIRE_LINE constant in the form table_1.Setbackcolor(line,ENTIRE_LINE,color) To color entire grid column, use ENTIRE_COLUMN constant in the form table_1.Setbackcolor(ENTIRE_COLUMN,column,color) Examples label_1.Setbackcolor('black') label_1.Setbackcolor('red') label_1.setbackcolor(RGB(255,0,0)) !! same as above label_1.Setbackcolor(255) label_1.Setbackcolor(256*128 + 255) table_1.setbackcolor(counter_1,2,'white') table_1.setbackcolor(current_line,2,'white') table_1.setbackcolor(*,2,'white') !! same as above table_1.setbackcolor(*,ENTIRE_LINE,'white') !!entire current line table_1.se...

name: Setblinking
text: Setblinking Setblinking This method starts / stops blinking. Format Setblinking (interval, bkColor, txColor) Parameters - interval - blink interval in milliseconds, integer. Allowed values are between 0 and 2000. Value 0 stops blinking. If value is out of these limits, default is 500. - bkColor - background color, a numeric variable with valid RGB color value or one of the predefined colors or RGB function. Optional. - txColor - text color, a numeric variable with valid RGB color value or one of the predefined colors or RGB function. Optional. Applied To Text label. Note Examples obj.Setblinking(500,'black','white') obj.Setblinking(500,rgb(255,0,0),rgb(255,255,255)) obj.Setblinking(500,rgb(255,0,0)) !! blink background only obj.Setblinking(500,,rgb(255,255,255)) !! blink text only obj.Setblinking(0) !! stops blinking See also Methods

name: Setbookmark
text: Setbookmark Setbookmark This method transfers a value from program to an empty bookmark in Word document. Format Setbookmark(bookmark, variable [,code_page]) Parameters - bookmark - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the bookmark in Word document. - variable - value which will be written into the bookmark. If the variable is not an alphanumeric one, default editing will be applied. - codepage - this parameter specifies variable's code page. The parameter is very important because MS Office tools (Excel, Word, ...) use UNICODE to represent text data, while text data in programs use, in most cases, single-byte representation. Default is code page set by previous Setcodepage method. If Setcodepage was not used, default is ANSI code page. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Bookmark not found Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Setbookmark("date_from",today) $stat = Word.Setbookmark("date_to",tomorrow) $stat = Word.Setbookmark("amount",edit(acc_1,"zzz.zz9,99")) $stat = Word.Setbookmark("name",$name,1250) Using Word

name: Setbookmarktext
text: Setbookmarktext Setbookmarktext This method sets bookmark text (reports only). Format Setbookmarktext (text) Parameters - text - concatenated expression or alpha literal. Applied To Sourcefield in a report. Note By default, run-time engine stores current field's value into bookmark. This method enables us to change the default behavior. Report painter procedure: - Denote one field as a bookmark in report painter, let's say SRC_1. - Apply this method to the field: SRC_1.setbookmarktext('New mark' | field). Examples src_1.Setbookmarktext('New bookmark text') src_1.Setbookmarktext('Bookmark text '|field1) src_1.Setbookmarktext(Compress('Bookmark text',field1)) See Methods

name: Setbordercolor
text: Setbordercolor Setbordercolor This method sets the border color of an object. Format Setbordercolor (color) Parameters - color - a numeric variable with valid RGB color value or one of the predefined colors or RGB function. Notes Examples label_1.Setbordercolor("black") label_1.Setbordercolor("red") label_1.Setbordercolor(RGB(255,0,0)) !! same as above label_1.Setbordercolor(255) label_1.Setbordercolor(256*128 + 255) rectangle_1.Setbordercolor("black") See also Setbackcolor, Settextcolor, Excel.Setbordercolor Methods

name: Setcellfocus
text: Setcellfocus Setcellfocus This method sets the focus to a grid cell. Format Setcellfocus(line, column) Parameters - line - grid line, numeric value. The lines are counted from 1.To specify current line, we can use the internal variable CURRENT_LINE or star (*). - column - grid column, numeric value. The columns are counted from 1. Applied To Grid. Notes - If the grid is invisible or disabled, SetCellfocus will not be executed. - Compared to traditional programming, SetCellfocus corresponds to GOTO statement. Statements after SetCellfocus will not be executed. Example table_1.SetCellFocus(current_line,1) See also Methods

name: Setcellfont
text: Setcellfont Setcellfont This method changes font attributes of an Excel cell. Format 1 Setcellfont (range, [font_name], [font_size], [bold], [italic], [underline]) Format 2 Setcellfont (line, column, [font_name], [font_size], [bold], [italic], [underline]) Parameters - range - range of cells in the form STARTING_CELL:ENDING_CELL, alpha variable. ENDING_CELL can be omitted. - line - row, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1. - column - column, numeric value, integer. The first column is 1. Instead of column number, an alpha literal containing column header can be used: 'A', 'B', ..., 'AA', 'AB', ... - font_name - alpha literal or variable, font name: "Arial", "Times New Roman", .... Optional parameter. - font_size - an integer, font size (8 or 10 or 12 or ...). Optional parameter. - bold - intensity, > 400 bold, <= 400 normal. Optional parameter. - italic - integer, value 1 = italic, value 0 = normal. Optional parameter. - underline - integer, value 1 = underline, value 0 = normal. Optional parameter. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -3 Error Applied To Excel object. Examples $stat = Excel.Setcellfont(1,1,'Arial') $stat = ...

name: Setcodepage
text: Setcodepage Setcodepage This method sets function popuplink(url) { window.open(url, "hspopup", "top=10, left=10, width=750 ,height=750,resizable,scrollbars"); } code page of an object. Format Setcodepage(codepage) Parameters - codepage - code page Applied To All objects. Example See also Methods

name: Setcolumnwidth
text: Setcolumnwidth Setcolumnwidth This method sets column width in a grid. Format Setcolumnwidth (column, width) Parameters - column - column number, integer. The columns are counted from 1. - width - column width in pixels, integer. If width = -1, autosize operation will be applied, i.e. column will be automatically sized to fit text. Applied To Grid. Examples Table_1.Setcolumnwidth(1,20) Table_1.Setcolumnwidth(1,-1) !! autosize column 1 See also Setnumbercolumns, Excel.Setcolumnwidth

name: Setcurrentdirectory
text: Setcurrentdirectory Setcurrentdirectory This method changes the directory on the server. Format Setcurrentdirectory(dirname) Parameters - dirname - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies new directory. Return Value Alpha variable. If connecting was successful, value is blank. Otherwise, the variable holds an error message. Applied To Ftp object. Note Examples $msg = Ftp.Setcurrentdirectory('/dir1/dir2') if $msg <> SPACE Pause 'Error: ' | $msg end_if Using FTP

name: Setcurrentline
text: Setcurrentline Setcurrentline This method sets current line to line. Format Setcurrentline(line) Parameters - line - numeric variable or numeric literal. Constant LAST_LINE denotes last grid line. Applied To Display-only grid. Note This method is used usually in 'Table ending actions', when all the grid lines are displayed. Example Table_1.Setcurrentline(10) Table_1.Setcurrentline(LAST_LINE) See also Methods

name: Setcurrentpage
text: Setcurrentpage Setcurrentpage This method sets current page in tab control. Format Setcurrentpage() Parameters None. Applied To Tab control. Note This method just switches between two tab pages, displaying previously displayed fields. If you want to execute program code in new selected page, Init method should be used. Examples Tab_1.tabPage1.Setcurrentpage() $loc = 'tab_1.tabpage_2' $loc.Setcurrentpage() ! Change page, init fields and execute code Tab_1.tabPage_2.Setcurrentpage() Tab_1.tabPage_2.Init() Tab_1.Setfocus() See also Methods

name: Setenable
text: Setenable Setenable This method enables or disables mouse and keyboard input. Format Setenable(value) Parameters - value - a numeric variable or numeric literal. value = 0, object will be disabled, value <> 0, object will be enabled. Applied To Combobox, Checkbox, Radiobutton, Pushbutton, Bitmapbutton, Grid, Form, Destinationfield, Sourcefield. Notes FORM.Setenable(0) disables Close button. SOURCEFIELD.Setenable(0) just changes text color to dark gray. Using SETENABLE we can disable a button (standard or hyperlink) in the grid. Because this method does not use line and column arguments, we can disable a button in the current line only. The best place for this action is 'Line ending actions': if condition button_grid.setenable(0) end_if If we use this method to disable a hyperlink in the grid, then text gets dark gray color and hand cursor does not appear when the cursor hovers over the cell. Example Button_1.Setenable(0) !! Disables input Button_1.Setenable(1) !! Enables input See also Methods Isenabled

name: Setfocus
text: Setfocus Setfocus This method sets the focus to the specified object. Format Setfocus() Parameters - none Applied To Destination field, push button, bitmap button, combo box, check box, radio button, grid, tree, data selector. Notes - If the object is invisible or disabled, Setfocus will not be executed. - Compared to traditional programming, Setfocus corresponds to GOTO statement. Statements after Setfocus will not be executed. Example table_1.SetFocus() See also Methods

name: Setfont
syntax: Setfont(line, column, font_name, font_size, bold, italic, underline)
text: Setfont Setfont This method changes font attributes of an object. Format 1 Setfont(font_name, font_size, bold, italic, underline) Format 2 Setfont(line, column, font_name, font_size, bold, italic, underline) Parameters - font_name - alpha literal or variable, font name: "Arial", "Times New Roman", .... - font_size - an integer, font size (8 or 10 or 12 or ...). - bold - intensity, an integer from 0 to 1000 (normal intensity is 400, bold 700, extra bold 900). - italic - integer, value 1 = italic, value 0 = normal. - underline - integer, value 1 = underline, value 0 = normal. - line - grid line, numeric value. The lines are counted from 1.To specify current line, we can use the internal variable CURRENT_LINE or star (*). - column - grid column, numeric value. The columns are counted from 1. If this parameter has the value -1, the entire line will be changed. Applied To All the objects displaying text. Notes - Unused parameters can be omitted. - The font of a grid cell cannot be changed. - Use Format 2 to change font of a grid cell. Examples obj.SetFont("Arial") !! change to Arial obj.SetFont("Arial",,700) !! change to Arial, bold obj.SetFont(,14) !! set font size obj.SetFont(,,,1)...

name: Setindent
examples: label_1.Setindent(5) !! moves left margin 5 pixels to the right
text: Setindent Setindent This method sets new indentation, i.e. the margin between the border and the text. Format 1 Setindent(indent) Set indentation of an object Format 2 Setindent(line, col, indent) Set indentation of a grid cell Parameters - indent - new indentation, in pixels, integer literal or integer variable. - line - grid line, numeric value. The lines are counted from 1.To specify current line, we can use the internal variable CURRENT_LINE or star (*). - col - grid column, numeric value. The columns are counted from 1. Applied To Text and source field. Note If an object is left aligned (Horizontal alignment = left), this method changes left margin. If object is right aligned (Horizontal alignment = right), the method moves right margin to the left. Examples label_1.Setindent(5) !! moves left margin 5 pixels to the right sourcefield_1.Setindent(10) table_1.Setindent(counter_1,2,5) table_1.Setindent(current_line,2,5) table_1.Setindent(*,2,5) !! same as above See also Methods

name: Setlineheight
text: Setlineheight Setlineheight This method sets grid line height. Format Setlineheight (line, height) Parameters - line - grid line, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1. Special value is CURRENT_LINE. Column heading has line = -1. - height - line height in pixels, integer. If height = -1, autofit operation will be applied. Applied To Grid. Note The grid attribute ' Variable row height ' must be checked. Examples Table_1.Setlineheight(5,20) Table_1.Setlineheight(current_line,25) Table_1.Setlineheight($line,$h) Table_1.Setlineheight(-1,-1) !! Autofit column heading See also Setcolumnwidth

name: Setnumbercolumns
text: Setnumbercolumns Setnumbercolumns This method sets number of columns in a read-only grid. Format Setnumbercolumns (columns) Parameters - columns - number of columns, integer Applied To Read-only grid. Note New columns take on attributes (font, colors, alignment) from last created column. Example table_1.Setnumbercolumns(counter_1)

name: Setnumberlines
text: Setnumberlines Setnumberlines This method sets number of lines in the grid to be displayed in display phase. Format Setnumberlines(lines) Parameters - lines - number of lines, integer Applied To Data entry grid. Note 1/ When we use the grid for updating a table or a file, in 'Table starting actions' we load records using 'Read_all/load_key' statement. The loop 'Read_all ... end_read' fills up internal grid structures and the program knows initial number of the records, record keys, etc. After that, program displays existing lines and moves the cursor to the next row (if new lines enabled, see Enablenewlines), or to the first row if new line cannot be entered. Therefore, we must not use this method in this case. The next two statements are sufficient to initial grid filling: read_all/load_key myfile key=... end_read or, if we use database read_all/load_key mytbl Where ... end_read 2/ If we use the grid to update arrays, we MUST use this method to specify number of initial rows in the grid. In 'Table starting actions' we fill up arrays and call Setnumberlines: $arr1(1) = 10 $arr1(2) = 20 $arr1(3) = 30 $arr2(1) = 'AAA' $arr2(2) = 'BBB' $arr2(3) = 'CCC' table_1.Setnumberlines(3) Thi...

name: Setparttextcolor
text: Setparttextcolor Setparttextcolor This method sets the color of the part of text that matches the given pattern. Format 1 Setparttextcolor (pattern, bkColor, txColor) Sets color of an object Format 2 Setparttextcolor (row, column, pattern, bkColor, txColor) Sets color of a grid cell Parameters - pattern - text, alpha literal or alpha variable, against which a field is compared. The comparison is case insensitive. If a database field, displaying in a sourcefield object, is an ANSI field, the pattern should be ANSI one, too. If the database field is Unicode utf-16, the pattern should be in utf-16 format. - bkcolor - background color of the pattern, a numeric variable with valid RGB color value or one of the predefined colors or RGB function. Optional. - txcolor - text color of the pattern, a numeric variable with valid RGB color value or one of the predefined colors or RGB function. Optional. - row - grid row, numeric value. Rows are counted from 1.To specify current row, we can use the internal variable CURRENT_LINE or star (*). Also, the constant ENTIRE_COLUMN can be used to denote all rows in a column. - column - grid column, numeric value. The columns are counted from 1. Appli...

name: Set Password Protection
description: Set Password Protection Set Password Protection
text: Set Password Protection Set Password Protection

name: Setpos
text: Setpos Setpos This method sets the current position of the progress bar. Format Setpos(pos) Parameters - pos - numeric variable specifying current position of the progress bar. Applied To Progress bar. Example progress_1.setrange(1,100) counter_1 = 0 do_while counter_1 < 100 add 1 to counter_1 progress_1.Setpos(counter_1) end_do See also Setrange, Progress bar, Methods

name: Setposition
text: Setposition Setposition This method sets the new position and/or new size of an object. Format Setposition(x,y,width,height) Parameters - x - x position in pixels, integer - y - y position in pixels, integer - width - width in pixels, integer - height - height in pixels, integer Applied To All objects. Note Unused parameters can be omitted. If you use this method with an object X, Y denotes client coordinates (relative to the left upper corner of the form). But, if you use this method with FORM, X and Y denotes screen coordinates. Examples obj.Setposition(100,10) !! changes x, y obj.Setposition(,10) !! changes y obj.Setposition(100,10,200,50) !! changes x,y,width,height form.Setposition(200,100) !! screen coordinates See also Getposition, Methods

name: Setprefix
text: Setprefix Setprefix Defines qualifier of nodes or elements if they need to be qualified. Format Setprefix (qualifier) Parameters - qualifier - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies the qualifier. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok <> 0 Error Applied To Xml object. Note To disable current qualifier, call the method with empty string. Example $stat = Xml.Setprefix("def") See also Using XML

name: Setrange
text: Setrange Setrange This method sets the lower and upper limits of the progress bar. Format Setrange(from, to) Parameters - from - lower limit, integer - to - upper limit, integer Applied To Progress bar. Example progress_1.setrange(1,100) See also Setpos, Progress bar, Methods

name: Setreadonly
text: Setreadonly Setreadonly This method disables/enables keyboard and mouse input for an object. Format Setreadonly(value) Parameters - value - a numeric variable, integer. Value <> 0, object is read-only and cannot be edited or clicked, value = 0, object is not read-only. Applied To Active objects: Destination field, Combobox, Checkbox, Radiobutton, Pushbutton, Bitmapbutton, Grid, Form. Notes 1/ Read-only state remains active until object.Setreadonly(0), object.Setenable(1) or FINISH_WINDOW. 2/ In FORM.SETREADONLY(1), all the fields on the current subform become read-only, ie. can not be edited. This situation continues until the next subform initialization (finish_window). In this case, the good practice is to create a cancel button (sequence = 0) on the subform and do the following: if condition form.setreadonly(1) cancel_button.setreadonly(0) cancel_button.setfocus() end_if See also Setreadonlyline, Setenable, Methods

name: Setreadonlyline
text: Setreadonlyline Setreadonlyline This method disables fields editing in one line of a data-entry grid. Format Setreadonlyline(line, flag) Parameters - line - grid line, integer. - flag - integer. If flag <> 0, line is read-only and the fields can not be changed. If flag = 0, this is standard line. Initially, all displayed lines are standard ones. Applied To Applies to a data-entry grid. Note See also Setreadonly

name: Setsection
text: Setsection Setsection This method creates a section of a grid. The section is continuous group of lines that can be hidden. Format Setsection (line_start, line_end) Parameters - line_start - start of the section, numeric value, integer. The first line is 1. - line_end - end of the section, numeric value, integer. Applied To Grid. Note Examples Table_1.Setsection(5,20) Table_1.Setsection($line_from,$line_end) See also

name: Setsheetname
text: Setsheetname Setsheetname This method changes the name of current sheet or inserts a new sheet. Format Setsheetname (sheet_name [,mode]) Parameters - sheet_name - new name, alpha variable or alpha literal. Length of sheet name is limited to 31 characters. You cannot use the following 7 characters: * / \ [ ] ? : in the name. These characters will be replaced by space character at run-time. - mode - mode of operation, alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. If this parameter has value "NEW", new sheet will be inserted to the right of all existing sheets. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Excel object. Examples $stat = Excel.Setsheetname('New name') $stat = Excel.Setsheetname($new_name) $stat = Excel.Setsheetname('New sheet', 'new') Using Excel

name: Settextcolor
text: Settextcolor Settextcolor This method sets the text (foreground) color of an object or grid cell. Format 1 Settextcolor (color) Sets text color of an object Format 2 Settextcolor (line,column,color) Sets text color of a grid cell Parameters - color - a numeric variable with valid RGB color value, one of the predefined colors or RGB function. - line - grid line, numeric value. The lines are counted from 1.To specify current line, we can use the internal variable CURRENT_LINE or star (*). - column - grid column, numeric value. The columns are counted from 1. If this parameter has the value -1, the entire line will be colored. Examples label_1.settextcolor("black") label_1.settextcolor("red") label_1.settextcolor(RGB(255,0,0)) !! same as above label_1.settextcolor(255) label_1.settextcolor(256*128 + 255) table_1.settextcolor(counter_1,2,"white") table_1.settextcolor(current_line,2,"white") table_1.settextcolor(*,2,"white") !! same as above table_1.settextcolor(*,-1,"white") See also Setbackcolor, Setbordercolor, Excel.Settextcolor Methods

name: Settimerinterval
text: Settimerinterval Settimerinterval This method sets new timer interval value, in milliseconds. Format Settimerinterval(time_interval) Parameters - time_interval - numeric integer variable or numeric literal. Applied To Timer. Note If the timer is active, this method stops the timer, sets new value and starts it again. If timer was stopped, this method just sets new value. Example Timer_1.Settimerinterval(10000) !! 10 sec See also Start Stop Methods

name: Settopline
text: Settopline Settopline This method sets the first visible line to line. Format Settopline(line) Parameters - line - a numeric variable or numeric literal. Constant LAST_PAGE denotes last grid page. Applied To Display-only grid. Note Used usually in 'Table ending.actions'. Example Table_1.Settopline(10) Table_1.Settopline(LAST_PAGE) See Gettopline, Methods

name: Setvisible
text: Setvisible Setvisible This method displays or hides an object. Format Setvisible(value) Parameters - value - a numeric variable or numeric literal. value = 0, hides the object, value <> 0, shows the object. Applied To All objects. Example Label_1.SetVisible(0) !! Set object Label_1 invisible See also Methods Isvisible

name: Setxmldecpoint
text: Setxmldecpoint Setxmldecpoint Defines decimal point for numeric elements if the application decimal point does not correspond to the xml output. Format Setxmldecpoint (xml_decpoint) Parameters - xml_decpoint - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies the decimal point (comma or point). Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok <> 0 Error Applied To Xml object. Example $stat = Xml.Setxmldecpoint(".") See also Using XML

name: SHIFT_ARRAY
description: This function shifts array elements left or right, simultaneously placing the element to be removed into the result variable. On exit, array is shifted and removed element is placed to the result.
text: SHIFT_ARRAY SHIFT_ARRAY This function shifts array elements left or right, simultaneously placing the element to be removed into the result variable. On exit, array is shifted and removed element is placed to the result. Format SHIFT_ARRAY(array, shift_direction[, starting_index]) Return Value Type Any type. Parameters - array - array to be shifted, any type. - shift_direction - shift direction, integer. Value = 0 left shift, value <> 0 right shift. - starting_index - index from which shift starts, integer. The first element has index 1. If omitted, value is 1. If the index is < 1 or > array_length, result is initialized and array stays unchanged. Algorithm Let's consider a local array $ARR with 7 elements. SHIFT_ARRAY($ARR,0) works as Result = $arr(1) $arr(1) = $arr(2) $arr(2) = $arr(3) $arr(3) = $arr(4) $arr(4) = $arr(5) $arr(5) = $arr(6) $arr(6) = $arr(7) $arr(7) initialized SHIFT_ARRAY($ARR,0,4) works as Result = $arr(4) $arr(1) unchanged $arr(2) unchanged $arr(3) unchanged $arr(4) = $arr(5) $arr(5) = $arr(6) $arr(6) = $arr(7) $arr(7) initialized SHIFT_ARRAY($ARR,1) works as Result = $arr(7) $arr(7) = $arr(6) $arr(6) = $arr(5) $arr(5) = $arr(4) $arr(4) = $arr(3) $arr(3) = $a...

name: Show
text: Show Show global fields This dialog shows all the global fields defined in the DD. General tab The list shows the following field attributes: - field name, - field type, - field size, - sign existence, - data-type and - edit mask Not used tab Shows all the fields not used by any record definition. Cross reference tab For particular field, shows all the records using that field definition.

name: Show
description: Shows list of tables and files used in the program.
text: Show Show Datasource Shows list of tables and files used in the program. Local fields Shows local fields defined in the program. Strings Shows strings defined in the Data Dictionary. Local procedure(s) / function(s) Shows local procedures and functions defined in the program. Global procedure(s) / function(s) Shows global procedures and functions defined in the Data Dictionary. Protection Shows protection information for this program. Starting actions Shows starting actions. Ending actions Shows ending actions. General Shows some information about program (Data Dictionary name, application name, program name, working directory, program type and version number). Subform calculations Shows calculations for this subform. Help screens Shows help screens defined in the Data Dictionary. List of values (iaflov.iaf) Sequence Shows sequence numbers for active objects. Fields Shows field names for data entry fields. Object names Shows object names. Object list Show object list with detailed information about objects. Layout Shows positions of all subforms if program has more than one subform. Z-Order Shows Z-Order for passive objects.

name: Show object list
description: Show object list Show object list
text: Show object list Show object list This dialog lists all the objects in the subform or report. Some suspicious objects (width and height equal 0; x,y beyond subform limits, invalid field type, etc.) are denoted by a question mark. In addition to list review, you can perform some housekeeping functions (remove an object, reset coordinates, hide object, sort z-order, etc.) Delete Removes selected object from the subform / report. Reset X,Y Sets x = 0 and y = 0. Reset gradient Resets gradient information (shading style, shading variant, gradient colors). Hide Temporary hides selected object. This option is very useful if two or more objects overlap. Unhide Unhides selected object. Unhide all Unhides all hidden objects. Set to Alpha, Num, Date, Time When a sourcefield or destinationfield object is created for the first time, the attribute "field type" is set to one of values "Alpha", "Num", "Date" or "Time". For example, Source=acc_1 has field_type=Num because acc_1 is a numeric field. Later, using editor we can change this instruction to, for instance, Source = program_name. Edit mask can also be changed from "ZZ9,99" to "XXXXXXXXX"; see dialog "Change edit mask". The problem is tha...

name: SKIP
description: This statement skips n lines in a report.
examples: SKIP_AFTER 2 !!Print this lineset and skip 32 pixels
SKIP_BEFORE 1 !! skip 16 pixels before printing the lineset
SKIP_BEFORE !!Same as above. Default skip is 16
SKIP_BEFORE PAGE !!Jump to the next page
SKIP_BEFORE COLUMN !!Jump to the top of the next column
text: SKIP SKIP This statement skips n lines in a report. Format - SKIP_AFTER [line | PAGE] - SKIP_BEFORE [line | PAGE | COLUMN] Where - line - a number of the lines which are skipped. Number has to be greater than zero and less than page size. - PAGE - determines jump to a new page. - COLUMN - valid for multi-column reports only. Current print position is moved to the top of the next column. Note Depending on a use of _AFTER or _BEFORE, lines are being skipped after or before the lineset where instruction was used. If line or PAGE is omitted, default skip is 1 line. In GUI reports one line is 16 pixels high. Examples SKIP_AFTER 2 !!Print this lineset and skip 32 pixels SKIP_BEFORE 1 !! skip 16 pixels before printing the lineset SKIP_BEFORE !!Same as above. Default skip is 16 SKIP_BEFORE PAGE !!Jump to the next page SKIP_BEFORE COLUMN !!Jump to the top of the next column

name: SORT_ARRAY
syntax: SORT_ARRAY array , sort_order , starting_index, ending_index ,collation [, dep_array1, dep_array2, ..., dep_arrayN]
text: SORT_ARRAY SORT_ARRAY This statement sorts an array. Format SORT_ARRAY array , sort_order , starting_index, ending_index ,collation [, dep_array1, dep_array2, ..., dep_arrayN] Where - array - main array to be sorted, required parameter. - sort_order - "A" for ascending, "D" for descending. Default is "A". If we use an alpha variable and the variable contains invalid value (a character different from "A" or "D"), ascending order will be applied. If the length of the variable is greater than 1, the first character will be used. - starting_index - index of the first element to be sorted, integer. Default value is 1 (the first element). - ending_index - index of the last element to be sorted, integer. Default is the last element. - collation - collation name. Default is binary sort. This parameter is ignored if main array is not an alpha one. - dep_array1, dep_array2, ..., dep_arrayN - dependent arrays. These arrays will be rearranged according to main array. Note Consider three arrays, each of them have 3 elements: $main_array $dep_arr1 $dep_arr2 '11' 101 1001 '12' 102 1002 '13' 103 1003 After command sort_array $main_array,'D',1,3,,$dep_arr1,$dep_arr2 array elements will be $main_...

name: SORT_FILE
description: This statement sorts a sequential file.
text: SORT_FILE SORT_FILE This statement sorts a sequential file. Format SORT_FILE seqfile, out_file, sort_key1, ..., sort_keyN Sort_keyN has the format field_name [COLLATE collation_name] [ASC | DESC] Where - seqfile - an input sequential file from Datasource dialog. - out_file - physical name of output file, optional parameter. If omitted, input file will be overwritten. Output file has the same record format as input file. Notes COLLATE is applicable only for alpha fields. Default collation_name is English. ASC is ascending sort order (default). DESC is descending sort order. Status of sort operation is in SORT_STATUS internal variable. If sort successful, SORT_STATUS contains number of sorted records. Negative value denotes sort error. Examples sort_file rseq,,fld1 !!ascending sort_file rseq,,fld1[1:3] sort_file rseq,,fld1,fld2,fld3 !!ascending sort_file rseq,,fld1 DESC !!descending sort_file rseq,,fld1 DESC, fld2 ASC sort_file rseq,,fld1 COLLATE English DESC, fld2 sort_file rseq,,fld1 COLLATE English_CI DESC if SORT_STATUS < 0 Pause 'Sort error='|sort_status else Pause 'Number of sorted records='|sort_status end_if sort_file rseq,'c:\temp\outfile.seq',fld1 sort_file rseq,'tmpdir:...

name: Sorttable
examples: table_1.sorttable(1,2) !! sort by col 1, then by col 2
text: Sorttable Sorttable This method sorts a display-only table (grid) by a column. Format Sorttable([col1[, col2, col3, col4]]) Parameters - col1, col2, col3, col4 - numeric literals or numeric variables representing sort columns. First column is 1, second one is 2, etc. Sort in descending order is represented by negative numbers: -1 for first column, etc. Applied To Display-only grid. Note Col1, col2, ... are optional. Default is column 1. Maximum number of sort columns is 4. To disable column sorting, enter 0 as the first parameter, i.e. Sorttable(0). The context menu commands 'Sort column' and 'Sort complex' will be grayed. The best place to use this method is 'Table ending actions'. At this moment the grid is filled and prepared to display. If we do not use this method, line appearance is determined by SELECT_START or START instruction that we used in 'Table starting actions'. Examples table_1.sorttable() !! sort by col 1 (default sort) table_1.sorttable(1) !! same as above table_1.sorttable(1,2) !! sort by col 1, then by col 2 table_1.sorttable(-1) !! sort by col 1, descending table_1.sorttable(0) !! disable sorting See also Methods

name: SOURCE
description: This statement defines fields for display on the screen or on report.
text: SOURCE SOURCE This statement defines fields for display on the screen or on report. Format SOURCE = val_exp [/FILL: char] [/FIRST] [/MOVE] [/RESET_ON_BREAK] Where - val_exp - the expression which is displaying - char - ASCII character Qualifiers /FILL replaces left blanks by char. /FIRST is used in reports only. Field with this qualifier is showed in the report only if value changes or if there is a jump to new page. /MOVE determines field for transfer from a subprogram to main program. /RESET_ON_BREAK is used in reports only, for page counter initialization. Can be used only with internal field PAGE_COUNTER. When page header break occurs, page_counter will be set to 1. Note The statement defines the source of the information only (field1, counter_1, acc_1, today, ...), but the form of the presentation (text, image, barcode) should be specified in corresponding object (property name = Source field style). Examples Source = page_counter Source = page_counter /reset_on_break Source = acc_1 /fill: * See also Source field

name: Source Field
text: Source Field Source Field A source field displays value expression (a variable, formula, function, ...). To insert new source field on the form, click button Source Field in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Source Field. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. A dialog "Sourcefield definition" appears on the screen. Enter the variable name (field to be displayed) or click Browse to choose a variable from a list. Click OK. If the variable exists, object SOURCEFIELD will be created: default edit mask is displayed on the screen, and one source line is placed in code buffer. The line is Source = variable_name See Source statement. The source field object has the two components: visual appearance (edit mask on the screen) and code buffer (contains source statements defining which value will be seen on the screen at run-time). The edit mask can be changed by context menu command "Change edit mask". To change the code, choose context menu command "Modify code". Display multiline field Enter the variable name, check "Multiline" check box and click OK. Resize the object. Display an image If an alpha variable contains path to image file, or if a blob variable contains i...

name: Source_field properties
text: Source_field properties Source_field properties Object name When the new source_field is created, generator automatically assigns the name Sourcefield_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Source field style Standard Display text. Image Display image. The variable contains path to the image file, like c:\image\img1.bmp or my_images:img1.bmp. Allowed types: ICO, BMP, JPEG, GIF. Barcode_XXX Draw barcode. The variable contains barcode string. See Barcode printing. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed. Display entire text, no clip By default, text is clipped to the rectangle. The part of the text that is inside the rectangle is visible. The remaining part is invisible (transparent). If we want to display entire text, regardless of clipping rectangle, this option should be checked. In t...

name: Spin
text: Spin Spin Not implemented yet !

name: Spin properties
text: Spin properties Spin properties

name: SPLIT_CSV
description: This function splits an alpha field containing csv string into alpha array.
text: SPLIT_CSV SPLIT_CSV This function splits an alpha field containing csv string into alpha array. Format SPLIT_CSV(separator,text_qualifier,code_page,alpha_field) Return Value Type Alpha array. Parameters - separator - alpha field, length = 1. Character that separates csv fields. - text_qualifier - integer denoting the character that encloses csv fields. Allowed values: 0 - no qualifier, 1 - quotation mark, 2 - apostrophe. All of the text between qualifiers is treated as one field, even if the field contains separator character. Two consecutive text qualifiers in the field are treated as one character. - code_page - integer. If output array is an utf-16 array, this parameter will be used in conversion. Value 0 denotes current code page. UTF-8 code page is 65001. See code page. - alpha_field - input alpha field. Note The function can be used in an assignment only. Syntax is: array() = SPLIT_CSV(separator,tq,cp,alpha_field) Examples If the input field $INP contains 100;200;"text1";"text2""ext";text3;text4 and $arrw is an alpha array, 10 utf-16 elements, the following statement $arrw() = SPLIT_CSV(';',1,0,$inp) gives the result $arrw(1) = '100' $arrw(2) = '200' $arrw(3) = 'text1' $ar...

name: SPLIT
description: This function splits an alpha field by separator into alpha array.
text: SPLIT SPLIT This function splits an alpha field by separator into alpha array. Format SPLIT(separator,alpha_field) Return Value Type Alpha array. Parameters - separator - alpha field, length = 1 Note The function can be used in an assignment only. Syntax is: array() = SPLIT(separator, alpha_field) Examples If $arr is an alpha array, 10 elements, the following statement $arr() = SPLIT(";","string1;string2;string3") gives the result $arr(1) = "string1" $arr(2) = "string2" $arr(3) = "string3" $arr(4) = $arr(5) = ... = space See Also COMPRESS_SEP, String Functions

name: SQRT
description: This function returns square root of a numeric value.
text: SQRT SQRT This function returns square root of a numeric value. Format SQRT(numeric_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - numeric_exp - Note Numeric_exp must be >= 0. Examples See Also

name: START
description: This statement positions on a record which is determined by a key.
text: START START This statement positions on a record which is determined by a key. Format START file [KEY[n] = key] [WHERE cond_exp] Where - file - the name of the file from datasource dialog. - key - concatenation - cond_exp - conditional expression. If FALSE, record wiil not be displayed in the grid. Note The statement is used with index-sequential or sequential files to fill a display-only grid. If an application uses RDB tables, see SELECT_START. Example START file1 START file2 KEY='ABC' START file2 KEY='ABC' Where fld1 <> 0

name: Starting actions
text: Starting actions Starting actions This piece of code executes after program initialization and before the first subform is displayed. Program initialization initializes some variables, reads command line parameters, opens/creates files, connects to database. At this moment, there is no objects on the screen and because of this, this code cannot reference any object. Maximum number of lines is 10. If this number is too little, PERFORM statement can be used to execute a procedure.

name: Start
description: This method starts the timer or GIF animation.
text: Start Start This method starts the timer or GIF animation. Format Start() Parameters - none Applied To Timer, Image, Pushbutton, Bitmapbutton. Note - If the timer is already started, this method will be ignored. Timer interval is defined statically (Timer interval property) or dynamically by Settimerinterval method. - To use an implicit animation on button click (pushbutton, bitmapbutton), following conditions should be satisfied: - Folder c:\iafw\iafres must hold file ANI_PROGRESS.GIF. - Define symbol ANI_BUTTON in HLM\Software\Sing\Iafw\1.0. Symbol value is #ani_progress.gif. Examples button_1.start() !! start implicit animation ... ... long lasting operations ... button_1.stop() !! stop implicit animation See also Stop Methods

name: INIT
description: This statement initializes field values (ZERO, SPACE, ZERO_DATE, ZERO_TIME).
text: INIT INIT This statement initializes field values (ZERO, SPACE, ZERO_DATE, ZERO_TIME). Format 1 INIT field_1[(index)], ..., field_n[(index)] Format 2 INIT field() Where - field_1,..., field_n - fields used in the program. - field() - an array. In this case, all the array elements will be initialized. Examples INIT acc_1, counter_1, $loc1, $loc2($ndx) INIT $loc_array() See also Init method

name: Statements
text: Statements Statements ADD adds numeric_exp to a numeric field. BREAK exits immediately innermost loop. BREAK_FIELD defines terms for group actions in batch programs. CALL calls function written in C programming language. CALL_PROGRAM calls an external executable program (EXE). CALL_STOREDPROCEDURE calls a stored procedure (ODBC). CALL_WINDOW calls a window (subform). CHAIN calls another program or procedure. CHECK controls entered value. CHECK_RECORD/CHECK_NORECORD controls (non)existing record. CLOSE closes file. COMMIT confirms transaction. DEFINE_LOCAL defines local field in a procedure. DEFINE_RECAP_RECORD defines recapitulation record in reports. DELETE_RECORD removes an existing record from an indexed file or RDB table. DESTINATION defines data entry field. DETAIL_CALC specifies part of a batch program which is executed. DISPLAY_WINDOW_FIELDS displays fields on the screen. DO_WHILE marks beginning of a cycle. END_ ends block of instructions. ERRMSG displays error message if the CHECK expression value is FALSE. EXECSQL executes a SQL statement. EXECUTE executes operating system commands. FINISH_WINDOW FUNCTION is a procedure which has arguments and returns a value. IF - con...

name: Stop
description: This method stops the timer or GIF animation.
text: Stop Stop This method stops the timer or GIF animation. Format Stop() Parameters - none Applied To Timer, Image. Note If the timer is already stopped, this method will be ignored. To start the timer again, use Start method. See also Methods

name: STOP_RUN
text: STOP_RUN STOP_RUN This statement stops program. Format STOP_RUN [/SYSTEM] Qualifier /SYSTEM ''jumps'' over ending actions. Note Control is transfered to ending actions of the program, executes ending actions and stops the program. If /SYSTEM qualifier is used, program control ''jumps'' over ending actions and goes directly to an operating system. Example STOP_RUN

name: STORED_FUNCTION
description: This function calls a stored function via ODBC. Stored function is a saved SQL routine that returns a value. Iaf can call stored functions that return scalar data types: number, date, character.
text: STORED_FUNCTION STORED_FUNCTION This function calls a stored function via ODBC. Stored function is a saved SQL routine that returns a value. Iaf can call stored functions that return scalar data types: number, date, character. Format STORED_FUNCTION(function_name[,arg1][,arg2][,...argN]) Return Value Type Alpha, numeric or date. Parameters - function_name - alpha literal, name of the stored function. Function name can be qualified by owner, database or catalog. Portable delimiter is DOT. If program uses Informix, this delimiter will be replaced by COLON. Valid names are "fnc1", "owner.fnc1", "database.owner.fnc1" (for MS SQL Server). - arg1,..., argN - arguments of the stored function. An argument can be: NULL (null value), literal or variable. Notes The function can be used in an assignment only. Syntax is: variable = STORED_FUNCTION(function_name [,arg1]...[,argN]) If the result can be NULL, the left-hand variable must be one from a database table, because standard Iaf variables have no NULL flag. Example counter_1 = STORED_FUNCTION("fnc_noarg") counter_1 = STORED_FUNCTION("fnc1", NULL, "ABC", $FLD1) tblfld = stored_function("fnc1",arg1) if tblfld NULL ... end_if !! we cannot ...

name: STR_FILL
description: This function fills alpha variable by 'fill string'.
text: STR_FILL STR_FILL This function fills alpha variable by 'fill string'. Format STR_FILL(fill_string[, repeat_count]) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - fill_string - alpha literal or alpha variable. - repeat_count - integer, optional. Number of times the fill_string is repeated. If repeat_count is omitted, whole output variable will be filled by fill_string. Notes - If fill_string is an alpha variable, function first removes trailing blanks and then uses remaining text as a fill_string. - If fill_string is an alpha literal, string is used without changes. - If output variable is a CLOB (field type = S), repeat_count is required. - Commands $A = str_fill('') (empty fill_string) $A = str_fill(' ') (fill_string = space) $A = str_fill(' ',0) (repeat_count = 0) have the same effect as INIT $A but INIT is slightly faster. Examples $res = STR_FILL('-') $res = STR_FILL('-',10) $res = STR_FILL('-=*') $res = STR_FILL('-=*',5) $fill= 'abc' $res = STR_FILL($fill) !! fill_string is 'abc' $fill= 'ab' $res = STR_FILL($fill) !! fill_string is 'ab' See Also String functions

name: STR_INDEX
description: This function returns the position of the first occurrence of a character in an alphanumeric string.
text: STR_INDEX STR_INDEX This function returns the position of the first occurrence of a character in an alphanumeric string. Format STR_INDEX(alpha_var, char) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - alpha_var - input alpha variable. - char - a character to search for, alpha literal or alpha field. If you specify an alpha variable, only the first character will be used in search process. Note If character is not found, result is –1, otherwise, result is between 1 and length of alpha_var. The leftmost character has position 1. Examples $res = STR_INDEX("ABCD","B") !! result is 2 $res = STR_INDEX("ABCD","X") !! result is -1 $res = STR_INDEX("ABCD","BX") !! result is 2 $AVAR = "BXY" $res = STR_INDEX("ABCD",$AVAR) !! result is 2 See Also SUBSTR_INDEX String functions

name: STRING_EDIT
description: This function returns string text, simultaneously replacing placeholders by parameters.
text: STRING_EDIT STRING_EDIT This function returns string text, simultaneously replacing placeholders by parameters. Format STRING_EDIT(alpha_var, par1, par2, ..., parN) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_var - an alpha variable, alpha literal or STRING function. The number of placeholders (%) in input string, and number of declared parameters should be the same. - par1, par2, ... parN - the variables whose values will replace placeholders. The first variable (par1) will replace the first placeholder, etc. Notes There are two special characters: % (parameter placeholder) and \ (escape character). The parameter placeholder enables inserting program variables into a text using. The escape character allows including into a text some characters which cannot be typed in directly. Parameter placeholder The percent sign (%) is placeholder. The next string contains two placeholders: Invalid value, must be in the range % to % Special characters Line feed \n Carriage return \r Tabulator \t Backslash \\ Percent sign \% The next string contains one placeholder and the escape character: The percent is % \% Examples errmsg = STRING_EDIT(string(ids_1),acc_1,acc_2) If the string IDS_1 conta...

name: STRING
text: STRING STRING This function returns string text. Format STRING(identifier) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - identifier - string identifier, case-insensitive. Examples errmsg = STRING(IDS_1) errmsg = string(ids_1) obj.Display(string(ids_1)) $loc = STRING(IDS_1) See Also STRING_EDIT Strings definition

name: String Functions
text: String Functions String Functions ALLTRIM removes white space from both ends of alpha_exp. ALPHA converts any field to alphanumeric one. ASC returns the ASCII code value of the leftmost character of alpha_exp. CHAR_OF extracts one character from an alpha_exp. CHR returns the character that has the ASCII code value specified by numeric_exp. CHRW returns the wide character that has the code value specified by numeric_exp. COMPRESS creates a concatenation of many alphanumeric values. COMPRESS_CSV creates a concatenation of many trimmed (left and right) alphanumeric values. COMPRESS_SEP creates a concatenation of many trimmed (left and right) alphanumeric values, adding a separator at the end of each input value. COMPRESS_TRIM creates a concatenation of many trimmed (left and right) alphanumeric values. CONVERT converts an alpha_exp from one character set to another. EDIT returns formatted representation of numeric, date or time variable. ENVVAR returns value of an environment variable. INITCAP converts the first letter of each word in uppercase, and all other letters converts in lowercase. INITCAP_NLS converts the first letter of each word in uppercase, and all other letters conver...

name: String operators
text: String operators String operators The only string operator is concatenation. The concatenation symbol is |. Example: $alpha = "ABC" | "abc" Result is "ABCabc".

name: Strings
text: Strings Strings Manipulation Enables creating, modifying and deleting the strings. Show Shows all the strings defined in the Data Dictionary. Translation Translates strings from your default language to any other language. We can translate strings stored in: Data Dictionary, menus and programs (text labels, ...).

name: Strings definition
text: Strings definition Strings definition In this dialog (menu command Define/Strings) we can define strings which will be used in our applications. Strings are global resources stored in DD7. The list shows all strings already defined in the Data Dictionary. New string Enter new string. Edit string Modify an existing string. Delete string Remove existing string from the DD7. Refresh Load strings into the list. Export Store strings to an external file. The file has UTF-8 format. The first three bytes in the export file, byte order mask, are EF BB BF hexadecimal. Each line consists of string identifier, one blank character and string text. Import Load strings definition into DD7 from an external UTF-8 file. Such file can be created by any text editor with UTF-8 option ('Save as' command, UTF-8 encoding). Line format is: string_identifier<blank>text Example: ids_1 This is message 1 ids_2 Message 2 ident_nosuchrecord Record with this key does not exist See Also Modify String, STRING, STRING_EDIT

name: STR_LENGTH
description: This function returns the position of the last non-blank character in an alphanumeric value.
text: STR_LENGTH STR_LENGTH This function returns the position of the last non-blank character in an alphanumeric value. Format STR_LENGTH(alpha_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - alpha_exp - alphanumeric field or literal. Note If alpha_exp contains all blanks result is 0, otherwise, result is between 1 and length of alpha_exp. The leftmost character has position 1. If alpha_exp contains an UTF-8 string, result is position of the last non-blank byte. Examples $len = STR_LENGTH("ABCD ") !! result is 4 $len = STR_LENGTH("ABCD E ") !! result is 6 $win1250str = "ŠЎ" !! Central European letters $len = STR_LENGTH($win1250str) !! result is 3 $res = convert($win1250str,1250,65001) !! convert to UTF-8 $len = STR_LENGTH($res) !! result is 6 See Also Convert

name: SUB_DAYS
description: This function subtracts a number of days from a date.
text: SUB_DAYS SUB_DAYS This function subtracts a number of days from a date. Format SUB_DAYS(date,numdays) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date - date_exp - numdays - number of days, numeric_exp Examples SUB_DAYS(today,5) SUB_DAYS(BEGINCY,-12) SUB_DAYS(today,counter_1) See Also Date Functions

name: Subform
text: Subform Subform A subform is complex object containing many related objects.These objects make an unit within main form, usually displaying/manipulating related set of data. Any program can have up to 20 subforms. First of all, define all the objects which belong to the subform. After that, click button Subform in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Subform. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click and select (rubber-band) rectangular area to surround all previously defined objects. Definition dialog appears on the screen.

name: Subform processing
text: Subform processing Subform processing The diagram bellow shows how run-time engine processes a subform.

name: Subform properties
text: Subform properties Subform properties Object name When the new subform is created, generator automatically assigns the name Subform_n, where n = 1, 2, ...20. Object name can be used in Methods. Subform name cannot be changed. Subform type Type Description Standard Normal subform. Tabbed subform All tabbed subforms occupy the same space on main frame, but only one could be visible at the moment. To change active subform, user should click appropriate tab. Slave (invoked by CALL_WINDOW) This type of subform could be invoked via CALL_WINDOW statement only. Overlay Many subforms overlays the same area in the parent subform. This means the following: parent subform and one of overlay subforms are active at the same moment. To display an overlay, use the DISPLAY method, for example, subform_5.display(""). Overlay + tab Tooltip Next subform Applies to standard and tabbed subform. Defines the subform where program control goes to the end of the current subform processing. Caption Subform title, applies to tabbed subforms. Conditional Applies to standard and tabbed subform. If the subform is conditional, run-time engine checks the condition before activating the subform. If condition is ...

name: Subforms
text: Subforms Subforms Add Adds new subform. Delete Deletes existing subform(s). Layout Shows positions of all subforms if program has more than one subform. Arrange Adjusts positions on the screen of subforms. Swap Changes mutual position of two subforms. Example: if we swap subform 7 and subform 2, subform 2 becomes subform 7 and subform 7 becomes subform 2. Move Move one subform from one position to the another one. For example, move subform 7 before subform 1. In this case, subform 7 becomes subform 1, subform 1 becomes subform 2, etc. Insert Inserts empty subform before selected subform. Include Inserts subform from another program. Quit subform Abandons all changes on current subform.

name: SUB_HOURS
description: This function subtracts a number of hours from a time or datetime field.
text: SUB_HOURS SUB_HOURS This function subtracts a number of hours from a time or datetime field. Format SUB_HOURS(time,numhours) Return Value Type If 'time' parameter is a time field, return value is time. If 'time' parameter is a datetime field, return value is datetime. Parameters - time - time_expression or datetime field - numhours - number of hours, numeric_exp Note 1 If 'time' parameter is a time, out-of-range result time will be corrected automatically. Note 2 If 'time' parameter is a datetime, result is datetime field and all the field's components will be adjusted. Examples $res_time = SUB_HOURS (systime,5) $res_time = SUB_HOURS (systime,counter_1) $res_time = SUB_HOURS ("00:50:00",1) !! result = 23:50:00 See Also Time Functions ADD_HOURS SUB_MINUTES SUB_SECONDS

name: SUB_MINUTES
description: This function subtracts a number of minutes from a time or datetime field.
text: SUB_MINUTES SUB_MINUTES This function subtracts a number of minutes from a time or datetime field. Format SUB_MINUTES(time, numminutes) Return Value Type If 'time' parameter is a time field, return value is time. If 'time' parameter is a datetime field, return value is datetime. Parameters - time - time_expression or datetime field - numminutes - number of minutes, numeric_exp Note 1 If 'time' parameter is a time, out-of-range result time will be corrected automatically. Note 2 If 'time' parameter is a datetime, result is datetime field and all the field's components will be adjusted. Examples $res_time = SUB_MINUTES (systime,5) $res_time = SUB_MINUTES (systime,counter_1) $res_time = SUB_MINUTES ("00:05:00",10) !! result = 23:55:00 See Also Time Functions ADD_MINUTES SUB_HOURS SUB_SECONDS

name: SUB_MONTHS
description: This function subtracts a number of months from a date.
text: SUB_MONTHS SUB_MONTHS This function subtracts a number of months from a date. Format SUB_MONTHS(date,nummonths) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date - date_exp - nummonths - number of months, numeric_exp Note Out-of-range result date will be corrected automatically. Examples SUB_MONTHS (today,5) SUB_MONTHS (BEGINCY,-3) SUB_MONTHS (today,counter_1) SUB_MONTHS ("31-may-2001",1) !! result = 30-apr-2001 SUB_MONTHS ("31-mar-2001",1) !! result = 28-feb-2001 See Also Date Functions

name: SUB_SECONDS
description: This function subtracts a number of seconds from a time or datetime field.
text: SUB_SECONDS SUB_SECONDS This function subtracts a number of seconds from a time or datetime field. Format SUB_SECONDS(time, numseconds) Return Value Type If 'time' parameter is a time field, return value is time. If 'time' parameter is a datetime field, return value is datetime. Parameters - time - time_expression or datetime field. - numseconds - number of seconds, numeric_exp Note 1 If 'time' parameter is a time, out-of-range result time will be corrected automatically. Note 2 If 'time' parameter is a datetime, result is datetime field and all the field's components will be adjusted. Note 3 SUB_SECONDS(time,-10) gives the same result as ADD_SECONDS(time,10). Examples $res_time = SUB_SECONDS (systime,5) $res_time = SUB_SECONDS (systime,counter_1) $res_time = SUB_SECONDS ("00:00:05",10) !! result = 23:59:55 $res_dt = SUB_SECONDS("2009-12-01 15:00:00",5) See Also Time Functions ADD_SECONDS, SUB_MINUTES, SUB_HOURS

name: SUBSTR_INDEX
description: This function returns the position of the first occurrence of a substring.
text: SUBSTR_INDEX SUBSTR_INDEX This function returns the position of the first occurrence of a substring. Format SUBSTR_INDEX(alpha_var, sub_string, case_flag, direction_flag) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - alpha_var - input alpha variable. - sub_string - a substring to search for. - case_flag - case sensitivity flag, integer. If this parameter has value 0 (zero), the strings are case-sensitive. If you want to perform case-insensitive searching, enter code_page (1250, 1251, 1252, ...) to be used in internal uppercase conversion. - direction_flag - search direction flag, integer. Value 0 (zero) denotes searching from left to the right. Non-zero value specifies right to the left searching. Notes If substring is empty, result is 1. If substring was not found, result is –1. Otherwise, result is between 1 and length of alpha_var. The leftmost character has position 1. At the start of search process, the function trims trailing spaces from alpha_var. Also, trailing spaces from substring will be trimmed, if the substring is not a literal. Therefore, searching will be performed just in non-blank part of alpha_var. Let's consider the following example: local alpha variable $A20...

name: SUB_WEEKS
description: This function subtracts a number of weeks from a date.
text: SUB_WEEKS SUB_WEEKS This function subtracts a number of weeks from a date. Format SUB_WEEKS(date,numweeks) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date - date_exp - numweeks - number of weeks, numeric_exp Note 1 week = 7 days. SUB_WEEKS(date,numweeks) = SUB_DAYS(date,7*numweeks) Examples See Also Date Functions

name: SUB_YEARS
description: This function subtracts a number of years from a date.
text: SUB_YEARS SUB_YEARS This function subtracts a number of years from a date. Format SUB_YEARS(date,numyears) Return Value Type Date. Parameters - date - date value expression - numyears - number of years, numeric_exp Note Out-of-range result date will be corrected automatically. Examples SUB_YEARS (today,5) SUB_YEARS (BEGINCY,-3) SUB_YEARS (today,counter_1) SUB_YEARS("29-feb-2000",1) !! result = 28-feb-1999 See Also Date Functions

name: SUM
description: This function returns sum of elements of a numeric array.
examples: acc_1 = sum($arr1) !! sum of all elements
acc_1 = sum($arr1,1,12) !! sum of the first 12 elements
text: SUM SUM This function returns sum of elements of a numeric array. Format SUM (array[,start_index, end_index]) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - array - numeric array. - start_index - integer, starting index (1, 2, ...). - end_index - integer, ending index >= starting index. Examples acc_1 = sum($arr1) !! sum of all elements acc_1 = sum($arr1,1,12) !! sum of the first 12 elements acc_1 = sum($arr1,1,counter_1) See Also TOTAL

name: Sumcol
examples: acc_1 = table_1.sumcol(1) !! sum values in column 1
acc_1 = table_1.sumcol(9) !! sum values in column 9
text: Sumcol Sumcol This method returns sum of values in a column of a grid. Format Sumcol(column_number) Parameters - column_number - numeric literal or numeric variable representing column. If this column contains non-numeric fields, result will be zero. The first column is 1, second one is 2, etc. Applied To Grid. Examples acc_1 = table_1.sumcol(1) !! sum values in column 1 acc_1 = table_1.sumcol(9) !! sum values in column 9 See also Sumcolif, Methods

name: Sumcolif
examples: acc_1 = table_1.sumcolif(1, runningcell(2)='D') !! sum values in column 1
acc_1 = table_1.sumcolif(9, runningcell(8)<0) !! sum values in column 9
text: Sumcolif Sumcolif This method returns conditional sum of values in a column of a grid. Format Sumcolif (column_number, criteria) Parameters - column_number - numeric literal or numeric variable representing sum column. If this column contains non-numeric fields, result will be zero. The first column is 1, second one is 2, etc. - criteria - conditional expression that defines which cells will be added. Conditional expression has the form RUNNINGCELL(comp_column) comparison_operator value This means the following: if cell in column COMP_COLUMN has the value 'VALUE', then the corresponding value in column COLUMN_NUMBER will be added to the sum. Applied To Grid. Note Column_1 Column_2 100 D 200 D 300 P 400 P 500 P Result for Table.sumcolif(1, runningcell(2) = 'D') is 300. Result for Table.sumcolif(1, runningcell(2) = 'P') is 1200. Examples acc_1 = table_1.sumcolif(1, runningcell(2)='D') !! sum values in column 1 acc_1 = table_1.sumcolif(9, runningcell(8)<0) !! sum values in column 9 See also Sumcol, Methods

name: SUMOBJ
description: This function summarizes values of numeric objects (source field, destination field).
text: SUMOBJ SUMOBJ This function summarizes values of numeric objects (source field, destination field). Format SUMOBJ(obj1_name, obj2_name, ..., objN_name) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - obj1_name,..., objN_name - Note If an object is not visible, its value is 0 (zero). Examples acc_1 = sumobj(sf_1, sf_2, sf_3) acc_1 = sumobj(sf_1, formula_1, formula_2) See Also

name: Suppress horizontal region
description: Suppress horizontal region Suppress horizontal region
text: Suppress horizontal region Suppress horizontal region Horizontal region is a region within one lineset. One horizontal region cannot be extended across multiple linesets. Maximum number of regions in one report is 10. For each region you define region position and a condition which specifies when this region is to be suppressed. In Region column you have to enter start and the end of the region in pixels, in the form starting_pixel-ending_pixel Example: 101-120 Horizontal region between 101. (including) and 120. (including) pixels. The minus character is required. In Condition column, you have to enter the suppress condition. The maximal number of characters is 70. Example: acc_1 = 0 and acc_2 = 0 The above condition need to be read like this: when acc_1 has value 0 and acc_2 has value 0, the region from 101. up to 120. pixels will not be printed. What if the condition has more than 70 characters? In that case, a local variable can be defined, let's say $suppreg, who can have two values: 1 or 0. In a field, who belongs to that lineset, the value of the variable need to be calculated. Then, in Condition column you can write $suppreg = 1 or $suppreg = 0 depending on how the value ...

name: Suppress lineset
text: Suppress lineset Suppress lineset A lineset is a part of a report: page header, subheader, detail line, subtotal. For each lineset you can define a condition which specifies when this lineset is to be suppressed. Lineset In Lineset column you see all linesets in the report. Condition In Condition column, you have to enter the suppress condition. The maximal number of characters is 70. If a lineset always prints, this cell must be empty. Example: acc_1 = 0 and acc_2 = 0 The above condition need to be read like this: when acc_1 has value 0 and acc_2 has value 0, entire lineset (detail line, for example) will not be printed. What if the condition has more than 70 characters? In that case, a local variable can be defined, let's say $suppls, who can have two values: 1 or 0. In a field, who belongs to that lineset, the value of the variable need to be calculated. Then, in Condition column you can write $suppls = 1 or $suppls = 0 depending on how the value is calculated. Column B If you want to just skip a lineset, set check-box in column B. In this case, suppress lineset condition will be checked at the very beginning of the lineset. If the condition is met, lineset will be skipped an...

name: SYMBOL
description: This function returns value of a symbol.
text: SYMBOL SYMBOL This function returns value of a symbol. Format SYMBOL(symbol_name) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - symbol_name - Examples See Also

name: Symbols
text: Symbols Symbols Symbols represent one of mechanisms of inter-program communication. One program writes a symbol using WRITE_EXTERNAL statement. Another program reads that symbol by READ_EXTERNAL statement. Internally, symbols can be stored in the two ways. Symbols in internal file symntt00.iaf If we use ordinary symbol names, like write_external/NAME: symnam1 ... read_external/NAME: symnam1 ... symbols will be stored in internal file symntt00.iaf. This is standard text file which resides in the same directory as executable program. Each symbol definition occupies one line in the file. Line structure is the following: symbol_name:=="symbol_value" If Write_external stores many fields simultaneously, the structure is symbol_name:=="field1_value<TAB>field2_value<TAB>...fieldN_value" Symbol name is maximum 30 characters long, symbol_value length is up to 250 bytes. Symbols in System Registry (for Windows operating systems only) If we use symbol names starting with dollar sign, like write_external/NAME: $symnam1 ... read_external/NAME: $symnam1 ... symbols will be stored in System Registry under the key HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Sing\Iafw\RT. The statement write_external/name: $sym1 ...

name: Symbol Table Maintenance
description: Symbol Table Maintenance Symbol Table Maintenance
text: Symbol Table Maintenance Symbol Table Maintenance

name: Synchronize table definitions
description: Synchronize table definitions Synchronize table definitions
text: Synchronize table definitions Synchronize table definitions This dialog enables you to compare table structures in your Data Dictionary to table structures in the database. Compare all Compare all the tables from the list. For each datasource in the list, the dialog 'Enter username/password' appears on the screen. If you click 'Cancel', current table will not be compared. If you click 'Skip this datasource', all the tables belonging the datasource will be ignored. Compare selected Compare selected tables only. Show Show table structure from the Data Dictionary and appropriate structure from the database. Remove from list Remove selected tables from the list. All the table definitions stay unchanged in the Data Dictionary. If you want to remove all the tables from a datasource, just sort column 'Datasource', select the tables and click 'Remove from list'. Database schema (optional) If database supports schemas, you will see all the tables from your default schema after connection. But, if you use a table from another schema in your application, you must specify appropriate schema to successfully read table structure.

name: Tab control
text: Tab control Tab control The tab control is an object consisting of a number of pages that occupy the same space. To insert new tab control on the form, click button Tab control in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Tab control. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click.
