This is the top left corner of the tab control. Drag to the right and bottom and release the button. New tab control with three pages is placed on the form. Note: Tab control cannot be created within another tab control. To change tab control properties double click on the tab control -or- select the tab control, right click to display context menu and choose Properties. Add new page Right click tab control and select 'Add TabPage' in context menu. Remove page Right click tab control and select 'Remove TabPage' in context menu. Change page position Right click tab control and select 'Properties' in context menu.

name: TABLE
description: This statement defines the table with which the program is currently working.
text: TABLE TABLE This statement defines the table with which the program is currently working. Format TABLE = file[.record] Where - file - the name of the file (table) from datasource - record - the name of the record if the file (table) has more record redefinitions Example

name: Table gradient colors
description: Table gradient colors Table gradient colors
text: Table gradient colors Table gradient colors In this dialog we define gradient style and gradient colors for grid headings. Shading style Specifies that Color_from fades gradually to Color_to. Values: None No shading Horizontal Vertical Shading variant Valid if shading style is Horizontal or Vertical. Possible values are 1 to 4: 1 Ascending 2 Descending 3 Ascending/Descending 4 Descending/Ascending Corner button Color to Corner button ending color. Valid if shading style is Horizontal or Vertical. Side heading Color to Side heading ending color. Valid if shading style is Horizontal or Vertical. Top heading Color to Top heading ending color. Valid if shading style is Horizontal or Vertical. Note: in the all cases, Color_from is heading background color.

name: Table (grid)
text: Table (grid) Table (grid) A table (grid) is most complex object in the painter because the table contains another objects: source field, destination field, check box, combo box or button. Display-only table is a table containing source fields, read-only check boxes or buttons. In other words, such table (grid) just displays data. Data-entry table contains at least one destination field, check box or combo box. Maximum number of columns in 100. Insert new table (grid) To insert new table on the form, click button Table(grid) in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Table(grid). Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. A new table appears on the screen having just one column. Insert new column Right click on the top (column) heading of an existing column and select 'Insert left' or 'Insert right' option in the context menu. New column has the same width as current column. Insert new field Click appropriate button in toolbox (Source field, Destination field, check box, combo box or button) or choose corresponding menu command. Place the cursor to the first line (the line just below top heading) and click. Modify column heading Double click on the top (column) heading a...

name: Table modification
text: Table modification Table modification This dialog enables you to change table structure just in your Data Dictionary. Table structure in database will not be modified. All modifications are in effect until next Load table definitions from datasource. In the most number of cases, you should not modify table structure. But, there are situations where the modification is necessary. Some fields could be simply removed from the table definition. As we already said, table structure in the database remains unchanged, but our programs will not deal with these removed field. Primary key fields must not be removed. Some ODBC drivers do not return information about primary key of a table. This information is very important to the generator, and you should specify primary key segments manually (grid column Key). Also, some ODBC drivers, like driver for DB2, do not return proper autoincrement information of a column. Autoincrement columns must not be used in SQL INSERT and UPDATE statements. This is a reason you should adjust this attribute manually, if the loading process have not done this properly. Field name can not be modified because it represents field name used in I/O statements (SEL...

name: Table properties
text: Table properties Table properties Object name When the new table (grid) is created, generator automatically assigns the name Table_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Grid data entry options Valid if grid is 'data-entry', i.e. at last one column contains destination field, combo-box or check-box. There are three options: Update a table, update arrays and spreadsheet. Update a table If this option is selected, data will be stored into fields of a database table. The table must have primary key. Run-time engine uses internal memory table to store primary keys of the table. In 'Table starting actions' internal table must be initialized by SELECT_ALL/LOAD_KEY * from Tbl1 Where ... END_SELECT This set of instructions creates internal memory table, stores primary keys into it and implicitly defines initial number of lines in the grid. In 'Line ending actions', records are updated or inserted by IF RECORD Tbl1 FOUND UPDATE tbl1 ELSE WRITE tbl1 END_IF If the database changes should be permanently committed ...

name: Tables Manipulation
text: Tables Manipulation Tables Manipulation The list shows already loaded tables. Modify To modify a table definition, select the table in the list and click Modify -or- Double click the table name in the list. This option should be used very carefully because the modifications apply to Data Dictionary definition only. If some field attributes not loaded properly, such as primary key information, we can change them in this dialog. Also, some fields can be removed from the definition if we do not want to use them in our programs. Table definition in the datasource stays unchanged. If we load table definition again from the datasource, all these changes will be lost. Delete To delete a table definition from the Data Dictionary, select the table in the list and click Delete. Delete unused tables To delete all unused tables (the tables not used in programs), click Delete unused tables. The list of unused tables appears on the screen. We can delete all these table definitions or just some of them. Documentation In this dialog we can enter description of a table. Unlock If Data Dictionary Maintenance program crashes during table modification, the table definition remains locked. To unlock...

name: Text (label)
text: Text (label) Text (label) To insert new text on the form, click button Text in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Text. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. A small single-line edit box appears on the screen. Enter the text and press Enter or click outside the edit box. -or- Move the cursor to the desired position, then click and select (rubber-band) an arbitrary rectangular area enabling multiline text label. Enter the text and press Enter or click outside the edit box. To change text properties double click on the text -or- select the text, right click to display context menu and choose 'Properties'. Note If the symbol IAFUNICODE has value 1, all text labels will be saved in Unicode format.

name: Text properties
text: Text properties Text properties Object name When the new static text is created, generator automatically assigns the name Label_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Member of group (information only, cannot be changed) If the object is a member of a group, the name of this group is displayed. An object can be the member of one group only. Caption The text which appears on the screen or report. If you want to display single ampersand in the caption, enter two consecutive ampersands. Visible Determines whether the object is visible when the subform is first displayed. Font name Choose one from offered font names. Font size Specifies font size of the text. Bold Specify that font weight is 700. Normal weight is 400. Italic Specify whether text is italic. Underline Specify whether text is underlined. Orientation Orientation of text, in degrees. Horizontal text has orientation 0. Possible values are between -90 and 90 degrees. Background style Normal Background is filled with the current background color T...

name: TIME
description: This function converts a value expression to a time field.
examples: $time_field = "10:59:30" !! same as above. In this case
!In the next lines we MUST use TIME function.
text: TIME TIME This function converts a value expression to a time field. Format TIME(val_exp) Return Value Type Time. Parameters - val_exp - time literal, alpha literal, numeric literal, alpha field or datetime. Note 1 The input field must be in the proper format. Alpha and numeric literals must have 4 or 6 digits. Note 2 If 'val_exp' is a datetime field, the function simply returns the time part of the field. Note 3 The instruction time_field = TIME(datetime_field) has the same effect as time_field = datetime_field See Notes in Assignment operator. Examples $time_field = time("10:59") !! time literal $time_field = time("10:59:30") !! time literal $time_field = "10:59:30" !! same as above. In this case !we can use simple assignment because the left and right operands !are compatible. !In the next lines we MUST use TIME function. $time_field = time("1059") !! alpha literal $time_field = time("105930") !! alpha literal $time_field = time("171215") !! alpha literal $time_field = time("10593") !! invalid, 5 digits $time_field = time(1059) !! numeric literal $time_field = time(0815) !! numeric literal $time_field = time(142530) !! numeric literal if $a6 time $time_field = time($a6) end_i...

name: Time Functions
text: Time Functions Time Functions ADD_HOURS adds number of hours to a time. ADD_MINUTES adds number of minutes to a time. ADD_SECONDS adds number of seconds to a time. DIFF_TIME returns time difference between two time values. HOUR returns hours of a time. MAKETIME returns time value calculated from the hour, minute and second. MINUTE extracts minutes of a time. SECOND returns seconds of a time. SUB_HOURS subtracts a number of hours from a time. SUB_MINUTES subtracts a number of minutes from a time. SUB_SECONDS subtracts a number of seconds from a time. TIME converts a numeric or an alphanumeric field to a time.

name: Timer
description: The timer is an object enabling a code (procedure) activation when a time period elapses. The object picture, visible in the form painter, will not be seen at run-time.
text: Timer Timer The timer is an object enabling a code (procedure) activation when a time period elapses. The object picture, visible in the form painter, will not be seen at run-time. To insert new timer on the form, click button Timer in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Timer. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. New timer is placed on the form. To change timer properties double click on the timer -or- select the timer, right click to display context menu and choose Properties.

name: Timer properties
text: Timer properties Timer properties Object name When the new timer is created, generator automatically assigns the name Timer_nnn, where nnn = 1, 2, ... Object name can be maximum 30 characters long. Allowed characters are A-Z, 0-9, _ and $. First character must be a letter. Object name is case-insensitive. Object name can be used in Methods. Timer interval When this time interval elapses, the code (procedure) defined by 'Timer actions' will be executed. Time interval unit is millisecond. For instance, if you want to specify 30 seconds, 30000 should be entered. This parameter can be changed by Settimerinterval method. Timer activity Program The timer will be created at the start of the program and remains active disregarding which subform is currently active. Such timers should be created on the first subform. Active subform The timer of this type will be active only if its subform has focus. When the subform loses the focus, the timer stops. Automatic start at runtime Determines if the timer will be started after its creation. If the value is FALSE, then the Start method must be used somewhere in the program to start this timer. See also Start Stop

name: Toolbar
text: Toolbar Toolbar The toolbar contains button palette for most frequent commands. To use these buttons, just select object(s) and click appropriate toolbar button.

name: Toolbox
text: Toolbox Toolbox The toolbox contains button palette for object creating. To create an object, click a toolbox button, move the cursor at desired position on work area and click. The buttons on the toolbox work as toggle switch: one click puts the button in active state enabling object creating, the next click returns the button to inactive state. If an object is successfully created, the button automatically returns to inactive state. To create next object, we must click the button again, etc. If we want to create more objects of the same type, one after another, Ctrl/Click has to be used. In that case, the button remains active until next Click.

name: Tooltip text definition
description: Tooltip text definition Tooltip text definition
text: Tooltip text definition Tooltip text definition The tooltip attribute applies to the following objects: - Text label - Source field - Destination field - Combo box - Check box - Radio button - Push button - Bitmap button Define tooltip Choose tooltip type (static, dynamic, classic, balloon, super, subform). Enter "tooltip text" field, or click "tooltip parameters" button and, in next dialog, enter parameters. Remove tooltip Select "Static" radiobutton. "Tooltip text" field will be enabled. Delete this text. Static tooltip Static tooltip is defined in the painter and cannot be changed at run-time. Dynamic tooltip Dynamic tooltip can be changed at run-time. User should prepare alpha variable containing tooltip text. The "tooltip text" field can be defined using two formats: $tooltext or $tooltext = tooltip_func(p1, p2, ...) where is: $tooltext - local alpha variable of arbitrary length, tooltip_func - any function (local or global) returning an alpha variable. In the first format, local variable $tooltext is filled somewhere in the program. When tooltip text is about to be displayed, run-time engine just picks-up contents of the variable and displays it in tooltip window. In the s...

name: TOTAL
description: This aggregate function returns sum of numeric_exp for all processed records between two breaks.
text: TOTAL TOTAL This aggregate function returns sum of numeric_exp for all processed records between two breaks. Format TOTAL(numeric_exp [,cond_exp]) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - numeric_exp - numeric expression to be sumarrized. - cond_exp - conditional expression. Note If cond_exp does not exist, the function unconditionally sumarizes numeric_exp. If cond_exp exists, the function sumarizes numeric_exp only from records which calculate cond_exp to TRUE. Numeric_exp and cond_exp are calculated in detail lineset or detail calculations. This function can be used in reports and batch programs only. The following set of instructions, used in a subtotal, is invalid if cond_expr Source = total (field1) end_if because TOTAL summarizes values from all records. The correct syntax is: Source = TOTAL(field1, cond_expr) Examples TOTAL(field1) TOTAL(field1 + field2 + field3) TOTAL(field1*field2) TOTAL(field1, field2 > 10) See Also COUNT, AVG, MAX, MIN, SUM

name: TOTAL_CALC
description: Part of batch calculations which are executed at break, i.e. when break field change its value.
text: TOTAL_CALC TOTAL_CALC Part of batch calculations which are executed at break, i.e. when break field change its value. Format TOTAL_CALC Example TOTAL_CALC BREAK_FIELD = fld1 | fld2 ... some code ... BREAK_FIELD = fld1 ... some code ... BREAK_FIELD = LAST_RECORD ... some code ... See also Detail calculations, Break_field

name: TRANSLITER
description: This function converts a text from Latin script to Cyrillic script and vice versa (Serbian language only).
text: TRANSLITER TRANSLITER This function converts a text from Latin script to Cyrillic script and vice versa (Serbian language only). Format TRANSLITER(alpha_field, conv_direction) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_field - field to be converted, alpha literal or alpha variable (ANSI or Unicode UTF-16). - conv_direction - conversion direction, alpha literal or alpha variable. Possible values are: "tocyril" and "tolatin". Tocyril - convert Latin to Cyrillic Tolatin - convert Cyrillic to Latin. Notes Latin LJ / lj converts to ? / ? Cyrillic. Latin NJ/ nj converts to ?/ ? Cyrillic. Latin D�/ d� converts to ?/ ? Cyrillic. Possible cases: - ANSI_result = transliter(ANSI_field,'tocyril'). ANSI_field has to contain a text in code page 1250. Result will be the Cyrillic text in code page 1251. - UTF16_result = transliter(ANSI_field,'tocyril'). ANSI_field has to contain a text in code page 1250. Result will be the Cyrillic Unicode text. - UTF16_result = transliter(UTF16_field,'tocyril'). UTF16_field has to contain a Unicode Latin text. Result will be the Cyrillic Unicode text. - ANSI_result = transliter(ANSI_field,'tolatin'). ANSI_field has to contain a text in code page 1251. Result ...

name: Tree
description: We use a tree to display hierarchical structure of data.
text: Tree Tree We use a tree to display hierarchical structure of data. To insert new tree on the form, click button Tree in toolbox or choose menu command Objects/Tree. Move the cursor to the desired position, then click. This is the top left corner of the tree. Drag to the right and bottom and release the button. Tree definition dialog appears on the screen. Num levels number of the levels in the tree. If, for example, you want to display companies and their departments, number of levels should be 2: in the first level the company names will be displayed, and in the second level, departments belonging to each company will be seen. Initial filling If TRUE, the level will be filled when program control reaches this object by sequence. If FALSE, this level will be empty initially. If the user, at run-time, wants to see child items, double click will activate appropriate 'Insert code' and expand the node. Sort elements determines order of appearance of the tree items. If TRUE, all the items within one level will be sorted internally by the tree. If FALSE, order of appearance is defined by insertion order. Normal image the two images are assigned to each tree item. Type of image is BMP,...

name: Tree properties
text: Tree properties Tree properties

name: TRNLOG
description: This function translates a logical name.
text: TRNLOG TRNLOG This function translates a logical name. Format TRNLOG(logical_name) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - logical_name - alpha literal or alpha variable containing logical name. Note All definitions of logical names are stored in the file logntt00.iaf, see Logical Name Table Maintenance. The question is: how a program knows where the file logntt00.iaf resides on? The algorithm is the following: Run-time engine retrieves full path of the executable program. If the path does not start with two backslashes (\\), run-time engine tries to open file on this directory. If the path starts with two backslashes, denoting a local area network location (according to Universal naming convention, file name is in the form \\servername\sharename\path\file_name), run-time engine retrieves current working directory for the current process and try to open file from that location. In the other words, if programs reside on local PC, logntt00.iaf must be at the same directory as executable programs. If programs reside on server PC, runtime engine looks for logntt00.iaf at working directory. Working directory can be set in two ways: - via menu interpreter command (IAFMENUN menu_name wor...

name: TRUNC
description: This function truncates a numeric value.
text: TRUNC TRUNC This function truncates a numeric value. Format TRUNC(numeric_exp, numdec) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - numeric_exp - numeric value to be truncated. - numdec - number of resulting decimals, numeric. - If numdec is greater than zero, then it represents number of decimals. - If numdec is 0, input number will be truncated to integer. - if numdec is less than zero, then the input number is truncated to the left of the decimal point. Note Numdec is defined as follows: Numeric value 1 8 2, 5 6 Numdec -3 -2 -1 0 1 2 Examples acc_31 = 157,567 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,2) !! result is 157,56 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,1) !! result is 157,5 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,0) !! result is 157 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,-2) !! result is 100 acc_31 = -157,567 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,2) !! result is -157,56 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,1) !! result is -157,5 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,0) !! result is -157 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,-2) !! result is -100 acc_31 = 9,999 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,2) !! result is 9,99 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,1) !! result is 9,9 $C = TRUNC(acc_31,0) !! result is 9 See Also Numeric functions ROUND

name: Unhidecolumn
text: Unhidecolumn Unhidecolumn This method displays (shows) hidden columns. Format Unhidecolumn(column1, column2, ..., columnN) Parameters - column1, column2, columnN - a numeric variable or numeric literal, integer. Applied To Grid. Note Column = -1, show side heading. Column = 0, show all columns. Column = 1, 2, ... show a column. Example Table_1.Unhidecolumn(5) Table_1.Unhidecolumn(5,6) See also Hidecolumn Methods

name: UPDATE
description: This statement updates existing record of a file (rdb table).
examples: READ file1 KEY="AAA" !! read record by primary key
text: UPDATE UPDATE This statement updates existing record of a file (rdb table). Format 1 UPDATE file UPDATE rdb_table Format 2 UPDATE rdb_table [ [OMIT] (field_1, field_2, field_3, ...)] Where - file - the name of the file from datasource dialog. Access to a file has to be UPDATE or UPDATE_NO_CREATE. Record must be previously read by READ or READ_ALL. - rdb_table - rdb table from datasource dialog. Iaf generates SQL command using all the fields, except blob ones: UPDATE TABLE rdb_table SET field1=value, field2=value, ... fieldN=valueN WHERE pk_seg1=value1 AND pk_seg2=value2 ... AND pk_segN=valueN In format 2, we can write the list of fields and Iaf will generate SQL command using just the fields from the list: UPDATE TABLE rdb_table SET field1=value, field2=value, ... WHERE pk_seg1=value1 AND pk_seg2=value2 ... AND pk_segN=valueN If some fields should be omitted from UPDATE command, use OMIT keyword with list of fields. In both cases, the record (row) must be previously read by READ, READ_ALL or SELECT statement. If we use SELECT statement, all the segments of the primary key must be in select_list. Therefore, tables with no primary key, cannot be modified by UPDATE command (see Exe...

name: UPLOAD
description: This function sends HTTP POST request to an Internet site.
text: UPLOAD UPLOAD This function sends HTTP POST request to an Internet site. Format Upload (URL, message [,headers, username, password]) Parameters - URL - universal resource locator, like http://mysite/files/test.php, etc. - message - a CLOB variable (field type = S). Text to be sent via POST request. - headers - additional headers to be sent to the HTTP server, optional. Two or more header fields should be separated by CRLF (carriage-return/line feed pair). - username - an alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. If username is not an empty string, the function generates HTTP header field Authorization: Basic username:password - password - an alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. Return Value CLOB variable. Possible values: -1 Invalid (empty) message. -2 Invalid URL. -3 Cannot establish connection -4 Cannot open http request -5 Upload file not found other Server response Notes - HTTP request header generated by this function is: POST target_object HTTP/1.1 Host: hostname[:port] Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded Content-Length: nnn If username and password supplied, additional header line is added: Authorization: Basic ... - To send JSON text, use additional he...

name: Uploadfile
text: Uploadfile Uploadfile This method uploads file to the connected FTP server. Format Uploadfile(localfile, remotefile [,progressbar_object]) Parameters - localfile - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Local file name. - remotefile - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the file on the server. - progressbar_object - an alpha variable or alpha literal, optional. Name of a progress_bar used to display progress of file transfer. Return Value Alpha variable. If connecting was successful, value is blank. Otherwise, the variable holds an error message. Applied To Ftp object. Note Transfer method is BINARY. Examples $msg = Ftp.Uploadfile('c:\temp\a.exe','file1.exe','progress_1') $msg = Ftp.Uploadfile('c:\temp\a.exe','file1.exe')!!no progress bar Using FTP See also Downloadfile

name: UPPER
description: This function converts an alpha field to upper case.
text: UPPER UPPER This function converts an alpha field to upper case. Format UPPER (alpha_exp) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - alpha field, ANSI or UTF-16. Note If alpha field is ANSI one, the conversion is performed according to the current code page. Examples $res = Upper('abcd') !! Result = ABCD See Also LOWER, LOWER_NLS, UPPER_NLS, INITCAP, INITCAP_NLS

name: UPPER_NLS
description: This function converts all characters in alpha_exp to upper case.
text: UPPER_NLS UPPER_NLS This function converts all characters in alpha_exp to upper case. Format UPPER_NLS(alpha_exp, code_page) Return Value Type Alpha. Parameters - alpha_exp - alpha field to be converted. - code_page - a valid code page Examples $res = Upper_nls('abcd',1252) !! Result = ABCD $res = Upper_nls('abcdš�',1250) !! Result = ABCDŠ� $res = Upper_nls('abcdüö',1252) !! Result = ABCDÜÖ See Also LOWER, LOWER_NLS, UPPER, INITCAP, INITCAP_NLS

name: Using XML - example
description: Using XML - example Using XML - example
text: Using XML - example Using XML - example Read a database table and store it into the XML file. $xmlstat = xml.Open("xmldir:outfile.xml") if $xmlstat = 0 $xmlstat = xml.Writetext('<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>') !! XML declaration $xmlstat = xml.Setxmldecpoint('.') $xmlstat = xml.Writenode('records') select_all * from tbl1 where [condition] $xmlstat = xml.Writenode('record') $xmlstat = xml.Writeelement('alpha_1',alpha_field) $xmlstat = xml.Writeelement('num_1',num1_field,"ZZZ9") $xmlstat = xml.Writeelement('num_2',num2_field,"ZZZ9,99") $xmlstat = xml.Writeelement('date_1',date_field,'YYYY-MM-DD') $utf8 = convert(alpha_field_1,1250,65001) $xmlstat = xml.Writeelement('alpha_2',$utf8) $xmlstat = xml.Writeendnode('record') end_select $xmlstat = xml.Writeendnode('records') $xmlstat = xml.Close() else Pause "Open file error" | $xmlstat end_if See also Using XML

name: Value_date
text: Value_date VALUE_DATE This function converts a date literal to date or datetime value. Format VALUE_DATE(input_date, date_format) Return Value Type Date or datetime. Parameters - input_date - alpha field or alpha literal containing date literal. - date_format -date format in input field. Possible values: 'ymd' for year-month-day, 'dmy' for day-month-year, 'mdy' for month-day-year. If this parameter is empty, expected format is ymd. Notes The function converts alpha field containing date or date-time literal to date field, because alpha fields cannot be used in calculations. The function skips leading spaces. Formatting characters, like slash, minus or dot are ignored. If input date is invalid, result is ZERO_DATE. Examples $datefld = value_date(' 2011-12-20','ymd') $datefld = value_date(' 2011-12-20','') $datefld = value_date('20/12/2011','dmy') $datefld = value_date('20.12.2011','dmy') $datefld = value_date('20-12-2011','dmy') $datefld = value_date('2/1/2012','dmy') !! 2-jan-2012 $datefld = value_date('2/1/2012','mdy') !! 1-feb-2012 $datefld = value_date('31/04/2011','dmy') !! Invalid day $dt = value_date('2011-12-22 10:11:12','ymd') $dt = value_date('2011-12-22T10:11:12','ymd'...

name: VALUE
description: This function converts numeric literal, date, date-time or time field to the numeric value.
text: VALUE VALUE This function converts numeric literal, date, date-time or time field to the numeric value. Format VALUE(value_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - value_exp - a value expression: alpha, date, time, datetime or numeric. Notes The function is usually used to converts alpha fields containing numeric literals to numeric fields, because alpha fields cannot be used in calculations. The function skips leading spaces and ends with the first non-digit character. Thousands group separator is ignored. If input literal has more decimals than output field, excess decimals will be truncated. If 'value_exp' is a numeric field, this is an ordinary numeric move. If 'value_exp' is date, time or datetime, the result is an integer with appropriate number of digits. Examples ! decimal point = comma (,) $numfld = value('-1234,56') !!is the same as $numfld = value('-1.234,56') !!is the same as $numfld = value(' -1234,56') !!is the same as $numfld = value('-1234,56abc') !!is the same as $numfld = value(' -1234,56abc') !!is the same as $numfld = -1234,56 ! ! decimal point = point (.) $numfld = value('-1234.56') !!is the same as $numfld = value('-1,234.56') !!is the same as $numfld =...

name: Value Expression
examples: A numeric literal is a string of digits with an optional sign (+ or –) and decimal point.
A date literal has the form "[D]D-MMM-YYYY" where is
A time literal has the form "[H]H:[M]M[:SS]" where is
text: Value Expression Value Expression Definition 1 A value expression (value_exp) is a symbol or string of symbols used to calculate a value. Definition 2 A value expression (value_exp) is a combination of variables, operators, constants and literals that calculates a value. The value can be: 1. alpha value (alpha_exp). Alpha field is ANSI if each character occupies one byte, or Unicode (UTF-16) if one character occupies two bytes. 2. numeric (numeric_exp) 3. date (date_exp) 4. time (time_exp) Literals An alpha literal is zero or more characters enclosed in delimiters: double (") quotation marks or apostrophes ('). First and last delimiter must be the same. To include quotation marks in a literal, use apostrophes as delimiters and vice versa. Any non-printable character can be entered into alpha literal using hexadecimal escape sequence in the form <xNN>. Such sequence contains exactly 5 characters: <, little x, two hexadecimal digits denoting a character and >. For example <x0A> denotes line feed, <x0D> carriage return, <0x1B> an escape character. To enter an Unicode character, 7-character sequence <wNNNN> should be used. For instance <w0401>, <w0110>. If you want to enter a cyrill...

name: View definition
text: View definition View definition The view determines the way of the records reading in the program cycle (reports and batch programs). The maximal number of the lines in view definition is 200. Special case: the view is empty. If the main table represents a RDB table, the generator generates SELECT * FROM main_table ORDER BY primary_key_segments If the main table represents a sequential file, program reads whole file. If the main file represents an ISAM files (like Btrieve), program reads whole file sorted by primary key. Standard view If the main table represents a RDB table, we use a standard SELECT statement. Although in the program cycle we want to read many records (rows), we use SELECT, not SELECT_ALL. In the view we can write down just one SELECT statement. Examples: SELECT select_list FROM tbl1,tbl2 WHERE ... ORDER BY ... SELECT * from tbl1 LEFT OUTER JOIN tbl2 ON fld1=fld2 WHERE ... SELECT * from tbl1 UNION tbl2 WHERE ... ORDER BY ... View with sequential file If the main table represents a sequential file, we use an old syntax FILE_NAME [Where ...] [[NATIONAL_]SORTED_BY sort_field1, ..., sort_fieldN] FILE_NAME is main file from Datasource dialog. SORT_FIELDn has the for...

name: View
description: These options define visibility status of some parts of the generator.
text: View View Toolbar Toolbox Alignment Status bar These options define visibility status of some parts of the generator. Grid If this option is checked, grid is displayed on the working area. The grid allows easier objects positioning. Snap to grid If the grid is displayed and 'Snap to grid' is checked, newly created object will be aligned to the grid. Refresh Redraws all objects. Window edge hit If this option is checked, a subform can be selected if we click on its edge only. On the contrary, subform is selected if we click at any point within the subform. Show tooltips If this option is checked, tooltip appears on the screen when cursor hovers over an object. Insert at last point clicked If this option is checked, the paste command creates new objects at position defined by last click. If the option is not checked, new object will be created at (0,0). See Paste command in Edit section. Editor options Activates dialog to define some editor options: keyword colors and tab size.

name: WAIT
description: This statement suspends the program execution for a specified interval (seconds or milliseconds).
text: WAIT WAIT This statement suspends the program execution for a specified interval (seconds or milliseconds). Format WAIT time_interval Where - time_interval - time interval. If time_interval > 0, this parameter denotes seconds. If time_interval < 0, the parameter denotes milliseconds. Example Wait 3 !! wait 3 seconds Wait -100 !! wait 100 milliseconds

name: WARMSG
description: This statement displays warning message and continues program execution (moves input focus to the next field according to the sequence number).
text: WARMSG WARMSG This statement displays warning message and continues program execution (moves input focus to the next field according to the sequence number). Format WARMSG = message Where - message - concatenated expression or number of the message from message file or an user defined function. Example Destination = fld1 Check fld1 >= 0 WARMSG = "Entered number is negative !!!" See Also ERRMSG ERRWARMSG

name: Watermarks
text: Watermarks Watermarks printing A watermark is text placed behind report objects. To add a watermark to the report 1. Choose menu command Define/Watermark printing from main menu. 2. In the Enable watermark printing dialog, select a watermark from the list. 3. Check 'Enable printing watermark in [Report N]'. 4. Choose 'Print on' option. 5. Eventually check 'Show watermark in the painter'. 6. Click OK. To disable enabled watermark 1. Choose menu command Define/Watermark printing from main menu. 2. In the Enable watermark printing dialog, uncheck 'Enable printing watermark in [Report N]'. 3. Click OK. To create new watermark In the Enable watermark printing dialog, click 'New'. Watermarks are stored in the Data Dictionary. The watermark, if enabled, implicitly creates object named WATERMARK with two methods: WATERMARK.Setvisible(0) - sets watermark to the invisible state. WATERMARK.Display(some_text) - changes watermark text at run-time. If report type is Report, each report can have different watermark. Also, if report type is Form then each page can have different watermark. Object names are WATERMARK_1 for page 1, WATERMARK_2 for page 2, etc. Commands to suppress watermark on pa...

name: WEEK_OF_YEAR
description: This function returns week of a year. According to ISO 8601, week 01 of a year is the first week that has the Thursday in that year, which is equivalent to the week that contains the fourth day of January.
text: WEEK_OF_YEAR WEEK_OF_YEAR This function returns week of a year. According to ISO 8601, week 01 of a year is the first week that has the Thursday in that year, which is equivalent to the week that contains the fourth day of January. Format WEEK_OF_YEAR(date_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. If result is 0, date_exp is zero_date or NULL. If result is -1 and date_exp <= 3-jan-YYYY, date_exp belongs to the last week of the previous year. If result is -1 and date_exp >= 29-dec-YYYY, date_exp belongs to the first week of the next year. Otherwise, the result is 1 to 53. Parameters - date_exp - input parameter, date or datetime. Notes Week 01 may contain days from the previous year. The last week of a year may contain days from the next year. The first week of the year 2004 lasts from 29-dec-2003 to 4-jan-2004. The first week of the year 2005 lasts from 3-jan-2005 to 9-jan-2005. The first week of the year 2006 lasts from 2-jan-2006 to 8-jan-2006. The first week of the year 2007 lasts from 1-jan-2007 to 7-jan-2007. Examples $week = week_of_year("1-jan-2006") !! result is -1 $week = week_of_year("2-jan-2006") !! result is 1 $week = week_of_year("10-jan-2006") !! result is 2 See Also Date Fu...

name: WHENEVER
description: This statement specifies action to be performed when fatal error occurs during the execution of a SQL statement.
text: WHENEVER WHENEVER This statement specifies action to be performed when fatal error occurs during the execution of a SQL statement. Format 1 WHENEVER SQLERROR STOP_RUN Displays an ODBC error message and stops the program. This is the default action. Format 2 WHENEVER SQLERROR CONTINUE Stores five-characters ODBC error into internal variable IAF_SQLERROR, native error into internal variable IAF_SQLNATIVE and continues. These variables should be tested after each SQL statement in the block. Scope of the statement is: local procedure, global procedure or any block of statements (subform calculations, field calculations, etc.). Explanation: When Iaf uses a database, there exist two types of errors: standard and fatal. Only three standard errors exist: record not found, record is duplicated and record is locked. These errors can be processed by Iaf: if record REC_XYZ not_found if record REC_XYZ duplicated if record REC_XYZ locked All other errors are considered as fatal ones. When they happen, Iaf displays error message, rollbacks the transaction and stops the program. By using WHENEVER SQLERROR CONTINUE, we can control fatal errors. Example 1: Procedure locproc !! procedure start: wh...

name: WINDOW
description: This statement transfers control to n-th subform.
text: WINDOW WINDOW This statement transfers control to n-th subform. Format WINDOW nn Where - nn - the number of the subform on which the program execution is continuing. Number has to be integer or a field. Value has to be greater than zero, and less or equal to the number of a subforms in the program. Example Window 2 !! goto subform 2 $wnd = 5 Window $wnd

name: Close
description: This method discards any changes, closes document and stops Word. After this, another document can be opened, etc.
text: Close Close This method discards any changes, closes document and stops Word. After this, another document can be opened, etc. Format Close() Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Word object. Note This method is usually used after the reading input document. Examples $stat = Word.Close() Using Word

name: Example 1
text: Example 1 Using Word - Example 1 Open existing Word file enabling review, editing and all other options. $wstat = Word.Open("docdir:text1.doc") if $wstat = 0 $wstat = Word.Run() end_if Open existing Word file just enabling review. $wstat = Word.Open("docdir:text1.doc","read_only") if $wstat = 0 $wstat = Word.Run() end_if Print Word file on default printer without user action. $wstat = Word.Open("docdir:text1.doc","read_only") if $wstat = 0 $wstat = Word.Run("Print") $wstat = Word.Close() end_if Example 2, Example 3 Using Word

name: Example 2
text: Example 2 Using Word - Example 2 Open existing Word file (template), store data into three bookmarks "name", "date" and "amount", and save document under new name. $wstat = Word.Open("docdir:template.doc") if $wstat = 0 $wstat = Word.Setbookmark("name",$name,1250) $wstat = Word.Setbookmark("date",today) $wstat = Word.Setbookmark("amount",edit(acc_1,"---.---.--9,99")) $wstat = Word.Run("save","outdir:newname.doc") else Pause "Error openning input file" end_if After the Run instruction, file outdir:newname.doc is created and Word is active. Important note After Setbookmark method, using Run without some of 'save' options is not recommended. An user could activate, accidentally, 'Save' command in Word and overwrite original template by active document. Active document does not contain bookmarks, and next time the program will fail because it will not find any bookmark. Therefore, the following combination is not recommendable: ... $wstat = Word.Setbookmark("somebm",variable) $wstat = Word.Run() ... Example 1, Example 3 Using Word

name: Example 3
text: Example 3 Example 3 Prepare data file for mailmerge, activate mailmerge method and save document under new name. We suppose that merge_template.doc has been previously created and supplied by necessary mailmerge information. $wstat = Word.Mergeopen("docdir:mergefile.txt") if $wstat = 0 ! write field names $wstat = Word.Mergedata("fld1") $wstat = Word.Mergedata("fld2") $wstat = Word.Mergedata("fld3") $wstat = Word.Mergewrite() ! write fields itself $wstat = Word.Mergedata($name) $wstat = Word.Mergedata(today) $wstat = Word.Mergedata(edit(acc_1,"------9,99")) $wstat = Word.Mergewrite() ! close data file $wstat = Word.Mergeclose() ! open tempate document $wstat = Word.Open("docdir:merge_template.doc") if $wstat = 0 $wstat = Word.Run("mailmerge_save","outdir:newname.doc") end_if else Pause "Error creating data file" end_if After the Run instruction, file outdir:newname.doc is created and Word is active. Note Using methods Mergeopen, Mergedata, Mergewrite and Mergeclose is the simplest way to create sequential, field delimited, files. Field delimiter is TAB, data format is Unicode. Example 1, Example 2 Using Word

name: Introduction
text: Introduction Using Word - Introduction Programs can transfer data to a MS Word document. For this purpose we use object named Word and its methods. There is only one Word object in a program. This means that two documents cannot be opened at the same time. Program transfers data to MS Word in two ways: via bookmarks or using mailmerge method. All methods return a value. A program uses COM (OLE) interface to manipulate objects belonging to MS Word. MS Word must be installed on local PC. Object name: Word Methods: Append - appends a file (Word or Excel) to the current document ExportPDF - Export to PDF file Close - discards any changes and stops Word Open - starts MS Word and opens document Run - saves or prints document Runmacro - runs a macro Setbookmark - stores text into bookmark Setcodepage - sets default code page for subsequent calls Opentable - creates new table or opens the existing one Insertrow - insert row into table Setcell - stores value into table cell Repeatheader- repeats table heading Closetable - closes table opened by Opentable Mergeopen - creates and opens data file for mailmerge Mergedata - stores one field into the mailmerge record Mergewrite - writes one re...

name: Open
description: This method starts MS Word and opens existing document.
examples: Opens existing file, changes not allowed
text: Open Open This method starts MS Word and opens existing document. Format Open(filename [,readonly]) Parameters - filename - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the existing Word document. Logical names in filename are allowed. - readonly - second parameter, if used, is alpha literal containing text "read_only". In that case, document cannot be changed. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Cannot activate Word -2 Document already opened -3 File not found -4 Logical name not found -5 File locked by another user Applied To Word object. Note To release current Word document use Close or Run method. Examples Variants Open(filename) Opens existing file. Open(filename,"read_only") Opens existing file, changes not allowed $stat = Word.Open("c:\doc\test.doc") $stat = Word.Open("docdir:test.doc") $stat = Word.Open($file_name) $stat = Word.Open("c:\doc\test.doc","read_only") if $stat = 0 ... end_if Using Word Excel.Open File.Open Ftp.Open

name: Run
description: This method saves the document, breaks connection between program and MS Word, and gives control to the user. After this, another document can be opened, etc.
examples: Run("print") !! Prints document on default printer
Run("print 3") !! Prints 3 copies on default printer
Run($pcmd) !! number of copies is variable
text: Run Run This method saves the document, breaks connection between program and MS Word, and gives control to the user. After this, another document can be opened, etc. Format Run([command [,output_filename]]) Parameters - command - this parameter, if used, is alpha literal or alpha variable containing one of the following values: "print" - prints document on default printer, "print N" - prints N copies of document on default printer, N < 10. "save" - invokes 'Save as', "save_exit" - invokes 'Save as' and stops Word, "save_print" - invokes 'Save as', prints document on default printer and stops Word, "save_print N" - the same as above + copies, N < 10, "mailmerge" - invokes 'Mailmerge' "mailmerge_save" - saves documents, Word is active "mailmerge_exit" - invokes 'Mailmerge', saves document and stops Word, "mailmerge_print" - invokes 'Mailmerge', saves and prints document and stops Word. - output_filename - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Specifies name of the output file which will be used in 'Save as'. Logical names in filename are allowed. If command has some of 'save' or 'mailmerge' options, this parameter is required. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values:...

name: Setcell
text: Setcell Setcell This method stores value into cell of previously opened table. Format Setcell(line,column,variable [,codepage]) Parameters - line - row, numeric value. - column - column, numeric value. - variable - value which will be written to the cell. - codepage - if variable is an alpha_exp, this parameter specifies variable's code page. The parameter is very important because MS Office tools (Excel, Word, ...) use UNICODE to represent text data, while text data in programs use, in most cases, single-byte representation. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened -2 Table not opened Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Opentable("#1",0,0) if $stat = 0 $stat = Word.Setcell(1,1,acc_1) $stat = Word.Setcell(1,2,today) $stat = Word.Setcell(1,3,textdata,1250) $stat = Word.Closetable() end_if Using Word See also Closetable, Opentable

name: Setcodepage
text: Setcodepage Setcodepage This method sets the code page for subsequent "set" methods. Format Setcodepage(codepage) Parameters - codepage - a numeric variable or numeric literal. Denotes valid code page. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 Document not opened Applied To Word object. Examples $stat = Word.Setcodepage(1250) $stat = Word.Setbookmark("b1",fld1) $stat = Word.Setbookmark("b2",fld2) $stat = Word.Setbookmark("b3",fld3,1252) !! overrides 1250 Using Word

name: WRITE
description: This statement writes record into a file (table).
examples: WRITE filename.rec1 !! file with many record redef.
WRITE/NOTRUNCATE filename !!do not remove trailing blanks
WRITE rdbtable !! insert all fields into the table
WRITE rdbtable (fld1,fld2) !!insert two fields only: fld1 and fld2
text: WRITE WRITE This statement writes record into a file (table). Format 1 writes record into a file WRITE[/NOTRUNCATE] file[.record] Format 2 inserts row into RDB table WRITE [catalog.][schema.]RDBtable [ [OMIT] (field1, ..., fieldN)] Where - file - the name of the file from datasource dialog. Access to the file has to be WRITE, WRITE_NEW or UPDATE. - record - record name if the file has many record redefinitons. - schema - database schema, optional. If omitted, current schema is used. - RDBtable - the name of the RDB table from datasource dialog. By default, all the fields will be inserted into the table. To insert specified fields into the table, use list of fields. If some fields should be omitted from INSERT command, use OMIT keyword with list of fields. Qualifiers /NOTRUNCATE can be used for sequential files with variable record format. In that case, trailing blanks will NOT be removed from the record buffer. By default, trailing blanks are removed from the buffer. Note The second format of the instruction inserts specified fields into the RDB table. The generator converts this statement into the appropriate SQL one: INSERT INTO rdbtable (field1,field2, ..., fieldN) VALUES (:v...

name: WRITE_AUDIT
description: This statement writes record in journal file.
text: WRITE_AUDIT WRITE_AUDIT This statement writes record in journal file. Format 1 WRITE_AUDIT conc_expr Format 2 WRITE_AUDIT /NAME: "file_name" conc_expr Format 3 WRITE_AUDIT /NAME: $local_variable conc_expr Where - file_name - a physical name of a journal file. If this qualifier is omitted, the name of audit trail file from Change setup parameters dialog will be taken. - conc_exp - concatenation, which has to be written in journal file. Size of a concatenation must not be greater than 250 characters. - local_variable - the standard local alpha variable. This variable must be filled by appropriate file name before invoking this statement. Note The journal file is a standard ANSI text file. Such files can be reviewed by any text editor. Examples Write_audit "The audit text" Write_audit "The audit text: " | counter_1 Write_audit/name: "aa.atf" "The audit text" $locatf = "c:\temp\aa.atf" Write_audit/name: $locatf "The audit text"

name: Writeelement
text: Writeelement Writeelement This method writes starting tag, ending tag and data in the XML file. Format Writeelement (element_name, field_value [,edit_mask]) Parameters - element_name - an alpha variable or alpha literal, element name. - field_value - data field or NULL. - edit_mask - if the data field is numeric, date or time, this parameter defines presentation form of a field. If the data field is alphanumeric or NULL, this parameter have to be omitted. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 File not opened > 0 File input/output error Applied To Xml object. Note The first line in XML file should be XML declaration in the form: <?xml [attributes]?> One of attributes is ENCODING. This attribute specifies character set that is used in the document. Attribute values can be "WINDOWS-1250", "WINDOWS-1252", "ISO-8859-1", "UTF-8", "UTF-16", etc. All alpha fields have to contain characters from the character set defined by ENCODING attribute. Default value is "UTF-8". Examples $stat = Xml.Writeelement("fld1", acc_1, "ZZZZ9,99") Output line in XML file will be <fld1>100.00</fld1> $stat = Xml.Writeelement("fld1", NULL) Output line in XML file will be <fld1/> See ...

name: Writeendnode
text: Writeendnode Writeendnode This method writes ending tag of the node. Format Writeendnode (node_name) Parameters - node_name - an alpha variable or alpha literal, node name. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 File not opened > 0 File input/output error Applied To Xml object. Examples $stat = Xml.Writeendnode("node1") Result line in XML file will be </node1> See also Writenode Using XML

name: WRITE_EXTERNAL
description: This statement writes or deletes a symbol.
examples: WRITE_EXTERNAL/NAME: sym1 counter_1 !! write into symntt00.iaf
text: WRITE_EXTERNAL WRITE_EXTERNAL This statement writes or deletes a symbol. Format 1 WRITE_EXTERNAL /NAME: symbol_name field_1[,..., field_n] Format 2 WRITE_EXTERNAL /VARNAME: alpha_variable field_1[,..., field_n] Format 3 WRITE_EXTERNAL /DELETE /NAME: symbol_name Format 4 WRITE_EXTERNAL /DELETE /VARNAME: alpha_variable Where - symbol_name - the name of a symbol. The first character of name must not be underline (_). Blanks in name not allowed. If the first character is a dollar sign ($), the symbol will be written into the system registry under the key HKEY_CURRENT_USER\software\sing\iafw\rt. Otherwise, the symbol will be written into SYMNTT00.IAF file residing on the default directory. Also, the full name of registry key can be used in the form: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\subk1\subk2\sym or HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\subk1\subk2\sym. - alpha_variable - variable containing symbol name. - field_1,..., field_n - list of fields, making symbol value. Formats 1 and 2 write a symbol. If the symbol already exists, symbol value will be modified. Formats 3 and 4 remove a symbol. If we want to delete many symbols, wildcard character can be used: write_external/delete/name: $* -> delete all symbols from HKEY...

name: Write
description: This method writes one record to a text file.
text: Write Write This method writes one record to a text file. Format Write(field) Parameters - field - buffer containing text, an alpha variable. If text file is utf-16 type, this variable must be a UTF-16 one. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok. -2 Write error -3 File not opened Applied To File object. Example $stat = File.write($rec) See also WRITE Read Working with files

name: Writenode
text: Writenode Writenode This method writes starting tag of the node and possible attributes. Format Writenode (node_name [, attributes]) Parameters - node_name - an alpha variable or alpha literal, node name. - attributes - node attributes, can be omitted. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 File not opened > 0 File input/output error Applied To Xml object. Examples $stat = Xml.Writenode('node1', 'type="uint"') Result line in XML file will be <node1 type="uint"> See also Writeendnode Using XML

name: Writetext
text: Writetext Writetext This method writes one line of text into the XML file. Format Writetext (text) Parameters - text - an alpha variable or alpha literal. Return Value Status, numeric variable. Possible values: 0 Ok -1 File not opened > 0 File input/output error Applied To Xml object. Example $stat = Xml.Writetext('<?<xml version = "1.0"?>') See also Using XML

name: XML_ADD_FIELD
description: This function adds one field to a XML stream.
text: XML_ADD_FIELD XML_ADD_FIELD This function adds one field to a XML stream. Format XML_ADD_FIELD(xml_variable, node_name, field_value[,code_page]) Result Value Type Xml variable (type = S). Parameters - xml_variable - alpha variable containing XML stream. Variable type is CLOB, longchar (type = S). - node_name - name of XML node, alpha literal or alpha variable, required. - field_value - field to be inserted into XML stream, required. - code_page - if field_value is an ANSI alpha, this parameter specifies variable's code page. The parameter is optional, default value is ANSI code page. Note The function inserts starting node, field value and ending node in the form <node_name>field_value</node_name>. Alpha fields will be converted internally into UTF-8. Default edit mask for a date field is 'YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00'. Default edit mask for a datetime field is 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS'. Default edit mask for a numeric field is -------------9. Decimal point is POINT. Examples ! Local field $xml has type S init $xml $xml = xml_add_field($xml,'fld1',today) $xml = xml_add_field($xml,'fld1',timestamp) $xml = xml_add_field($xml,'fld1',counter_1) !! num without decimals $xml = xml_add_field($xml,'...

name: XML_ADD_TEXT
description: This function adds text to a XML stream.
text: XML_ADD_TEXT XML_ADD_TEXT This function adds text to a XML stream. Format XML_ADD_FIELD(xml_variable, text) Result Value Type Xml variable (type = S). Parameters - xml_variable - alpha variable containing XML stream. Variable type is CLOB, longchar (type = S). - text - text to be inserted into XML stream, alpha literal or alpha variable. Note Alpha ANSI text must be in UTF-8 format. Alpha UTF-16 text will be converted into UTF-8. Trailing blanks will be removed. Examples ! Local field $xml has type S init $xml $xml = xml_add_text($xml,'Free text') $xml = xml_add_text($xml,'<field_1>xxx</field_1>') See also XML_ADD_FIELD XML_GET_FIELD XML_DATASET_FIELD

name: XML_DATASET_FIELD
description: This function reads one field from a XML dataset.
text: XML_DATASET_FIELD XML_DATASET_FIELD This function reads one field from a XML dataset. Format XML_DATASET_FIELD(xml_variable, node_name) Result Value Type Any field. Parameters - xml_variable - alpha variable containing XML dataset. Variable type is CLOB, longchar (type = S). - node_name - node name, alpha literal or alpha variable. Note XML dataset must be established by READ_ALL command, format 3. Examples read_all xml_dataset($s,'books.book') $fld = xml_dataset_field($s,'title') $fld = xml_dataset_field($s,'group_1.gf_1') end_read See also READ_ALL XML_GET_FIELD

name: XML_GET_FIELD
description: This function reads one field from a XML stream.
text: XML_GET_FIELD XML_GET_FIELD This function reads one field from a XML stream. Format XML_GET_FIELD(xml_variable, node_name) Result Value Type Any field. Parameters - xml_variable - alpha variable containing XML stream. Variable type is CLOB, longchar (type = S). - node_name - XML node name, alpha literal or alpha variable. Note Separators < and > can be used in the node_name without changing the result. For example, '<field_1>' gives the same result as 'field_1'. Examples ! Local field $xml has type S $status = LOAD_FILE('c:\test\test.xml',$xml) if $status = 0 $fld = xml_get_field($xml,'field_1') $fld = xml_get_field($xml,'<field_1>') !! same as above $fld = xml_get_field($xml,'group_1.field_1') end_if See also XML_ADD_FIELD XML_DATASET_FIELD

name: XML
description: This function opens and reads an XML file (obsolete).
examples: Copy Test.xml into standard seq file.
text: XML XML This function opens and reads an XML file (obsolete). Format XML(operation[,arg1,arg2,...]) Result Value Type Numeric. Parameters - operation - file operation, an alpha literal. Possible values are: "OPEN", "CLOSE", "NODE", "RECORD", "FIELD", "ATTRIBUTE", "SKIP". - arg1, arg2,... argN - arguments Usage a/ Open a XML file. XML("open", file_name, root_element) file_name XML file name root_element name of the root element This call should be invoked before any other XML call. Example: $status = XML("open","c:\dir\test.xml","root") if $status <> 0 Pause "Error opening file" end_if b/ Close an opened XML file XML("close") c/ Position at the next node XML("node",node_name) d/ Read a field into a host variable XML("field",field_name,host_variable) e/ Read an attribute into a host variable XML("attribute",attribute_name,host_variable) f/ Read a record XML("record",record_name) Record record_name must be defined in the Iaf Data Dictionary and declared in Datasource dialog. g/ Skip fields up to the end of the current level XML("skip") Note If the function succeeds, the result is zero. If the function fails, result contains error status. Examples Copy Test.xml into standard seq fil...

name: YEAR
description: This function returns 2- or 4-digit year of a date.
text: YEAR YEAR This function returns 2- or 4-digit year of a date. Format YEAR(date_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date_exp - Notes If date_exp = zero_date or date_exp = NULL, result is 0. If date_exp is a 8-digit date, result is a 4-digit number. If date_exp is a 6-digit date, result is a 2-digit number. Examples See Also Date Functions

name: YEAR2
description: This function returns 2-digit year of a date.
text: YEAR2 YEAR2 This function returns 2-digit year of a date. Format YEAR2(date_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date_exp - Note Result is a number in the range 0 to 99. If date = zero_date or date = NULL, result is 0. Examples See Also Date Functions

name: YEAR_MONTH
description: This function returns 6-digit year and month of a date.
text: YEAR_MONTH YEAR_MONTH This function returns 6-digit year and month of a date. Format YEAR_MONTH(date_exp) Return Value Type Numeric. Parameters - date_exp - Notes If date = zero_date or date = NULL, result is 0. If date_exp is a 6-digit date, the first two digits of the result will be 19 or 20, depending on IAF_CENTURY_START symbol. Examples YEAR_MONTH("1-jan-2001") !! result is 200101 YEAR_MONTH("10-feb-2001") !! result is 200102 See Also Date Functions

name: IAF Help
description: The Z-Order defines the position (depth, layer) of the object when many passive objects overlaps each other. The object with Z-Order = 0 is on the top and overlaps all other objects.
text: IAF Help The Z-Order defines the position (depth, layer) of the object when many passive objects overlaps each other. The object with Z-Order = 0 is on the top and overlaps all other objects. Value of -1 denotes one layer bellow top objects, -2 denotes next lower layer, etc. The passive objects are: Text Line Rectangle 3D Panel Ellipse Image Frame
